questions.stem: In what formats is the technical manual provided for the Schilling HD ROV?
options: On a CD-ROM and as a printed volume set
Only as a downloadable PDF on the manufacturer's website
Only as a printed volume set
On a USB flash drive and as a printed volume set
questions.correct_answer: On a USB flash drive and as a printed volume set
questions.explanation: The manual is specifically provided in both digital format on a USB flash drive and as physical printed volumes, giving operators multiple ways to access the technical documentation.
questions.source_quote: The technical manual is provided on a USB flash drive and as a printed volume set.

questions.stem: Where is the Final Acceptance Tests (FAT) documentation for the ROV system stored?
options: System Logs folder
Maintenance Records folder
Support Files folder
Configuration folder
questions.correct_answer: Support Files folder
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the as-built Final Acceptance Tests documentation is stored in the Support Files folder. This is the designated location for keeping FAT records accessible for reference and audit purposes.
questions.source_quote: As-built:Final Acceptance Tests (FAT) document in the Support Files folder

questions.stem: Where are nominal system weight specifications documented for the Schilling HD ROV?
options: On the ROV frame nameplate
On the main control panel display
In the operator manual preface section
In the system brochure located in the Support Files folder
questions.correct_answer: In the system brochure located in the Support Files folder
questions.explanation: The manual specifically references that nominal system weight specifications are documented in the system brochure, which is stored in the Support Files folder rather than in the main manual body.
questions.source_quote: Nominal: See the system brochure in the Support Files folder

questions.stem: Where can you find payload and trim specifications for the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Volume 1: ROV Operations Manual
Volume 2: ROV Maintenance Procedures
Volume 4: ROV System Part Lists and Drawings
Volume 3: ROV Electrical Systems
questions.correct_answer: Volume 4: ROV System Part Lists and Drawings
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifically directs operators to Volume 4 for payload and trim specifications, as this volume contains the detailed part lists and assembly drawings needed for proper configuration.
questions.source_quote: For payload and trim specifications:—ROV assembly drawing in Volume 4: ROV System Part Lists and Drawings

questions.stem: Where is the USB flash drive containing the technical manual typically stored?
options: In the rear pocket of Volume 2 of the printed manual set
Attached to the ROV control panel with a tether cable
Inside the main console compartment near the power supply
In the front cover of Volume 1 of the printed manual set
questions.correct_answer: In the front cover of Volume 1 of the printed manual set
questions.explanation: The USB flash drive is specifically stored in the front cover of Volume 1, making it easily accessible when the operator needs to reference the technical manual during operations.
questions.source_quote: The USB flash drive is typically found in the front cover of Volume 1 of the printed manual set.

questions.stem: What information does the ROV system stackup and tooling interface drawing provide?
options: Shows power consumption requirements for each component
Shows maximum operational depth ratings for the system
Shows recommended maintenance intervals and schedules
Shows how components are arranged and connected
questions.correct_answer: Shows how components are arranged and connected
questions.explanation: The stackup and tooling interface drawing is a schematic that specifically illustrates the physical arrangement and interconnection of all system components. This is essential for understanding system layout, cable routing, and how tooling interfaces connect to the ROV.
questions.source_quote: ROV system stackup and tooling interface drawing

questions.stem: How can you view the technical manual for the Schilling HD ROV?
options: By requesting a printed copy from the manufacturer
Only through the ROV control system display
Using a web browser or PDF files
Using specialized ROV viewing software only
questions.correct_answer: Using a web browser or PDF files
questions.explanation: The manual is designed for easy digital access and can be opened directly in any standard web browser or as a PDF file on your computer.
questions.source_quote: You can view the manual using a web browser or using PDF files.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, what does the technical manual explain how to do?
options: Navigate underwater terrain and perform diving operations
Program the ROV's artificial intelligence and update satellite maps
Operate the ROV system, understand system design, perform service on components, and get information about component parts
Conduct maintenance on the support vessel and manage crew schedules
questions.correct_answer: Operate the ROV system, understand system design, perform service on components, and get information about component parts
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states it covers operating the ROV system, understanding system design, performing service on components, and getting component information—these are the four core functions of this technical manual.
questions.source_quote: This technical manual explains how to operate the ROV system, understand the system design, perform service on system components, and get information about component parts.

questions.stem: What do topside equipment drawings show?
options: The mounting arrangement and interconnections of surface components
Electrical schematics for thruster motor circuits
The physical dimensions and weight limits of subsea equipment
Recommended maintenance schedules and intervals
questions.correct_answer: The mounting arrangement and interconnections of surface components
questions.explanation: Topside equipment drawings specifically illustrate how surface components are mounted and how they interconnect with each other, helping operators understand the physical layout and connections of equipment in the control room and on deck.
questions.source_quote: Diagram of topside component mounting and interconnections

questions.stem: What type of information do OEM device data sheets and/or manuals typically provide?
options: Exclusive manufacturing process details
General marketing and advertising content
Information about installing and using the devices
Only price and billing contact information
questions.correct_answer: Information about installing and using the devices
questions.explanation: OEM devices typically include documentation specifically focused on practical guidance for installing and operating the equipment, not administrative or promotional content.
questions.source_quote: Many OEM devices come with data sheets and/or manuals that provide information about installing and using the devices.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV system documentation, where should you look for OEM equipment manuals?
options: Test App Launcher
Volume 2: ROV System Controls and Software
Component Resource Control console equipment
Support Files folder
questions.correct_answer: Support Files folder
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that OEM equipment manuals are collected as digital files and stored in the Support Files folder. While Volume 2 covers system controls and software, and the console equipment hosts the Technical Manual System itself, neither of these locations is where OEM manuals are stored.
questions.source_quote: Using the Technical Manual System or Com- ponent Resource Control console equipment (like control console computers and video equipment) ● Volume 2: ROV System Controls and Software ● For OEM equipmen

questions.stem: What is considered an OEM device on a Schilling ROV system?
options: Any device that has been repaired or rebuilt by an external service provider
Any device originally installed on the ROV by Schilling Robotics
Any device that is permanently attached to the ROV frame
Any device manufactured by a company other than Schilling Robotics
questions.correct_answer: Any device manufactured by a company other than Schilling Robotics
questions.explanation: OEM stands for 'original equipment manufacturer.' By definition, an OEM device is any component on the ROV system that was manufactured by a company other than Schilling Robotics.
questions.source_quote: A device that is manufactured by some other company is called an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) device.

questions.stem: Where can you find the digital OEM documents in the ROV system technical manual?
options: In the Maintenance Procedures folder
In the main root folder of the technical manual
In the OEM Docs subfolder of the Support Files folder
In the Hardware Specifications folder
questions.correct_answer: In the OEM Docs subfolder of the Support Files folder
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that digital OEM documents are stored in the OEM Docs subfolder, which is located within the Support Files folder of the ROV system technical manual.
questions.source_quote: Most OEM documents are collected as digital files and are available in the ROV system technical manual (in the OEM Docs subfolder of the Support Files folder).

questions.stem: Where may paper copies of OEM documents be found in an ROV system?
options: Paper copies of OEM documents must be submitted to the vessel's classification society.
Paper copies of OEM documents are only required for the main propulsion system.
Paper copies of OEM documents are only stored in the control room operator station.
Paper copies of OEM documents may be included in the ROV system document package or in an equipment item's supplied case.
questions.correct_answer: Paper copies of OEM documents may be included in the ROV system document package or in an equipment item's supplied case.
questions.explanation: OEM documentation may be supplied in paper form either as part of the overall ROV system documentation package or physically packaged with specific equipment items in their supplied cases.
questions.source_quote: Paper copies of OEM documents may also be supplied by manufacturers and included in the ROV system document package or in an equipment item's supplied case.

questions.stem: Where do you navigate to find digital OEM documentation on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Support Files folder → OEM Docs subfolder → OEM device folder
Documentation folder → OEM Files subfolder → Device folder
System Files folder → Docs subfolder → Manufacturer folder
Settings folder → OEM Documentation subfolder → Device folder
questions.correct_answer: Support Files folder → OEM Docs subfolder → OEM device folder
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that digital OEM documentation is located by navigating to the Support Files folder, then the OEM Docs subfolder, and finally selecting the folder for the specific OEM device you need documentation for.
questions.source_quote: navigate to the Support Files folder and then to the OEM Docs subfolder. From there, pick the appropriate folder for the OEM device.

questions.stem: On the Schilling HD ROV, what type of devices are thrusters, depth sensors, and subsea lights?
options: Standard safety equipment
OEM devices
Operator-installed upgrades
Third-party accessories
questions.correct_answer: OEM devices
questions.explanation: According to the manual, thrusters, depth sensors, and subsea lights are examples of OEM devices that come pre-installed on the ROV system from the original manufacturer.
questions.source_quote: Thrusters, depth sensors, and subsea lights are examples of OEM devices.

questions.stem: From which computer must you execute test applications when troubleshooting equipment on the ROV?
options: Any computer connected to the ROV network
A laptop connected via Ethernet to the ROV system
The main control station computer
The computer that is directly connected to the equipment item under test
questions.correct_answer: The computer that is directly connected to the equipment item under test
questions.explanation: Test applications must be run from the computer that is physically attached to the equipment being tested. This ensures the test can communicate directly with the device and accurately diagnose the issue.
questions.source_quote: You must run test apps from the computer that is attached to the equipment item being tested.

questions.stem: Where is the Test App Launcher located on the manual flash drive?
options: In the Main Applications folder
In the root directory
In the Support Files folder
In the Configuration folder
questions.correct_answer: In the Support Files folder
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that the Test App Launcher is available in the Support Files folder on the manual flash drive. This is where all support utilities and tools are stored for easy access during operations.
questions.source_quote: The Test App Launcher is available in the Support Files folder.

questions.stem: Where do you navigate to find documentation for a specific OEM light?
options: Support/OEM Docs/Lights/
Documentation/OEM Files/Lights/
Support Files/OEM Docs/Cameras/
Support Files/OEM Docs/Lights/
questions.correct_answer: Support Files/OEM Docs/Lights/
questions.explanation: The manual specifies navigating to Support Files/OEM Docs/Lights/ to access documentation for individual OEM lights. The correct path uses 'Support Files' as the top-level folder, followed by 'OEM Docs', then 'Lights' as the subfolder.
questions.source_quote: navigate to Support Files/OEM Docs/Lights/. In the Lights subfolder, pick the folder for the individual light.

questions.stem: What is the Test App Launcher?
options: A software program that provides access to equipment test applications along with their usage instructions
A hardware device used to physically launch the ROV from its cradle
A single test application for checking hydraulic systems
A monitoring program that displays real-time sensor data during operations
questions.correct_answer: A software program that provides access to equipment test applications along with their usage instructions
questions.explanation: The Test App Launcher is specifically designed as a software program to organize and provide access to multiple equipment test apps, including the instructions needed to use each one properly.
questions.source_quote: The Test App Launcher is a software program that lets you access a suite of equipment test apps (along with instructions for using the apps.)

questions.stem: Where should you look to find detailed specifications for the Schilling HD ROV's hydraulic and compensation system?
options: Assembly drawings
Online vendor catalogs
The system's warning labels
The troubleshooting section of the operator manual
questions.correct_answer: Assembly drawings
questions.explanation: Assembly drawings contain the detailed technical specifications and dimensions for hydraulic components. The operator manual directs you to these drawings for specific measurements, tolerances, and configuration details.
questions.source_quote: See assembly drawings for specifications.

questions.stem: What happens when communication between the ROV and the TCU is lost?
options: An alarm is triggered
Power to the ROV is cut immediately
The TMS immediately reels in the tether
The ROV automatically returns to the surface
questions.correct_answer: An alarm is triggered
questions.explanation: The system continuously monitors the communication link between the ROV and the TCU. When this link is lost, the system detects the fault and triggers an alarm to alert the pilot so they can take appropriate action.
questions.source_quote: Losing communications with the TCU produces an alarm.

questions.stem: What happens when you energize the B coil on a directional valve?
options: The valve rotates clockwise
The valve de-energizes
The valve remains in its current position
The valve rotates counterclockwise
questions.correct_answer: The valve rotates counterclockwise
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV manual, energizing the B coil specifically produces counterclockwise rotation of the directional valve.
questions.source_quote: Energizing the 'B' coil produces counterclockwise rotation.

questions.stem: How do you override the automatic shutdown to enable emergency HPU operation on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: Accessing a separate emergency panel near the TMS junction box
Power cycling the entire ROV system
Using the control console touch screen
Using a physical switch located directly on the HPU unit
questions.correct_answer: Using the control console touch screen
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV allows operators to override automatic shutdowns through the control console touch screen when emergency HPU operation is required. This is the designated interface for making this safety-critical override.
questions.source_quote:
If emergency HPU operation is required, you can override the automatic shutdown using the control
console touch screen.

questions.stem: When PLC communication is lost, what is the maximum main pressure setting an operator can use?
options: Exactly 2000 psi
Any value below 2000 psi
Up to 2500 psi
Pressure is fixed and cannot be changed
questions.correct_answer: Any value below 2000 psi
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that when PLC communication is lost, operators can manually set the main pressure to any value below 2000 psi as a backup control method.
questions.source_quote: You can set a pressure lower than 2000 psi

questions.stem: What is the maximum main pressure the hydraulic system will maintain when PLC communication is lost?
options: 3000 psi
2500 psi
2000 psi
1500 psi
questions.correct_answer: 2000 psi
questions.explanation: When PLC communication is lost, the system defaults to a safe operating limit of 2000 psi maximum. Setting the pressure higher has no effect because the system cannot exceed this limitation without PLC control.
questions.source_quote: Setting the pressure higher has no effect

questions.stem: What happens when the A coil on a directional valve is energized?
options: The B coil is activated instead
The valve remains in its current position
The valve rotates counter-clockwise
The valve rotates clockwise
questions.correct_answer: The valve rotates clockwise
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, energizing the A coil produces clockwise rotation of the directional valve.
questions.source_quote: Energizing the 'A' coil produces clockwise rotation.

questions.stem: What automatic action does the ROV control system take if the PLC loses communications during operation?
options: The control system increases main pressure to maximum operating pressure
The control system waits for the operator to manually reduce pressure
The control system automatically reduces main pressure to 2000 psi
The control system immediately shuts down the ROV
questions.correct_answer: The control system automatically reduces main pressure to 2000 psi
questions.explanation: The control system has an automatic safety response when PLC communications are lost. If main pressure was above 2000 psi, it automatically reduces pressure to 2000 psi to maintain safe operating conditions until communications are restored.
questions.source_quote: the control system decreases main pressure to 2000 psi

questions.stem: What does a lit green diagnostic LED indicate on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: The ROV has entered standby mode
12 volts is being supplied for main power distribution
5 volts is supplied to the microprocessor for each function circuit
The TMS is properly connected and operational
questions.correct_answer: 5 volts is supplied to the microprocessor for each function circuit
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the green LED lights up specifically when 5 volts is present at the microprocessor for each function circuit. This is a direct indicator of proper microprocessor power supply, not overall system status or other voltage levels.
questions.source_quote: Green LED: lit when 5 V is present at the microprocessor for each function.

questions.stem: How many amber LEDs are associated with the A coil circuit on the TCU PCB?
options: Two
One
Three
Four
questions.correct_answer: Two
questions.explanation: The TCU PCB has two amber LEDs specifically for the A coil circuit. These LEDs provide visual indication of the A coil status in the system.
questions.source_quote: there are two amber LEDs for the 'A' coil.

questions.stem: When is contact with spinning thrusters considered a hazard on the ROV?
options: During TMS deployment operations
When the ROV is on deck
When the ROV is powered down for maintenance
When the ROV is submerged in water
questions.correct_answer: When the ROV is on deck
questions.explanation: Spinning thrusters are a hazard specifically when the ROV is on deck, where personnel are most likely to be in close proximity without the protective barrier of water.
questions.source_quote: When the ROV is on deck

questions.stem: What is the purpose of the TCU's transparent cover?
options: To allow direct observation of the diagnostic LED panel
To display the unit's serial number and model information
To provide easy access for routine maintenance of the circuit board
To protect the circuit board from water damage during operation
questions.correct_answer: To allow direct observation of the diagnostic LED panel
questions.explanation: The TCU is specifically designed with a transparent cover so operators can directly view the red, amber, and green diagnostic LEDs on the circuit board without having to open or remove any panels.
questions.source_quote: TCU is supplied with a transparent cover for viewing the red, amber, and green diagnostic LEDs

questions.stem: What does the amber LED closest to the green LED indicate on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: 26 VDC coil power is being supplied to the A coil circuit
24 VDC power is available in the system
A fault has been detected in the coil circuit
Power is being supplied to the B coil circuit
questions.correct_answer: 26 VDC coil power is being supplied to the A coil circuit
questions.explanation: The amber LED closest to the green LED is an indicator that illuminates specifically when 26 VDC coil power is present in the A coil circuit. This is a direct indicator of power supply status, not a fault warning.
questions.source_quote: Amber LED closest to green LED: lit when 26 VDC coil power is present.

questions.stem: What does it indicate when the amber LED farthest from the green LED lights up on the ROV control panel?
options: A 24 V command signal is present for the A coil
No command signal is being sent to the system
A 5 V command signal is present for the A coil
The A coil has failed and needs replacement
questions.correct_answer: A 5 V command signal is present for the A coil
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the amber LED farthest from the green LED lights up specifically when a 5 V command signal is present for the A coil. This is a direct indicator of that exact signal condition, not other voltage levels or fault conditions.
questions.source_quote: Amber LED farthest from green LED: lit when 5 V command signal is present.

questions.stem: According to the ROV safety manual, what can contact with spinning thrusters cause?
options: Minor cuts and abrasions
Temporary hearing loss
Serious injury or death
Electrical shock
questions.correct_answer: Serious injury or death
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly warns that contact with spinning thrusters can cause injury or death, making this the only correct answer among the options provided.
questions.source_quote: WARNING! Contact with spinning thrusters can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: What type of signal does the valve pack controller send to operate the valve solenoids?
options: Pneumatic pressure signals
Mechanical linkages
Direct hydraulic fluid flow
Electrical signals
questions.correct_answer: Electrical signals
questions.explanation: The valve pack controller sends electrical signals to the solenoids, which then open or close the hydraulic passages to control fluid flow through the valve.
questions.source_quote: Electrical signals from the valve pack controller cause the valve solenoids to open or close hydraulic passages, thereby controlling the fluid flowing through the valve.

questions.stem: What is the base part number for the Sub-Atlantic SA-380 hydraulic thruster?
questions.explanation: The SA-380 hydraulic thruster manufactured by Sub-Atlantic is identified by base part number 101-7360 according to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual.
questions.source_quote: Hydraulic thruster, model SA-380 (Sub-Atlantic)... THRUSTER, HYD, SA380-41... 101-7360

questions.stem: On 8-lpm valve modules equipped with check valves, what component position determines whether the check valves are active or bypassed in the hydraulic circuit?
options: The bypass spool position (all the way in or all the way out)
The flow rate adjustment knob on the module
The pressure setting on the main relief valve
The manual override lever on the control panel
questions.correct_answer: The bypass spool position (all the way in or all the way out)
questions.explanation: The bypass spool controls whether check valves are in or out of the hydraulic flow path. Positioning the bypass spool all the way in or all the way out determines the check valve configuration for the circuit.
questions.source_quote: the valve module can be configured to put the check valves either in or out of the hydraulic flow path by positioning the bypass spool all the way in or all the way out.

questions.stem: Where is the PCA backplane located in a Schilling valve pack, and what is its primary purpose?
options: On top of the valve modules, to provide physical mounting support
Underneath all rapid-change valve modules, to distribute power and enable communication
At the rear panel, to connect external tether cables
Inside the hydraulic manifold block, to regulate fluid pressure
questions.correct_answer: Underneath all rapid-change valve modules, to distribute power and enable communication
questions.explanation: The PCA backplane is located underneath all rapid-change valve modules and serves to distribute electrical power throughout the valve pack and enable communication between components.
questions.source_quote: Each Schilling valve pack has a printed circuit assembly (PCA) backplane that extends underneath all rapid-change valve modules

questions.stem: What must be used to cover empty ports on the valve pack before the valve pack is submerged?
options: Rubber gaskets
O-ring seals
Thread tape
Blanking plates
questions.correct_answer: Blanking plates
questions.explanation: Blanking plates are specifically designed to seal empty ports and prevent contamination or fluid loss when the valve pack is underwater. Other options like O-rings, thread tape, and rubber gaskets are not appropriate for covering empty ports in this application.
questions.source_quote: blanking plates must be used to cover empty ports before the valve pack is sub-

questions.stem: According to factory configuration, which manipulator joints do NOT have check valves in the hydraulic flow path?
options: All manipulator joints
Shoulder, elbow, and rotate joints
Jaw and wrist joints
Shoulder and elbow joints only
questions.correct_answer: Jaw and wrist joints
questions.explanation: The factory setting places check valves in the hydraulic flow path for most manipulator joints, but intentionally excludes the jaw and wrist joints from this configuration.
questions.source_quote: The factory setting is to have the check valves in the hydraulic flow path on all manipulator joints except the jaws and wrist.

questions.stem: How many discrete positions can the hydraulic spool be placed in?
options: Multiple positions along a continuous range
Three - inserted, middle, and extended
Two - fully inserted or fully extended against the retaining ring
Four - inserted, partially inserted, partially extended, and extended
questions.correct_answer: Two - fully inserted or fully extended against the retaining ring
questions.explanation: The hydraulic spool operates in only two discrete states - it must be either fully inserted or fully extended against the retaining ring. There is no middle position or continuous adjustment range.
questions.source_quote: The spool should be either fully in or fully out (backed up against the retaining ring in the bore)

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, where can shock hazards exist in the ROV system?
options: Only on the deck surface at the power connection points
Only in the ROV and TMS while they are submerged
Primarily in the control van where the main power equipment is located
Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.correct_answer: Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that shock hazards exist throughout the entire ROV system, not just in isolated areas. This means personnel must treat all components—control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV—as potentially dangerous and follow proper safety procedures when working near or on any part of the system.
questions.source_quote:
SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages throughout the ROV system (including the control van,
deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV) can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: Where are the power requirements for LARS and deck HPU documented?
options: LARS maintenance log
ROV system interconnect drawing
Main control panel schematics
TMS operator manual
questions.correct_answer: ROV system interconnect drawing
questions.explanation: According to the manual, LARS and deck HPU power requirements are listed separately in the ROV system interconnect drawing, not in general operator documentation or other system manuals.
questions.source_quote: LARS and deck HPU power requirements are listed separately in the ROV system interconnect drawing

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure before repositioning the bypass spool?
options: Verify the hydraulic pump is in standby mode
Release output circuit pressure by loosening fittings
Check the spool alignment indicators
Close the main hydraulic supply valve
questions.correct_answer: Release output circuit pressure by loosening fittings
questions.explanation: The safety protocol requires releasing output circuit pressure by loosening fittings before repositioning the bypass spool to prevent hydraulic system damage or personal injury.
questions.source_quote: You must relieve output circuit pressure by cracking fittings before moving the spool

questions.stem: What happens when the hydraulic spool is rotated counterclockwise until it contacts the retaining ring?
options: Flow is directed exclusively through the check valves
Hydraulic flow bypasses the check valves (bypass mode)
The hydraulic pump automatically shuts down
Hydraulic flow is stopped to isolate the system
questions.correct_answer: Hydraulic flow bypasses the check valves (bypass mode)
questions.explanation: Rotating the spool counterclockwise until it contacts the retaining ring routes hydraulic flow around the check valves, engaging bypass mode. This allows fluid to bypass the check valves rather than flowing through them.
questions.source_quote: When the spool is turned counterclockwise until it rests against the retaining ring in the bore, the hydraulic flow bypasses the check valves.

questions.stem: What is the maximum power rating of the UHD-III ROV?
options: 300 kVA
225 kVA
400 kVA
336 kVA
questions.correct_answer: 336 kVA
questions.explanation: The UHD-III is listed with two power ratings: 225 kVA and 336 kVA, making 336 kVA the maximum power rating available for this model.
questions.source_quote:
UHD-III
Power
225 kVA
336 kVA
Voltage
440-690 VAC
Amperage
295 A (440 VAC)
188 A (690 VAC)
441 A (440 VAC)
281 A ( 690 VAC)
Hz
60 Hz
Phase
Three-phase
005-16000 Compass PDUs cannot use 50 Hz power.

questions.stem: On the Schilling HD ROV, where is the PDU Remote Access switch status displayed?
options: TMS pendant
PDU junction box panel
HMI (Human Machine Interface)
Pilot's handheld controller
questions.correct_answer: HMI (Human Machine Interface)
questions.explanation: The HMI displays the Remote Access switch status as either engaged or disengaged, allowing the pilot to quickly verify the switch position during operations.
questions.source_quote: Shows status of the Remote Access switch (engaged or disengaged).

questions.stem: What type of values does the Analog Input Reading function display from PLC analog inputs?
options: Historical averaged values
Real-time unscaled values
Calibrated scaled values
Processed digital values
questions.correct_answer: Real-time unscaled values
questions.explanation: The Analog Input Reading function specifically displays current unscaled readings directly from the PLC analog inputs, meaning the raw data before any scaling or calibration is applied.
questions.source_quote: Analog Input Reading: Contains current unscaled readings of all PLC analog inputs

questions.stem: What does the TMS HV Trends screen display?
options: Numerical readouts of low-voltage power consumption
Controls for the TMS tether payout and retrieval functions
Video footage from the TMS camera systems
Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements on the TMS
questions.correct_answer: Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements on the TMS
questions.explanation: The TMS HV Trends screen specifically displays graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements on the TMS system, allowing pilots to monitor voltage patterns over time.
questions.source_quote: TMS HV Trends: Displays the Trends graphing screen for high-voltage trends on the TMS

questions.stem: What causes a Phase Sequence Fault in the PDU?
options: A ground fault is detected in the system
One of the power phases has an open circuit
The PDU main breaker is tripped due to overload
L1, L2, and L3 are wired in the wrong sequence
questions.correct_answer: L1, L2, and L3 are wired in the wrong sequence
questions.explanation: A Phase Sequence Fault specifically occurs when the three-phase power lines (L1, L2, L3) are connected to the PDU in an incorrect order. This wiring error causes the fault condition, and the fix is to rearrange these connections to the proper sequence.
questions.source_quote: The order of L1, L2, and L3 must be re-arranged

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV operator manual, which two PDU settings are specifically password-protected?
options: Emergency Stop and Manual Override
Display Brightness and Language Settings
Thrust Calibration and Heading Control
Soft Shutdown Override and Voltage Setup
questions.correct_answer: Soft Shutdown Override and Voltage Setup
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that only the Soft Shutdown Override and Voltage Setup settings within the PDU require a password for access, making these sensitive configuration areas protected from unauthorized changes.
questions.source_quote: Password-protected settings (Soft Shutdown Override and Voltage Setup).

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to clear a Phase Sequence Fault on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Rewire L1, L2, and L3 to the correct phase sequence
Check and replace the L1 fuse only
Reset the fault using the fault reset button
Replace the main power controller
questions.correct_answer: Rewire L1, L2, and L3 to the correct phase sequence
questions.explanation: Phase sequence faults occur when the three-phase power connections are in the wrong order, and the only way to clear this fault is to physically rewire L1, L2, and L3 so they match the correct phase sequence as specified in the manual.
questions.source_quote: must be re-arranged

questions.stem: What does it mean when the Phase Sequence Fault light on the PDU is illuminated?
options: The primary input feed is incorrectly connected to the PDU
The PDU thermal limits have been exceeded
There is a hydraulic system overpressure condition
The power supply to the PDU has been interrupted
questions.correct_answer: The primary input feed is incorrectly connected to the PDU
questions.explanation: The Phase Sequence Fault light specifically indicates that the primary input feed wiring is reversed or incorrectly phased when being connected to the PDU. This must be corrected before the system can operate safely.
questions.source_quote: Lights up when the primary input feed is improperly connected to the PDU

questions.stem: What does the PDU Setup and Status screen allow you to access?
options: Camera and lighting system adjustment screens
All screens that contain PDU alarms and settings
Thruster configuration and motor control screens
Only the main power distribution on/off controls
questions.correct_answer: All screens that contain PDU alarms and settings
questions.explanation: The PDU Setup and Status screen is specifically designed as a central menu for accessing all PDU-related alarms and settings screens, making it the hub for monitoring and configuring power distribution functions.
questions.source_quote: The Setup and Status screen lets you access: All screens that contain PDU alarms and settings

questions.stem: What authentication method must be completed before using the Soft Shutdown Override function?
options: Password authentication
No authentication is required
Supervisor verbal approval
Physical key insertion
questions.correct_answer: Password authentication
questions.explanation: The Soft Shutdown Override is password-protected, meaning a valid password must be entered before the function can be accessed or activated.
questions.source_quote: Soft Shutdown Override (password protected)

questions.stem: What does the ROV HV Trends function display?
options: Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements on the ROV
Status indicators for all ROV thruster motors
Temperature readings from the vehicle's hydraulic system
Communication signal strength between ROV and surface
questions.correct_answer: Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements on the ROV
questions.explanation: The HV Trends screen is specifically designed to display graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements, allowing pilots to monitor the ROV's electrical system over time.
questions.source_quote: ROV HV Trends: Displays the Trends graphing screen for high voltage trends on the ROV

questions.stem: What does the Main Power Trends feature display on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Real-time video feeds from the ROV's camera systems
Hydraulic pressure readings from the ROV's maneuvering systems
Navigation coordinates from the ROV's positioning system
Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements across ROV system power circuits
questions.correct_answer: Graphical trend data for high-voltage measurements across ROV system power circuits
questions.explanation: The Main Power Trends specifically graphs high-voltage electrical measurements to monitor the health of the ROV's power distribution system. It is not used for video, hydraulics, or navigation data.
questions.source_quote: Main Power Trends: Displays the Trends graphing screens for high-voltage trends for ROV system power circuits

questions.stem: What is required before you can access security-protected PDU settings?
options: Physical key insertion into the PDU
Shutting down the ROV system
Supervisor approval via radio
Logon access with your credentials
questions.correct_answer: Logon access with your credentials
questions.explanation: The PDU requires users to log in with their credentials before gaining access to security-protected settings, ensuring only authorized personnel can modify critical power distribution parameters.
questions.source_quote: Logon: Logs in to the security-protected settings

questions.stem: After completing configuration tasks in the security-protected PDU settings, what does the Logoff function do?
options: Resets the PDU to factory default settings
Exits the security-protected PDU settings
Saves all current settings to memory
Restarts the entire PDU system
questions.correct_answer: Exits the security-protected PDU settings
questions.explanation: The Logoff function is specifically designed to exit the security-protected PDU settings after completing configuration tasks, returning the operator to the standard non-secure interface.
questions.source_quote: Logoff: Logs out of the security protected settings

questions.stem: What does the Read Counter on the PDU HMI indicate?
options: It displays the input voltage from the main feed
It shows the nominal voltage value set by the user
It indicates the LAN module is operating properly
It indicates that the PLC is communicating with the PQube
questions.correct_answer: It indicates that the PLC is communicating with the PQube
questions.explanation: The Read Counter monitors communication between the PLC and PQube. If the value is not incrementing, it means the communication link between the PLC and PQube is broken and needs troubleshooting.
questions.source_quote: Power Distribution Unit (PDU) HMI Feature Function Voltage [V] Displays the input voltage from the main feed (L-L is phase-to-phase voltages). Current [A] Displays the input current from the main feed

questions.stem: What does the Soft Shutdown Override function allow an operator to bypass?
options: The emergency jettison system
Physical circuit breakers and fuses in the PDU
Software-monitored shutdown functions such as short circuits, overcurrent, current imbalance, and overtemperature
All automatic and manual shutdown systems on the ROV
questions.correct_answer: Software-monitored shutdown functions such as short circuits, overcurrent, current imbalance, and overtemperature
questions.explanation: The Soft Shutdown Override specifically allows bypassing software-monitored protection functions like short circuits, overcurrent, current imbalance, and overtemperature shutdowns—it does not bypass manual systems, physical breakers, or unrelated systems like jettison.
questions.source_quote: Soft Shutdown Override: Allows the user to bypass software-monitored shutdown functions such as short circuits, over current, current imbalance, and temperature

questions.stem: What color are faults that have returned to normal displayed in on the Alarm History screen?
options: Red
Blue
Green
Yellow
questions.correct_answer: Green
questions.explanation: The Alarm History screen displays recovered faults in green to clearly indicate they have returned to normal operating condition.
questions.source_quote: Faults that have recovered to a normal condition are shown in green.

questions.stem: What information is recorded with each fault entry in the Alarm History?
options: Time and date stamp
Date only
Operator ID only
Sequence number only
questions.correct_answer: Time and date stamp
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, each fault in the Alarm History is automatically stamped with both the time and date it occurred, allowing operators to track when issues happened during operations.
questions.source_quote: Each fault has a time and date stamp.

questions.stem: What does a flashing green PLC Comm indicator on the HMI tell you?
options: There is a communication error that requires immediate shutdown
There is good communication between the HMI and the PLC
The PLC needs to be restarted
The PLC has lost communication with the HMI
questions.correct_answer: There is good communication between the HMI and the PLC
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the flashing green indicator lamp in the top right of the HMI screen confirms good communication status between the HMI and the PLC. This is normal operating status, not an error condition.
questions.source_quote: The flashing green indicator lamp in the top right of the screen indicates good communication with the PLC

questions.stem: What information does the Alarm History screen display on the PDU?
options: System configuration and parameter settings
Only currently active fault warnings
A log of all faults that have occurred on the PDU
Real-time video feed from ROV cameras
questions.correct_answer: A log of all faults that have occurred on the PDU
questions.explanation: The Alarm History screen specifically stores and displays a historical record of every fault that has happened on the PDU, allowing pilots to review past issues even after they have been cleared.
questions.source_quote: The Alarm History screen displays all faults that have occurred on the PDU.

questions.stem: What color are unrecovered faults displayed in on the Alarm History screen?
options: Yellow
Green
Red
Black
questions.correct_answer: Red
questions.explanation: According to the manual, faults that have not recovered are shown in red on the Alarm History screen. This visual indicator helps pilots quickly identify which faults are still active and need attention.
questions.source_quote: Faults that have not recovered are shown in red.

questions.stem: What does a red I/O MODULES OK indicator tell you to do?
options: Reduce system load
Reset the PDU power
Check the thruster connections
Replace the input or output modules
questions.correct_answer: Replace the input or output modules
questions.explanation: According to the manual, a red I/O MODULES OK light means the input or output modules have failed and need to be replaced. This is a corrective action that requires swapping out the faulty module(s).
questions.source_quote: A red light means that input or output modules must be replaced

questions.stem: What does it mean when the FAULT indicator on the ROV is lit?
options: A fault exists somewhere in the system
The exact location of the fault is displayed
The fault is specifically in the power distribution unit
The system has a minor warning that can be ignored
questions.correct_answer: A fault exists somewhere in the system
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the FAULT indicator lights up whenever there is any fault present anywhere in the system, not just in one specific area.
questions.source_quote: Indicates the existence of a fault anywhere on the system

questions.stem: What does a steady green PLC Comm indicator on the HMI display mean?
options: Active communication is successfully established between the HMI and PLC
The HMI is waiting for communication or there is no communication with the PLC
The PLC has detected a fault and requires immediate attention
The system is performing a normal power-on self-test
questions.correct_answer: The HMI is waiting for communication or there is no communication with the PLC
questions.explanation: According to the manual, a steady green PLC Comm indicator specifically indicates the HMI is waiting for communication or there is no communication with the PLC. This is not an indicator of successful communication or a fault condition.
questions.source_quote: Steady green means waiting for communication or no communication

questions.stem: When a Battery Fault appears on the PLC, what corrective action should you take?
options: Reset the PLC control system
Replace the main ROV power supply unit
Check and refill the hydraulic fluid reservoir
Recharge or replace the external PLC battery
questions.correct_answer: Recharge or replace the external PLC battery
questions.explanation: A Battery Fault on the PLC specifically indicates a problem with the external PLC battery, which requires recharging or replacement. Other corrective actions like replacing the power supply or resetting the PLC will not address this specific fault.
questions.source_quote: A "Battery Fault" will require the re-charge or replacement of the external PLC battery

questions.stem: What does the 'Copy Time from PLC' function do in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Transfers power status data from the PLC to the touchscreen
Copies alarm settings from the touchscreen to the PLC memory
Transfers time and date registers from the PLC RAM to the blue cells on the touchscreen
Synchronizes the touchscreen with the vehicle's GPS coordinates
questions.correct_answer: Transfers time and date registers from the PLC RAM to the blue cells on the touchscreen
questions.explanation: The 'Copy Time from PLC' function specifically copies time and date registers from the PLC RAM memory to the blue cells displayed on the touchscreen interface.
questions.source_quote:
COPY TIME FROM PLC
Copies time and date registers from the PLC RAM to the blue cells.

questions.stem: What must be done before the Copy Time from PLC and Write Time to PLC functions can be operated?
options: The time display must be reset to factory defaults
The backup battery must be disconnected
Write Copy Enable control must be activated
The PLC system must be powered down completely
questions.correct_answer: Write Copy Enable control must be activated
questions.explanation: The Write Copy Enable control is a prerequisite for operating the Copy Time from PLC and Write Time to PLC functions. Without first activating Write Copy Enable, these time-related functions cannot be accessed or operated.
questions.source_quote:
WRITE COPY ENABLE
Allows the "Copy time from PLC" and "Write Time to PLC" keys to be operated.

questions.stem: Where can you find the fault wiring diagram for each survey junction box model?
options: Hydraulic system diagram and pressure specifications
Survey J-box drawing and electrical schematic
ROV frame assembly drawing and material list
Operator console manual and maintenance log
questions.correct_answer: Survey J-box drawing and electrical schematic
questions.explanation: The manual directs you to consult the survey J-box drawing and electrical schematic for fault wiring diagrams of each survey junction box model.
questions.source_quote:
fault wiring diagram of each survey junction box model, see the survey J-box drawing and
the electrical schematic.

questions.stem: When using the Survey J-Box with compatible models in its factory default configuration, how many HD Video ports are supported?
options: Six HD Video ports (ports 1 through 6)
Four HD Video ports (ports 1, 2, 3, and 4)
Two HD Video ports (ports 1 and 2)
Eight HD Video ports (ports 1 through 8)
questions.correct_answer: Four HD Video ports (ports 1, 2, 3, and 4)
questions.explanation: The factory default configuration of the Survey J-Box supports HD Video on ports 1, 2, 3, and 4, giving you four HD video channels with compatible models.
questions.source_quote: 4 x HD Video, Argus, Ports 1, 2, 3, 4

questions.stem: Which ports on the Survey J-Box support Gbit connections in the factory default configuration?
options: Ports 5 and 6
All 8 ports
Ports 1 and 2
Ports 7 and 8

questions.explanation: The factory default configuration for the Survey J-Box designates ports 7 and 8 for Gbit connections, which must be used with compatible models to achieve gigabit speeds.

questions.stem: What configuration is the Survey J-Box set to when shipped from the manufacturer?
options: Configuration B
User-defined configuration
Configuration C
Configuration A
questions.correct_answer: Configuration A
questions.explanation: The Survey J-Box leaves the factory set to Configuration A as the default. This means it arrives ready for standard operation without any user configuration required.
questions.source_quote: As shipped, the survey J-box is configured for the devices shown

questions.stem: What is the factory default fiber configuration for the Survey J-Box?
options: Two fiber connections on ports 1 and 2 with SR specification
One fiber connection on port 1 with LR specification
One fiber connection on port 2 with SR specification
One fiber connection on port 1 with SR specification
questions.correct_answer: One fiber connection on port 1 with SR specification
questions.explanation: According to the factory default configuration, the Survey J-Box includes exactly one fiber connection, and it is always on port 1 with SR specification.
questions.source_quote: 1 x Fiber, SR spec., Port 1

questions.stem: Regarding the Survey J-Box factory default configuration, what is the status of SD Video support?
options: SD Video support is not included and must be manually configured if needed
SD Video requires a separate hardware module installed at the factory
SD Video support is automatically enabled in the factory default
SD Video is available through the standard video output ports
questions.correct_answer: SD Video support is not included and must be manually configured if needed

questions.stem: What port number is assigned to the EdgeTech interface in the Survey J-Box factory default configuration?
options: Port 5
Port 11
Port 13
Port 1

questions.explanation: The factory default configuration for the Survey J-Box assigns the EdgeTech interface to Port 11. This is the standard port assignment as specified by the manufacturer in the default setup.

questions.stem: Why must devices that require 48 VDC power be connected through the Survey J-box instead of directly to the DTS?
options: Because 48 VDC devices require special fiber optic cables that only the Survey J-box can provide
Because the DTS only supplies 26 VDC, so the Survey J-box provides the necessary 48 VDC through its relays
Because connecting 48 VDC devices directly to the DTS will cause system overheating
Because the Survey J-box has faster signal processing capabilities for 48 VDC devices
questions.correct_answer: Because the DTS only supplies 26 VDC, so the Survey J-box provides the necessary 48 VDC through its relays
questions.explanation: The DTS system only outputs 26 VDC, but devices requiring 48 VDC power must receive the correct voltage. The Survey J-box contains 48 VDC relays that convert the 26 VDC from the DTS SeaNet port output into the 48 VDC needed by these devices.
questions.source_quote: Survey Junction Box (Survey J-Box) 7.6 Supported Input Types The Survey J-box supports signals from devices that require 48 VDC power. It also supports some signals types that are not supported by the

questions.stem: When installing a Seabat 8125 into the survey J-box to free up a channel, which coaxial connectors must be swapped to move the HD signal to a spare SFP slot?
options: J18 and J22
J17 and J21
J16 and J20
J19 and J23
questions.correct_answer: J18 and J22
questions.explanation: Swapping the coaxial connectors at locations J18 and J22 moves the HD signal to a spare SFP slot, allowing the Seabat 8125 to transmit data through the 1610 nm SFP as required by the procedure.
questions.source_quote: Survey Junction Box (Survey J-Box) 7.7.2 Remove Third HD Camera, Install Seabat 8125 If Port 6 Has a Coaxial Cable Since the survey J-box comes configured with all optical channels in use, an HD camer

questions.stem: Why is it critical to match power and data connections to the port connections shown in the wiring diagram for the survey J-box?
options: To prevent device damage
To meet environmental compliance standards
To improve data transmission speed
To simplify maintenance procedures

questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that devices can be damaged if power and data connections are not consistent with the port connections shown in the wiring diagram for the survey J-box. Always verify connections before applying power.
questions.source_quote: Devices can be damaged if their power and data connections are not consistent with port connections shown in the wiring diagram for the survey J-box

questions.stem: Which Burton connectors can provide 48 VDC power when configured correctly?
options: Burton connectors 9 and 10
All Burton connectors
Burton connectors 6, 7, 8, and 11
Burton connectors 1, 2, 3, and 4
questions.correct_answer: Burton connectors 6, 7, 8, and 11
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, Burton connectors 6, 7, 8, and 11 have 48 VDC power available when the system is configured correctly for that output.
questions.source_quote: Burton connectors 6, 7, 8, and 11 have 48 VDC power available

questions.stem: When changing port wiring on the Schilling HD ROV, how should the Burton connector whip cage-clamp be reconfigured?
options: Using any standard connector configuration you have on hand
By removing it completely and reinstalling later
Only as specified in the manual
In any convenient manner that fits the available space
questions.correct_answer: Only as specified in the manual
questions.explanation: The Burton connector whip cage-clamp must only be reconfigured according to the specified procedure when changing port wiring. Deviating from these specifications could result in improper electrical connections, creating safety hazards or system failures.
questions.source_quote: reconfigure Burton connector only whip cage-clamp

questions.stem: What must you review before making any configuration modifications to the survey J-box ports or connectors?
options: The ROV console operation manual
The survey system calibration logs
The survey J-box wiring diagrams
The manufacturer warranty documentation
questions.correct_answer: The survey J-box wiring diagrams
questions.explanation: The wiring diagrams show the specific configuration and pin-out of the J-box, which is essential information before modifying ports or connectors to avoid wiring errors or system damage.
questions.source_quote: Before attempting any configuration modifications, see the survey J-box wiring diagrams

questions.stem: When installing a 101-5258 connector, what must be removed from port 11?
options: 7 pin FCR
13 pin FCR
15 pin connector
10 pin connector
questions.correct_answer: 13 pin FCR
questions.explanation: The procedure step specifies that the 13 pin FCR must be removed from port 11 before installing the 101-5258 connector. This is a direct component replacement procedure in the electrical/PDU subsystem.
questions.source_quote: Remove the 13 pin FCR from port 11, and install a 101-5258 connector.

questions.stem: To enable 48 VDC power at a connector, what should you do with the jumper at switches SW1, SW2, SW3, or SW4?
options: Set the jumper to position B
Set the jumper to position C
Remove the jumper completely
Set the jumper to position A
questions.correct_answer: Set the jumper to position A
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the jumper at each switch must be set to position A to enable 48 VDC power at the corresponding connector. Position A is the designated enable setting for these switches.
questions.source_quote: jumper at SW1, 2, 3, or 4 is located as shown in Enabling 48 VDC power to a device below A

questions.stem: When connecting devices not shown on the wiring diagrams, what must you verify before operation?
options: That the manufacturer's manual has been reviewed for calibration settings
That the device has been properly secured to the ROV frame
That power and telemetry connections are correct
That the wiring diagrams have been updated to include the device
questions.correct_answer: That power and telemetry connections are correct
questions.explanation: The procedure specifically requires verifying power and telemetry connections before operating any device not shown on wiring diagrams, as incorrect connections could damage equipment or cause safety hazards.
questions.source_quote: confirm that power and telemetry connections are correct or reconfigure as needed

questions.stem: What must be connected to a subsea node port for 48 VDC power to be available on a circuit?
options: The main vehicle power bus
The circuit's SeaNet cable
An external diagnostic tool
A separate 48 VDC power cable
questions.correct_answer: The circuit's SeaNet cable
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the SeaNet cable for the circuit must be connected to a subsea node port for 48 VDC power to be available. Without this connection, power will not be supplied to the circuit.
questions.source_quote: The circuit's associated SeaNet cable must also be connected to a subsea node port

questions.stem: How often does the Schilling HD ROV system send software packets between all nodes to check communication link quality?
options: Every 5 seconds
Every 2 seconds
Every 1 minute
Every 30 seconds
questions.correct_answer: Every 2 seconds
questions.explanation: The system is programmed to send software packets between all nodes every two seconds to continuously monitor and assess the quality of communication links throughout the ROV network.
questions.source_quote: Every two seconds, a software packet is sent between all nodes to assess the quality of the communication links

questions.stem: What components does the cable termination system connect or terminate in an ROV system?
options: Hydraulic lines, air supply, and water intake
Thrusters, sensors, and camera systems
Power supply, video monitors, and control interfaces
Deck cable, umbilical cable, tether, and slip rings
questions.correct_answer: Deck cable, umbilical cable, tether, and slip rings
questions.explanation: Cable terminations specifically connect and terminate the deck cable, umbilical cable, tether, and slip rings, which are the primary conductors for power, signal, and data transmission between the surface and the ROV.
questions.source_quote:
Cable Terminations
Various components are used to connect or terminate the deck cable, umbilical cable, tether, slip rings, and

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to disable 48 VDC power at a specific connector on the PDU?
options: Set the jumper at the corresponding switch to position A
Turn off the main power breaker for the entire PDU
Set the jumper at the corresponding switch (SW1, SW2, SW3, or SW4) to position B
Unplug the connector from the junction box
questions.correct_answer: Set the jumper at the corresponding switch (SW1, SW2, SW3, or SW4) to position B
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that to disable 48 VDC power at a specific connector, you must locate the appropriate switch (SW1, SW2, SW3, or SW4) and move its jumper to position B. This is a targeted method for controlling power to individual connectors without affecting the entire system.
questions.source_quote: Disable 48 VDC power by locating the jumper as SW1, 2, 3, or 4 as shown in Enabling 48 VDC power to a device below B

questions.stem: What happens when RSTP identifies a link as unreliable?
options: It automatically selects an alternative communication path that bypasses that link
It only alerts the operator but does not change the communication path
It shuts down all communication until the faulty link is repaired
The operator must manually switch to a backup communication path
questions.correct_answer: It automatically selects an alternative communication path that bypasses that link
questions.explanation: RSTP (Rapid Spanning Tree Protocol) is designed to automatically detect network failures and reconfigure the network path without operator intervention, ensuring continuous communication by bypassing any identified unreliable links.
questions.source_quote: RSTP selects a new communication path that bypasses that link

questions.stem: What happens to communications if a fiber cable or internal component of a node becomes damaged?
options: Other nodes automatically change their fiber paths within seconds to maintain communications
The system requires you to manually reconfigure all fiber paths through the touch screen
Only the damaged node loses communication while others continue normally
The entire system shuts down until manually reset
questions.correct_answer: Other nodes automatically change their fiber paths within seconds to maintain communications
questions.explanation: The dual fiber design combined with RSTP allows the system to automatically reroute communications around damaged components without human intervention, ensuring continued operation even when failures occur.
questions.source_quote: About the Digital Telemetry System Digital Telemetry System (DTS) can then use rapid spanning tree protocol (RSTP, in the node firmware) to decide which fiber will produce the best, most reliable comm

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV system, under what condition is a communication link flagged as unreliable?
options: Five consecutive software packets fail to be received
Two consecutive software packets fail to be received
A single software packet fails to be received
The communication link experiences any timeout during transmission
questions.correct_answer: Two consecutive software packets fail to be received
questions.explanation: The system uses a specific threshold of two consecutive failed packet receipts before flagging the link as unreliable. Single packet failures are expected in normal operations and do not trigger an unreliable status, and five consecutive failures would be a different, more severe condition.
questions.source_quote: Two consecutive packets fail to be received

questions.stem: At what incoming fiber light level does the SFP module consider a link unreliable?
options: When it drops below -10 dBm
When it drops below -40 dBm
When it drops below -30 dBm
When it drops below -23 dBm
questions.correct_answer: When it drops below -23 dBm
questions.explanation: The SFP module monitors incoming fiber light level, and if it falls below -23 dBm, the link is considered unreliable. This threshold represents the minimum acceptable signal strength for stable communications.
questions.source_quote: A node's SFP module detects a drop in the incoming fiber light level to less than -23 dBm

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to turn off RSTP?
options: Turn off power to the entire ROV system
Disconnect both fiber cables at the same time
Use the main RSTP toggle switch in the control room
Disconnect either Fiber 1 or Fiber 2
questions.correct_answer: Disconnect either Fiber 1 or Fiber 2
questions.explanation: According to the manual, RSTP can be turned off by disconnecting either Fiber 1 or Fiber 2 — you don't need to disconnect both or use any other method.
questions.source_quote: Disconnect Fiber 1 or Fiber 2

questions.stem: Can RSTP be disabled for the entire DTS system?
options: RSTP can only be turned off by the manufacturer remotely
No, RSTP must remain enabled at all times
Yes, RSTP can be turned off for the entire DTS
RSTP can only be turned off for individual components, not the entire DTS
questions.correct_answer: Yes, RSTP can be turned off for the entire DTS
questions.explanation: According to the manual, RSTP can be turned off for the entire DTS, giving operators flexibility in system configuration when needed.
questions.source_quote: RSTP can also be turned off for the entire DTS

questions.stem: What tool can the operator use to intervene in communications routing for the DTS?
options: The sonar interface panel
A dedicated manual routing switch
The hydraulic pressure controls
The ROV control system
questions.correct_answer: The ROV control system
questions.explanation: The ROV control system provides the operator with the capability to directly intervene in and manage the communications routing for the DTS system.
questions.source_quote: The operator can use the ROV control system to intervene in communications routing for the DTS

questions.stem: When you install a different interface module type or attach a new device to a node port on an ROV, what must you do?
options: Replace the existing firmware on all nodes
Restart the TMS completely before operation
Update the ROV control system to recognize the change
Recalibrate all thrusters immediately
questions.correct_answer: Update the ROV control system to recognize the change
questions.explanation: The ROV control system must be updated to recognize new hardware. Without this update, the system won't properly communicate with or control the new interface module or device.
questions.source_quote: you must tell the ROV control system about the change

questions.stem: What is the maximum continuous current that the entire Subsea Node can draw from all 16 ports combined?
questions.explanation: While each individual port can supply up to 15 A continuously, the node itself is rated for a maximum of 30 A total from all ports combined. This means not all ports can provide maximum power simultaneously, and operators must monitor cumulative current draw to avoid exceeding this limit.
questions.source_quote: Subsea Node 2.2 Node Ports Each of the 16 node ports contains an interface module that configures the port for serial, video, or Eth- ernet communication to an attached subsea device. Each interface m

questions.stem: When might a fiber light level drop fail to trigger an alert on the control console touch screen?
options: When the fiber optic cable is completely severed
When the drop was brief or momentary
When the ROV is operating at maximum depth
When the touch screen is in standby mode
questions.correct_answer: When the drop was brief or momentary
questions.explanation: The system may not register or display an alert if the fiber light level only dropped momentarily below threshold, even though this could indicate a developing problem.
questions.source_quote: This event may not appear on the control console touch screen if the level dropped only momentarily

questions.stem: Where are interface module configuration changes made for the Hammerhead system?
options: At the ROV frame-mounted keypad
Using functions on the control console touch screen
Using the dedicated configuration laptop
Through the tether junction box interface
questions.correct_answer: Using functions on the control console touch screen
