questions.source_quote: use configuration functions on the control console touch screen

questions.stem: Which movements does the STATIONKEEP function control to hold the ROV stationary?
options: All six degrees of freedom
Pitch and roll only
Vertical heave only
Fore/aft and starboard/port movements
questions.correct_answer: Fore/aft and starboard/port movements
questions.explanation: STATIONKEEP specifically maintains the ROV's position for fore/aft (forward/backward) and starboard/port (left/right) movements. It does not control vertical positioning or rotational movements like pitch and roll.
questions.source_quote:
STATIONKEEP holds the ROV stationary only for fore/aft and star-
board/port movements.

questions.stem: When changing the heading set point using the flight joystick, when does the ROV establish the new heading?
options: When you tap the number for the current set point
When you release the joystick to its neutral position
When you tap the STBD or PORT button
When you turn the HEADING scroll wheel
questions.correct_answer: When you release the joystick to its neutral position
questions.explanation: The procedure states that after using the joystick to yaw the ROV to a new heading, you must release the flight joystick to its neutral position. The ROV's heading at the moment you release the joystick becomes the new set point.
questions.source_quote: Flight Auto Functions Changing the set point ● Tap STBD or PORT. ● Tap the number for the current set point to allow keypad entry. ● Turn the flight control pod HEADING scroll wheel. ● If the LOCK CON

questions.stem: How do you activate PARK mode on the Schilling ROV system?
options: Navigate to the CONFIGURATION screen, tap OTHER, then tap PARK
Navigate to the HOME screen, tap OTHER, then tap PARK
Navigate to the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER, then tap PARK
Navigate to the FLIGHT screen, tap MODE, then tap PARK
questions.correct_answer: Navigate to the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER, then tap PARK
questions.explanation: According to the manual, PARK mode is activated by going to the FLIGHT screen, selecting OTHER, and then selecting PARK. The other options use incorrect screens or menu selections.
questions.source_quote: On the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER and then tap PARK

questions.stem: What happens to the forward lateral thrusters when the Schilling HD ROV is in PARK mode?
options: They continue to function normally for station-keeping
They are disabled to reduce turbulence near the work site
They operate at reduced power for fine positioning adjustments
They automatically reverse direction to provide braking
questions.correct_answer: They are disabled to reduce turbulence near the work site
questions.explanation: In PARK mode, the forward lateral thrusters are intentionally disabled to minimize turbulence that could disturb the work site. This is a deliberate design limitation to protect sensitive underwater operations.
questions.source_quote: forward lateral thrusters are disabled to reduce turbulence near the work site

questions.stem: When should the PARK auto function be used?
options: When the ROV needs to be emergency recovered to the surface
When the ROV is operating in free-swimming mode with no attachments
During the initial launch phase when the TMS is being deployed from the vessel
When the ROV has been secured by a manipulator grabbing onto a structure
questions.correct_answer: When the ROV has been secured by a manipulator grabbing onto a structure
questions.explanation: The PARK auto function is specifically designed to stabilize and secure the ROV after a manipulator has grabbed onto a structure, maintaining that parked position automatically.
questions.source_quote: Intended for use when the ROV has been parked by using a manipulator to grab onto a structure

questions.stem: What does the MANUAL VELOCITY function enable on the ROV?
options: Maximum thruster output for faster maneuvering speeds
Direct input of depth setpoints without auto-depth engagement
Dynamic braking to minimize overshoot caused by ROV momentum
Manual control of the tether management system
questions.correct_answer: Dynamic braking to minimize overshoot caused by ROV momentum
questions.explanation: MANUAL VELOCITY specifically enables dynamic braking, which controls ROV momentum to reduce overshoot when stopping or changing direction. This helps pilots make precise movements without overshooting their target position.
questions.source_quote: Allows dynamic braking, which minimizes overshoot due to ROV momentum

questions.stem: How do you turn the MANUAL VELOCITY control on or off?
options: By navigating through the system main menu
By holding down the control button for 3 seconds
By tapping the desired control button for each axis
By using the emergency stop button
questions.correct_answer: By tapping the desired control button for each axis
questions.explanation: According to the manual, MANUAL VELOCITY is activated or deactivated simply by tapping the appropriate control button for each axis - no long presses, menus, or emergency controls are required.
questions.source_quote: Tap the desired button for the MANUAL VELOCITY control

questions.stem: Which axes does the MANUAL VELOCITY system provide separate control buttons for on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: LATERAL, VERTICAL, and HEADING
FORWARD, BACKWARD, and LATERAL
DEPTH, PITCH, and YAW
LATERAL, HORIZONTAL, and VERTICAL
questions.correct_answer: LATERAL, VERTICAL, and HEADING
questions.explanation: The MANUAL VELOCITY control system specifically provides dedicated buttons for LATERAL (side-to-side), VERTICAL (up-down), and HEADING (rotation) axes, giving pilots precise, independent control over each movement axis.
questions.source_quote: LATERAL, VERTICAL, and/or HEADING

questions.stem: What happens to vertical thrusters when BOTTOM MODE is ON and the ROV is flying with pitch or roll?
options: Vertical thrusters automatically increase to maintain level flight
The control system does not use vertical thrusters to compensate for ROV motion due to attitude
Vertical thrusters are disabled entirely for all functions
Vertical thrusters switch to world coordinates instead of body coordinates
questions.correct_answer: The control system does not use vertical thrusters to compensate for ROV motion due to attitude
questions.explanation: When BOTTOM MODE is ON, the control system intentionally avoids using vertical thrusters to compensate for pitch or roll during flight. This minimizes stirring up sediment when operating near the seafloor, which is the primary purpose of BOTTOM MODE.
questions.source_quote: Flight Auto Functions Purpose To minimize stirring up sediment, vertical thrusters are not used to compensate for the effects of pitch or roll during flight. Access On the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER and

questions.stem: In Normal velocity mode, what happens when you return the joystick to the neutral position?
options: The ROV comes to an immediate stop
The thrusters reverse to slow the ROV down
The ROV automatically engages its brakes
The thrusters stop, but the ROV continues moving due to momentum
questions.correct_answer: The thrusters stop, but the ROV continues moving due to momentum
questions.explanation: In Normal velocity mode, returning the joystick to neutral stops the thrusters, but the ROV's momentum will cause it to continue moving until drag brings it to a stop.
questions.source_quote: Normal velocity. In this mode, thruster rpm is determined by joystick position. When you return the joystick to the neutral position, the thrusters stop. The ROV's momentum typically causes continued movement.

questions.stem: When you return the joystick to neutral in Manual velocity mode, what happens to stop the ROV?
options: The ROV continues at its current velocity until friction slows it down
An emergency stop procedure is automatically activated
Thrusters simply stop spinning immediately to halt motion
Dynamic braking applies reverse thrust to decelerate the ROV
questions.correct_answer: Dynamic braking applies reverse thrust to decelerate the ROV
questions.explanation: In Manual velocity mode, returning the joystick to neutral does not simply stop the thrusters—it commands zero velocity, which triggers the control system to apply reverse thrust through dynamic braking to bring the ROV to a stop.
questions.source_quote: Returning the joystick to the neutral position does not simply stop the thrusters. Instead, it commands zero velocity, causing the control system to apply reverse thrust to stop the ROV. (This feature is called dynamic braking.)

questions.stem: How do you access MANUAL VELOCITY on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: From the HOME screen, tap SYSTEM, then tap MANUAL VELOCITY
From the DIAGNOSTICS screen, tap SETTINGS, then tap MANUAL VELOCITY
From the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER, then tap MANUAL VELOCITY
From the CONFIGURE screen, tap OPTIONS, then tap MANUAL VELOCITY
questions.correct_answer: From the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER, then tap MANUAL VELOCITY
questions.explanation: The MANUAL VELOCITY function is accessed directly from the FLIGHT screen by tapping the OTHER menu, then selecting MANUAL VELOCITY. This is the specific navigation path defined in the system architecture.
questions.source_quote: On the FLIGHT screen, tap OTHER and then tap MANUAL VELOCITY

questions.stem: What type of AUTO HEADING mode is PARK on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: A manual heading mode that requires constant pilot input
An AUTO HEADING mode that uses only the aft horizontal thrusters
An AUTO HEADING mode that uses all four horizontal thrusters
A depth-hold mode that uses the vertical thruster to maintain position
questions.correct_answer: An AUTO HEADING mode that uses only the aft horizontal thrusters
questions.explanation: PARK specifically uses only the aft horizontal thrusters to maintain heading in AUTO HEADING mode, not all horizontal thrusters or vertical thrusters, and it operates automatically without requiring manual pilot input.
questions.source_quote: An AUTO HEADING mode that uses only the aft horizontal thrusters

questions.stem: Which sensors does manual velocity mode use for ROV control?
options: Camera, sonar, and altimeter
Torpedo system, tether, and manipulator
DVL, depth sensor, and gyro/MRU
Pressure sensors, GPS, and magnetometer
questions.correct_answer: DVL, depth sensor, and gyro/MRU
questions.explanation: Manual velocity mode relies on three key sensor inputs: the DVL for velocity measurements, the depth sensor for depth information, and the gyro/MRU for orientation and motion reference. These sensors work together to provide accurate ROV positioning and control.
questions.source_quote: control is based on inputs from the DVL, depth sensor, and gyro/MRU

questions.stem: Which three movements can be independently enabled for manual velocity control using the Manual Velocity panel?
options: Surge, sway, and heave
Forward, backward, and turning
Pitch, roll, and yaw
Lateral, vertical, and heading
questions.correct_answer: Lateral, vertical, and heading
questions.explanation: The Manual Velocity panel lets pilots turn on manual velocity control separately for lateral (fore/aft and port/starboard), vertical (up/down), and heading (yaw) movements, allowing independent control of each axis.
questions.source_quote: The MANUAL VELOCITY panel lets you turn on manual velocity control separately for: LATERAL movement (fore/aft and starboard/port), VERTICAL movement (up/down), HEADING movement (yaw)

questions.stem: What must you do in the NaviPac setup application before you can use AutoTrack?

Calibrate the navigation sensors for AutoTrack mode
Add a new data output set to Schilling Autofollow
Enable the AutoTrack plugin from the main menu

th AutoTrack, you
must add a new data output (set to Schilling Autofollow) in the NaviPac setup application.

questions.stem: What does turning the 'auto' function on and off actually do on a Schilling HD ROV?

Activate automatic depth hold mode

auto
function on and off is instead used to set ROV speed to zero (stop).

options: AutoTrack disables and all thrusters stop completely
AutoTrack remains enabled and thrusters continue responding to heading and cross-track error commands
Only the forward thrusters stop while lateral positioning thrusters are disabled
The entire ROV system shuts down and requires a full restart

questions.explanation: Tapping STOP only halts forward movement while keeping AutoTrack active, so thrusters continue to maintain heading and cross-track positioning as commanded.

questions.stem: What happens to the joystick/screen speed setting when you press the STOP button on a Schilling ROV?
options: It increases to maximum speed
It switches to autopilot mode
It is zeroed to zero
It remains at the previous setting
questions.correct_answer: It is zeroed to zero
questions.explanation: The STOP button immediately resets the joystick and screen speed settings to zero, stopping the ROV's propulsion. However, environmental factors like water currents can still affect the ROV's drift even after the speed is zeroed.
questions.source_quote: Although pressing STOP zeroes the joystick/screen speed setting, water current and/or

questions.stem: What are the steps to select and confirm your choice of manipulator in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Select the position-controlled manipulator and tap CANCEL
Select the velocity-controlled manipulator and tap CONTINUE
Select the position-controlled manipulator and tap CONTINUE
Select the position-controlled manipulator and tap CONFIRM

questions.explanation: The manual specifies that you must select the position-controlled manipulator and tap CONTINUE to proceed with your chosen manipulator.
questions.source_quote: Select the position-controlled manipulator that you wish to use, and tap CONTINUE.

questions.stem: Which of the following is a valid manual method to disable AutoTrack?

Tapping the touch screen AutoTrack button
Waiting for survey data to be interrupted for 3 seconds

Power cycle the entire ROV control system

questions.stem: What condition would cause the control system to automatically shut down the ROV HPU motor?
options: Hydraulic fluid appears discolored
Oil pressure drops below 500 PSI
Motor temperature exceeds 80 °C (176 °F)
Oil temperature exceeds 70 °C (158 °F)
questions.correct_answer: Motor temperature exceeds 80 °C (176 °F)
questions.explanation: The control system automatically turns off electrical power to the HPU motor if the motor temperature exceeds 80 °C (176 °F) as a safety limit to prevent damage.
questions.source_quote: Hydraulic Circuit Operation and Control 1. On the FLIGHT screen, tap HPU. 2. Tap the tab for the desired hydraulic circuit to display the circuit’s HYDRAULIC PRESSURE CONTROL panel. 3. To change the p

questions.stem: What determines whether a parameter on the Schilling HD ROV can trigger an alarm?
options: It is affected by user-set alarm thresholds
It is monitored by the camera system
It is connected to the main power bus
It is controlled by the hydraulic system
questions.correct_answer: It is affected by user-set alarm thresholds
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV defines alarm-triggering parameters as those affected by user-set alarm thresholds, allowing operators to configure acceptable ranges for specific functions or readings.
questions.source_quote: affected by user-set alarm thresholds.

questions.stem: Why must personnel and equipment stay clear of the pan and tilt during calibration?
options: Because the pan and tilt generates heat during calibration that poses a burn risk
Because the pan and tilt must be isolated from power for accurate calibration
Because the pan and tilt emits electromagnetic interference that can affect nearby electronics
Because the pan and tilt performs a series of automatic movements during calibration that could cause injury
questions.correct_answer: Because the pan and tilt performs a series of automatic movements during calibration that could cause injury
questions.explanation: During calibration, the pan and tilt executes a sequence of automatic movements. These unpredictable motions could strike personnel or damage equipment if they are too close, so maintaining a safe clearance is essential for injury prevention.
questions.source_quote:
Because the pan and tilt performs a series of automatic movements during calibration,
keep personnel and equipment clear of the pan and tilt.

questions.stem: What is the key functional difference between the flight joystick trigger and the foot pedal when controlling the TMS tether?
options: The trigger moves tether at a fixed speed, while the foot pedal provides proportional control that increases with pedal pressure
The trigger provides proportional control based on how far you push it, while the foot pedal moves at a fixed speed
The trigger only works when the foot pedal is released, while the foot pedal operates independently
The trigger controls TMS speed limits, while the foot pedal controls winch direction
questions.correct_answer: The trigger moves tether at a fixed speed, while the foot pedal provides proportional control that increases with pedal pressure
questions.explanation: The flight joystick trigger is a momentary rocker switch that moves the tether at a set speed (nonproportional), while the foot pedal provides proportional control where the farther you press toward the floor, the faster the tether moves within the selected speed range.
questions.source_quote: TMS Control Overview Device Function Flight joystick ● Trigger-type momentary rocker switch moves tether with nonproportional control (moves tether at a set speed). ● Push button switch toggles betwee

questions.stem: Before you can enable pump control on the Schilling HD ROV, what system configuration must first be completed?
options: Hydraulic pressure must reach maximum operating levels
The pump must be running at minimum speed
The thruster matrix must be calibrated
A valve must be assigned to the pump
questions.correct_answer: A valve must be assigned to the pump
questions.explanation: The pump control system requires a valve to be assigned before it can be enabled—this is a configuration prerequisite. Without this assignment, the system will not allow pump control to be activated.
questions.source_quote: A valve has not been assigned to the pump

questions.stem: Where can valve operation be assigned on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: Switches or knobs on the tool control pod
The joystick controller only
The main control panel
The hydraulic manifold directly
questions.correct_answer: Switches or knobs on the tool control pod
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, valve operation can be assigned to switches or knobs on the tool control pod, allowing operators to customize control mapping for specific tasks.
questions.source_quote: You can assign valve operation to a switch or knob on the tool control pod

questions.stem: Under what condition will pump control for an assigned valve fail to enable?
options: The manual dump valve is in the open position
The hydraulic fluid temperature exceeds normal operating limits
The ROV depth rating has been exceeded
The valve pack for the assigned valve has lost communications
questions.correct_answer: The valve pack for the assigned valve has lost communications
questions.explanation: Communications with the valve pack is a prerequisite for pump control—if the valve pack loses communications, the system will not allow pump control to be enabled for that valve as a safety measure.
questions.source_quote: The valve pack for the assigned valve has lost communications

questions.stem: What must be configured before pump control can be enabled on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Seafloor depth must be set
Hydraulic pressure must be calibrated
Camera system must be powered on
Tether tension must be adjusted
questions.correct_answer: Seafloor depth must be set
questions.explanation: The system will not allow pump control to be enabled until the seafloor depth has been properly set. This is a mandatory configuration step required by the ROV's control logic for proper hydraulics operation.
questions.source_quote: Seafloor depth has not been set

questions.stem: How do you start pump operations on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Press and hold the PUMP CONTROL button
Tap the PUMP CONTROL button
Toggle the HYDRAULIC POWER switch
Double-tap the PUMP CONTROL button
questions.correct_answer: Tap the PUMP CONTROL button
questions.explanation: The operator manual specifies a simple tap action on the PUMP CONTROL button to initiate pump operations; no holding, toggling, or multiple taps are required.
questions.source_quote: Tap the PUMP CONTROL button to start pump operations.

questions.stem: When exporting chart data from the ROV system, where should you connect the external memory device?

To the same client computer being used for the export operation
To a separate backup computer dedicated to data storage
Directly to the ROV tether connection
questions.correct_answer: To the same client computer being used for the export operation
questions.explanation: The procedure requires you to connect the external memory device to the same client computer that is running the export operation. This ensures the data transfers directly from the client to the memory device without additional steps or potential data routing issues.
questions.source_quote: Connect the external memory device (like a USB flash drive) to the client computer that you are using for the export operation

questions.stem: After assigning a valve to pod controls, what happens to the valve's touch screen controls?

They are automatically disabled
They continue to function normally
They must be manually reactivated before use

questions.explanation: The manual states that valves can still be operated directly through their touch screen controls even after assigning them to pod controls. Both control methods remain functional and available to the operator.
questions.source_quote: continue to operate the valve with the valve's touch screen controls

questions.stem: What will prevent pump control from being enabled on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: The hydraulic reservoir is below normal fluid level
The ROV is currently in manual steering mode
The pump motor has not reached operating temperature
The valve pack for the assigned valve is powered off
questions.correct_answer: The valve pack for the assigned valve is powered off

questions.source_quote: The valve pack for the assigned valve is powered off

questions.stem: When configuring hydraulic controls for an ROV, what type of control should proportional valves typically be assigned to?
options: Pedals
Knobs
Switches
Levers
questions.correct_answer: Knobs
questions.explanation: Proportional valves require gradual, adjustable control rather than simple on/off signals. Knobs allow operators to smoothly adjust flow rates and pressure, while switches only provide binary on/off states that cannot achieve proportional control.
questions.source_quote: proportional valves are typically assigned to knobs rather than switches

questions.stem: On the TACTILE CONTROL ASSIGNMENT panel, what do you select from the drop-down list to assign valve operation?

questions.stem: What is the first step to assign a valve to a pod control?

Navigate to Main Menu and tap TOOLING

Tap the HYDRAULICS tab in the upper right of the tooling menu
Press and hold the VALVES button in the center of the main menu
Double-tap the VALVES tab in the lower right of the tooling menu

Double-tap the lower right corner of the control interface
Press and hold the center button until the valve list appears
Swipe right on the touchpad to open the valve menu

A knob
A lever
A toggle switch

Flow through port A stops completely

questions.stem: What does a single-function switch control in the ROV hydraulic system?
options: The main hydraulic pressure
Multiple valves simultaneously
The hydraulic fluid flow rate
One valve

questions.explanation: A single-function switch is designed to operate a single valve, providing dedicated control for one specific hydraulic function.
