questions.stem: What does a two-function switch operate?
options: Four valves in sequence
Three valves at the same time
Two valves simultaneously
One valve at a time
questions.correct_answer: Two valves simultaneously
questions.explanation: A two-function switch is designed to control two valves at the same time, which is useful for coordinating hydraulic functions on the ROV.
questions.source_quote: Two-Function Switch (valve 1 and valve 2)

questions.stem: What functions does the Video Computer App provide for ROV video operations?
options: Edit video and transmit footage to shore
Stream live video only to the control station
Capture screenshots and generate mission reports
Preview, record, and play back video
questions.correct_answer: Preview, record, and play back video
questions.explanation: The Video Computer App is designed specifically for managing video at the workstation, allowing pilots to preview footage before recording, capture video during the dive, and review playback afterward. The other options describe functions not listed as capabilities of this app.
questions.source_quote: Preview, record, and play back video

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV manual, what is the purpose of place markers in video recordings?
options: To increase video resolution quality
To flag and identify the locations of specific events
To automatically track object movement across frames
To compress video files for storage
questions.correct_answer: To flag and identify the locations of specific events
questions.explanation: Place markers are a labeling tool that lets operators mark specific moments in video footage so they can quickly find and identify important events later.
questions.source_quote: Put place markers in videos to mark the locations of events

questions.stem: What video formats does the Video Computer App support?
options: SD (standard-definition) only
Both SD (standard-definition) and HD (high-definition)
HD (high-definition) only
4K and SD (standard-definition)
questions.correct_answer: Both SD (standard-definition) and HD (high-definition)
questions.explanation: The Video Computer App in the Schilling HD ROV system supports both standard-definition and high-definition video formats, providing flexibility for different operational requirements.
questions.source_quote: For both SD (standard-definition) and HD (high-definition) video

questions.stem: What can operators do with overlays on the ROV camera system?
options: Create and manage overlays that can be superimposed over live camera views and recorded video
Create overlays but only apply them to live camera views
Only view pre-made overlays that are permanently installed in the system
Delete overlays from recorded video but cannot add them to it
questions.correct_answer: Create and manage overlays that can be superimposed over live camera views and recorded video
questions.explanation: The system gives operators full control to create new overlays and manage existing ones, and these overlays function on both real-time camera feeds and recorded video playback.
questions.source_quote: Create and manage overlays that can be added to (superimposed over) camera views and recorded video

questions.stem: What function does the Video Computer App provide for ROV operators?
options: Download pre-mission survey maps
Adjust the camera's optical zoom remotely
Record audio annotations alongside video
Control the ROV's thruster power output
questions.correct_answer: Record audio annotations alongside video
questions.explanation: The Video Computer App includes a feature that allows operators to capture spoken notes while recording video, enabling documentation of observations directly synchronized with the visual feed.
questions.source_quote: Record audio annotations

questions.stem: When should you take a diagnostic snapshot on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: At the start of every dive as standard practice
During scheduled routine maintenance periods
Whenever you notice something unusual during operations
Only when instructed to do so by a factory support technician
questions.correct_answer: Only when instructed to do so by a factory support technician
questions.explanation: Diagnostic snapshots should only be taken when directed by factory support, as the technician will tell you when the file is needed for troubleshooting specific issues.
questions.source_quote: A support technician will tell you when this file is needed

questions.stem: What is the required file extension for snapshot files in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: .srs
.ssf
.jpg
.snap
questions.correct_answer: .srs
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV system requires snapshot files to have the .srs extension. Using any other extension will prevent the system from recognizing and saving the file properly.
questions.source_quote: The file name must have the extension .srs.

questions.stem: How do you change the default file name for a snapshot file?
options: Access the system settings menu and locate the file naming section
Contact the system administrator to update the naming convention
Press and hold the snapshot button for three seconds
Click the current name and type a new one
questions.correct_answer: Click the current name and type a new one
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you simply click on the current file name and type your replacement name directly. No menu navigation or admin involvement is required.
questions.source_quote: If you wish to change the default file name for the snapshot file, click the name and type a new one.

questions.stem: How do you create a complete system snapshot on the Schilling ROV?
options: Left-click the diagnostic snapshot icon and select 'Complete System Snapshot'
Right-click the diagnostic snapshot icon and select 'Partial System Snapshot'
Double-click the diagnostic snapshot icon and select 'Complete System Snapshot'
Right-click the diagnostic snapshot icon and select 'Complete System Snapshot'
questions.correct_answer: Right-click the diagnostic snapshot icon and select 'Complete System Snapshot'
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that creating a complete snapshot requires right-clicking (not left-clicking or double-clicking) the diagnostic snapshot icon and selecting 'Complete System Snapshot' from the menu. This is the correct procedure for capturing full system diagnostics.
questions.source_quote: To create a complete snapshot, right-click the icon and then click Complete System Snapshot

questions.stem: What does the diagnostic snapshot feature do on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Creates a file containing system information for factory technical support to use when diagnosing problems
Creates a display on the pilot console showing real-time system status
Generates a routine data log file stored locally for the pilot's reference
Performs automatic repairs on faulty system components
questions.correct_answer: Creates a file containing system information for factory technical support to use when diagnosing problems
questions.explanation: The diagnostic snapshot specifically creates a file with system information that factory technical support personnel use to diagnose problems remotely.
questions.source_quote: The diagnostic snapshot feature creates a file that contains information for use by factory technical support personnel in diagnosing problems

questions.stem: On which ROV system configuration are diagnostic snapshots available?
options: Systems equipped with HDEVS
Systems equipped with TAVS
Systems equipped with either HDEVS or TAVS
Systems without either HDEVS or TAVS
questions.correct_answer: Systems equipped with HDEVS
questions.explanation: Diagnostic snapshots are a feature that is only supported on ROVs that have the HDEVS hardware; the TAVS configuration does not include this capability.
questions.source_quote: This information applies only to ROV systems that have HDEVS rather than TAVS

questions.stem: How do you locate specific videos in the browser-accessible video log?
options: Navigate through the folder tree manually
Click the search icon and enter the video filename
Select the desired project using the project drop-down list
Use the date filter to sort all videos by creation date
questions.correct_answer: Select the desired project using the project drop-down list
questions.explanation: The project drop-down list in the browser-accessible video log allows you to filter and locate specific videos by their associated project.
questions.source_quote: Use the project drop-down list to select the desired project.

questions.stem: What should you do after naming a snapshot file to store it?
options: Click CANCEL to abort the operation
Click SAVE to store the snapshot
Click EXPORT to send the file
Click PRINT to create a hard copy
questions.correct_answer: Click SAVE to store the snapshot
questions.explanation: According to the manual, after naming the file you must click SAVE to actually store the snapshot on the system. EXPORT sends the file elsewhere, CANCEL aborts the action, and PRINT produces a physical copy rather than saving a digital file.
questions.source_quote: Click SAVE.

questions.stem: What must you do before physically removing an external memory device from the ROV system?
options: Safely eject the external memory device
Turn off the ROV main power first
Remove the device while the system is still recording
Wait for the data transfer light to flash
questions.correct_answer: Safely eject the external memory device
questions.explanation: Always safely eject the external memory device through the software before pulling it out physically. This prevents data corruption and ensures all files are properly saved and closed.
questions.source_quote: Safely eject the external memory device

questions.stem: What should you do with a saved .srs file when troubleshooting an issue with the ROV system?
options: Send it to a technical support representative for analysis
Keep it stored locally on the control room computer
Upload it to the ROV's main operating system
Delete it to free up system storage
questions.correct_answer: Send it to a technical support representative for analysis
questions.explanation: The procedure states that saved .srs files should be sent to a technical support representative who can analyze the file to help diagnose and resolve system issues.
questions.source_quote: send the .srs file to a technical support representative.

questions.stem: What happens when the internal drives of the Video Computer reach full storage capacity during continuous video backup mode?
options: The system switches to recording on only one drive to conserve space
Recording stops automatically and requires manual intervention to resume
Video is automatically transferred to an external memory device
The oldest video is automatically deleted to make room for new recordings
questions.correct_answer: The oldest video is automatically deleted to make room for new recordings
questions.explanation: The internal drives work in continuous backup mode, which means that once the drives are full, the system automatically overwrites the oldest video files to make room for new recordings. This ensures continuous recording capability without user intervention, while still maintaining simultaneous dual-drive protection.
questions.source_quote: Video Recording Start-to-Finish Guide 10.1 Overview of Video Computer Recording This information applies only to ROV systems that have HDEVS rather than TAVS. The start-to-finish guide for video recor

questions.stem: What type of port is used to connect external memory devices to the video computer?
options: USB 3 port
SD card slot
Serial RS-232 port
Ethernet port
questions.correct_answer: USB 3 port
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, external memory devices connect to the video computer using a USB 3 port.
questions.source_quote: using a USB 3 port

questions.stem: What must you do to use the client recording feature on the Schilling HD ROV video computer?
options: Connect an additional monitor to the video output
Attach an external memory device to a USB 3 port
Use the standard USB 2 port on the control panel
Install specialized recording software on the ROV
questions.correct_answer: Attach an external memory device to a USB 3 port
questions.explanation: The client recording feature works by connecting an external memory device directly to the video computer's USB 3 port, which allows you to copy video segments to that device while recording is in progress.
questions.source_quote: client recording feature lets you attach an external memory device to the video computer (using a USB 3 port) and copy video segments to that device during recording

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of client recordings in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: To store navigation data separate from video footage
To enable live video streaming to multiple locations simultaneously
To provide redundant copies of video in case the primary recording is lost or corrupted
To improve video quality through real-time processing
questions.correct_answer: To provide redundant copies of video in case the primary recording is lost or corrupted
questions.explanation: Client recordings serve as a backup system, creating redundant copies of video data so that if the primary recording is lost or corrupted, operators still have access to the footage.
questions.source_quote: These client recordings provide redundant

questions.stem: When adjusting the audio input volume for video recording annotations on the Schilling ROV system, what level should you aim for on the screen gauge?
options: Full volume (100%) of the screen gauge
Two-thirds (approximately 66%) of the screen gauge
One-third (approximately 33%) of the screen gauge
Half volume (50%) of the screen gauge
questions.correct_answer: Two-thirds (approximately 66%) of the screen gauge
questions.explanation: The procedure specifies that you should adjust the audio input volume until it fills two-thirds of the screen gauge. This ensures adequate audio levels without distortion from being too loud.
questions.source_quote: Video Recording Start-to-Finish Guide 1. In the Recording Details panel, select the required number of channels and the recording resolution (HD or SD). 2. Select the desired video quality. 3. If you

questions.stem: Which ROV system type requires the client recording procedure to be used?
options: Both HDEVS and TAVS
Neither HDEVS nor TAVS
HDEVS
TAVS
questions.correct_answer: HDEVS
questions.explanation: The client recording procedure is a limitation that applies only to ROV systems equipped with HDEVS, not TAVS. This is a specific feature distinction between the two system types.
questions.source_quote: This information applies only to ROV systems that have HDEVS rather than TAVS

questions.stem: Where do you configure the flight joystick on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Touch screen interface
Physical switch panel on the ROV frame
Laptop computer connected to the ROV
Handheld pendant controller
questions.correct_answer: Touch screen interface
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV system uses its integrated touch screen interface specifically for flight joystick configuration. This is done directly through the ROV system's built-in display, not through external computers, frame-mounted switches, or the pendant controller.
questions.source_quote: Flight joystick configuration is performed from the ROV system touch screen

questions.stem: Which joystick button can be configured to trigger unplanned events while you continue to fly the ROV?
options: Button 1
Button 3
Button 2
Any joystick button
questions.correct_answer: Button 2
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that flight joystick button 2 is designed to be configured as an event trigger, allowing you to mark events, capture still photos, and record audio without having to release the joystick or stop flying.
questions.source_quote: Video Recording Start-to-Finish Guide ●Adding an event place marker generates a still photo from every camera that was recording at the time of the event. ●At any time after a video segment has been r

questions.stem: Before beginning ROV operations, what should you do regarding client recording?
options: Check camera resolution
Start client recording
Stop client recording
Deploy the ROV to the work site
questions.correct_answer: Start client recording
questions.explanation: Client recording should be initiated at the start of operations to document all work performed. This ensures a complete record for the client and any required reporting.
questions.source_quote: Start client recording.

questions.stem: What happens when you click the camera icon below the playback progress bar on the PLAYBACK screen of the Video Computer App?
options: Adds an event marker that applies only to the currently viewed video segment
Creates an unplanned event at the current playback position AND creates a still photo of that video frame
Deletes the current video segment and starts a new recording
Compresses the video file to reduce storage space
questions.correct_answer: Creates an unplanned event at the current playback position AND creates a still photo of that video frame
questions.explanation: According to the manual, clicking the camera icon creates an unplanned event at the current playback position and also creates a still photo of that video frame. Additionally, this event place marker is added to all video segments that were being recorded at the same time as the segment you are viewing.
questions.source_quote: Video Recording Start-to-Finish Guide 6. Finalize all external memory devices (in the ENABLED STORAGE panel of the DRIVE MANAGEMENT screen of the Video Computer App). You must finalize each device to

questions.stem: Where can you view a list of all event place markers in recorded video segments?
options: Events tab
Recording tab
Markers tab
Video playback tab
questions.correct_answer: Events tab
questions.explanation: The Events tab is specifically designed to display all event place markers within recorded video segments for easy review and navigation.
questions.source_quote: Click the Events tab to see a list of all event place markers in the video segments

questions.stem: What are the steps to export video segments from the ROV system?
options: Right-click on segments and choose 'Save As', then select the destination folder
Check boxes next to desired segments, select a destination drive from the drop-down list, and click Export
Drag segments to the destination drive icon on the desktop
Click the Print button, then select Export from the submenu
questions.correct_answer: Check boxes next to desired segments, select a destination drive from the drop-down list, and click Export
questions.explanation: The correct procedure for exporting video segments is to first check the boxes next to the desired segments, then select the destination drive from the drop-down list, and finally click the Export button to complete the process.
questions.source_quote: Put check marks in boxes next to all video segments that you want to export. Select the destination drive from the drop-down list. Click Export again.

questions.stem: What happens to recorded audio annotations when video segments are exported from the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: They are saved as separate audio files only
They must be manually added after export
They are automatically incorporated into the video files
They are deleted and cannot be recovered
questions.correct_answer: They are automatically incorporated into the video files
questions.explanation: The system automatically embeds audio annotations into the exported video during the export process, so operators don't need to manually sync or add them afterward.
questions.source_quote: recorded audio annotations are incorporated into the video segments

questions.stem: When exporting video segments, which recording source typically provides the fastest export times?
options: External storage device
Client recording
Main system
Backup system
questions.correct_answer: Client recording
questions.explanation: Exporting video segments from client recording is faster than from the main system because client recording bypasses the additional processing overhead of the main system.
questions.source_quote: Exporting segments from client recording will typically take a shorter time

questions.stem: What is automatically created when video segments are exported to an external memory device?
options: A video log file
A separate audio transcription file
A backup copy of all system settings
An automatic email notification to the supervisor
questions.correct_answer: A video log file
questions.explanation: The system automatically generates a video log file whenever video segments are exported to external storage, providing documentation of the exported footage.
questions.source_quote: a video log is created

questions.stem: When setting up external memory devices for client recording on a Schilling ROV, which screen do you navigate to configure them?
options: Camera Setup screen
Drive Management screen of the Video Computer App
Network Diagnostics panel
System Configuration menu
questions.correct_answer: Drive Management screen of the Video Computer App
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you must go to the DRIVE MANAGEMENT screen of the Video Computer App and access the AVAILABLE STORAGE panel to configure external memory devices for recording. The other screens are unrelated to storage device management.
questions.source_quote: Edit the overlay layout according to the job specifications. 10.2.1.2 Setting up Devices for Client Recording Prepare for client recording by setting up the external memory devices. 1. Connect to the

questions.stem: What improvement can you expect when you delete video segments from the internal drives in the Video Computer App?
options: Faster sonar refresh rates
Automatic upload to external storage
Higher video resolution during recording
Improved navigation in the PLAYBACK screen
questions.correct_answer: Improved navigation in the PLAYBACK screen
questions.explanation: According to the manual, deleting video segments from internal drives makes it easier to navigate through the PLAYBACK screen of the Video Computer App by reducing the amount of stored data.
questions.source_quote: This will make navigation easier in the PLAYBACK screen of the Video Computer App

questions.stem: How does an operator delete videos using the Video Computer App?
options: Open the VIDEO MANAGER screen and click Delete Videos
Open the SETTINGS menu and select Delete Videos
Open the PLAYBACK screen and select Delete from the File menu
Open the PLAYBACK screen and click Delete Videos
questions.correct_answer: Open the PLAYBACK screen and click Delete Videos
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the correct procedure is to display the PLAYBACK screen of the Video Computer App and click Delete Videos. The other options use incorrect screen names or navigation methods.
questions.source_quote: Display the PLAYBACK screen of the Video Computer App and click Delete Videos

questions.stem: How do you access the tooling package screen in the Schilling HD ROV control software?
options: Swipe right from the HOME screen until you reach the tooling menu
Tap the TOOLING tab on any CONFIGURATION screen
Select TOOLING from the dropdown menu on the DASHBOARD screen
Press the TOOLING button on the main toolbar
questions.correct_answer: Tap the TOOLING tab on any CONFIGURATION screen
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you access the tooling package screen by tapping the TOOLING tab while on any CONFIGURATION screen. This is a direct navigation path through the software's configuration interface.
questions.source_quote: On any CONFIGURATION screen, tap the TOOLING tab

questions.stem: What function does the tooling package screen provide?
options: Configuring pilot control preferences
Viewing real-time camera feeds
Monitoring hydraulic pressure levels
Adding and removing tooling
questions.correct_answer: Adding and removing tooling
questions.explanation: The tooling package screen is specifically designed for managing the ROV's tooling configuration. It allows operators to add new tools to the system or remove tools that are no longer needed.
questions.source_quote: Add and remove tooling

questions.stem: What file name extension do configuration backup files stored on the external memory device use?
options: .srConfig
.backup
.srBackup
.cfg
questions.correct_answer: .srBackup
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifies that configuration backup files use the .srBackup extension when stored on external memory devices.
questions.source_quote: file name extension .srBackup

questions.stem: What information does the tooling package screen display in the HD ROV control interface?
options: Information about installed tooling packages including manipulators
Depth, heading, and altitude navigation data
Real-time video feeds from the ROV's camera systems
Thruster performance data and propulsion status
questions.correct_answer: Information about installed tooling packages including manipulators
questions.explanation: The tooling package screen is specifically designed to show operators what tooling packages and manipulators are currently installed on the ROV system.
questions.source_quote: View information about installed tooling packages (including manipulators)

questions.stem: After tapping the LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP button, what appears on the control room screens?
options: Both the right and left screens display configuration backup selection controls
A single configuration file auto-loads without user selection
Only the right screen displays configuration backup selection controls
The main navigation menu opens on both screens
questions.correct_answer: Both the right and left screens display configuration backup selection controls
questions.explanation: Tapping the LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP button simultaneously activates selection controls on both screens, allowing the pilot to choose which configuration backup to load from the right and left displays.
questions.source_quote: Both the right and left screens then display controls for selecting configuration backups.

questions.stem: What must happen to the CONFIGURATION button before an operator can restore a backup on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: A warning light must flash on the main control panel
The button must display BACKUP IN PROGRESS
The operator must manually press and hold the CONFIGURATION button for 5 seconds
It must automatically change to indicate LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP
questions.correct_answer: It must automatically change to indicate LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP
questions.explanation: The system requires the CONFIGURATION button to automatically change to indicate LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP as a precondition before the operator can proceed with restoring a backup.
questions.source_quote: when the CONFIGURATION button changes to LOAD CONFIGURATION BACKUP

questions.stem: What is the first step to view or edit tooling package information on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Navigate to the DIAGNOSTICS menu and select TOOLING
Open the SETTINGS menu and find the TOOLING option
Press the TOOLING button in the main toolbar
Tap the TOOLING tab on any CONFIGURATION screen
questions.correct_answer: Tap the TOOLING tab on any CONFIGURATION screen
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that on any CONFIGURATION screen, you tap the TOOLING tab as the first step to access tooling package information. The other options describe incorrect navigation paths that don't follow the actual procedure.
questions.source_quote: 1. On any CONFIGURATION screen, tap the TOOLING tab

questions.stem: What function does the tooling package screen provide in the Schilling HD ROV?
options: It displays real-time hydraulic pressure readings from all systems
It provides video feed monitoring for cameras and lights
It controls the main propulsion thruster outputs
It enables configuration of tooling valve assignments
questions.correct_answer: It enables configuration of tooling valve assignments
questions.explanation: The tooling package screen is specifically designed for configuring how the ROV's tooling valves are assigned and controlled. This allows pilots to set up hydraulic functions for attached tooling like manipulators or cutters. Other screens handle pressure monitoring, propulsion, and camera systems.
questions.source_quote: Configure tooling valve assignments

questions.stem: What does the letter 'P' indicate when it appears next to a valve function on a hydraulic schematic?
options: A pressure relief valve
A pilot-operated valve
A primary valve
A proportional valve
questions.correct_answer: A proportional valve
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the letter 'P' is used to identify a proportional valve. This designation helps pilots and technicians quickly recognize valves that can modulate flow proportionally rather than simply being fully open or fully closed.
questions.source_quote: a P identifies a proportional valve

questions.stem: Can you configure the hydraulic valve settings for position-controlled manipulators (Titan 4 and Conan 7P) through the ROV control system?
options: Yes, all manipulator settings can be adjusted directly through the ROV control system
Only the Titan 4 can be configured through the ROV control system; the Conan 7P cannot
Yes, but only when the ROV is powered by surface power supply
No, these manipulators use dedicated hydraulic valves that cannot be configured through the ROV control system
questions.correct_answer: No, these manipulators use dedicated hydraulic valves that cannot be configured through the ROV control system
questions.explanation: Position-controlled manipulators like the Titan 4 and Conan 7P use dedicated hydraulic valves that are hardwired and cannot be modified through the ROV control system. This is a fundamental design limitation of these manipulator models.
questions.source_quote: Because position-controlled manipulators (like the Titan 4 and Conan 7P) use dedicated hydraulic valves, you cannot configure these values through the ROV control system.

questions.stem: What is the first step to assign a valve in the ROV control interface?
options: Press and hold the function button for 3 seconds
Navigate to the system settings menu first
Tap the drop-down arrow for the desired function
Double-tap directly on the valve icon
questions.correct_answer: Tap the drop-down arrow for the desired function
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you tap the drop-down arrow for the desired function to display a list of available valve pack values. This opens the selection menu where you can then choose the appropriate valve.
questions.source_quote: Tap the drop-down arrow for the desired function, and the screen displays a list of available valve pack values

questions.stem: What is the first step to assign a valve to a tool function on the ROV control screen?
options: Navigate to the valve configuration menu in the system settings
Access the system diagnostics panel before proceeding
Tap directly on the valve icon in the main display area
Select the desired tool from the list on the left side of the screen
questions.correct_answer: Select the desired tool from the list on the left side of the screen
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that you must first select the desired tool from the list on the left side of the screen to view its available functions before you can assign a valve.
questions.source_quote: On the list of tools on the left side of the screen, tap the button for the desired tool

questions.stem: When configuring valve assignments in the ROV system, which valves will NOT appear in the available valve pack values list?
options: Valves that are already assigned to other functions
Valves that are currently experiencing hydraulic pressure
Valves that have been marked as defective during inspection
Valves located in the primary hydraulic manifold
questions.correct_answer: Valves that are already assigned to other functions
questions.explanation: The manual states that valves already assigned to other uses do not appear on the list. Once a valve is assigned to a specific function, the system removes it from the available options to prevent duplicate assignments.
questions.source_quote: Valves that are already assigned to other uses do not appear on the list

questions.stem: What is the first step to assign a valve to a tool function?
options: Open the MAIN MENU and select the TOOLING option
Go to the DIAGNOSTICS tab within any CONFIGURATION screen
Access any CONFIGURATION screen and tap the TOOLING tab
Navigate to the VALVE CONTROL screen and tap the ASSIGN button
questions.correct_answer: Access any CONFIGURATION screen and tap the TOOLING tab
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you must first access any CONFIGURATION screen and then tap the TOOLING tab to assign a valve to a tool function. This is the designated path for tool configuration.
questions.source_quote: On any CONFIGURATION screen, tap the TOOLING tab

questions.stem: What does tactile control configuration do on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Adjusts the camera zoom and focus settings
Assigns and sets up the function of controls on the flight joystick and tether control devices
Configures the hydraulic pressure settings for the manipulator arm
Monitors the performance of the ROV's propulsion system
questions.correct_answer: Assigns and sets up the function of controls on the flight joystick and tether control devices
questions.explanation: Tactile control configuration specifically handles assigning and setting up the function of controls on the flight joystick and tether control devices, which is what the control configuration function does in the Control Room and Software subsystem.
questions.source_quote: Configures and assigns controls on the flight joystick and tether control devices

questions.stem: On a Schilling HD ROV, what does the letter 'S' next to a valve function indicate?
options: It signals a spool valve
It marks a safety valve
It indicates a shutoff valve
It identifies a solenoid valve
questions.correct_answer: It identifies a solenoid valve
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that an S next to a valve function identifies a solenoid valve. This standardized marking system helps pilots quickly recognize solenoid-operated valves in the hydraulic system.
questions.source_quote: An S next to a valve function identifies a solenoid valve

questions.stem: How do you access tactile control settings on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Go to the DIAGNOSTICS menu and select TACTILE CONTROLS
Open any CONFIGURATION screen, tap the TACTILE CONTROLS tab, then select either JOYSTICK CONTROL or TETHER CONTROL
Press and hold the main menu button, then select TACTILE CONTROLS
Navigate to the CALIBRATION screen and tap TACTILE CONTROLS
questions.correct_answer: Open any CONFIGURATION screen, tap the TACTILE CONTROLS tab, then select either JOYSTICK CONTROL or TETHER CONTROL
questions.explanation: The tactile control settings are accessed through the CONFIGURATION menu by selecting the TACTILE CONTROLS tab, then choosing either JOYSTICK CONTROL or TETHER CONTROL.
questions.source_quote: tap the TACTILE CONTROLS tab. Then tap either the JOYSTICK CONTROL tab or TETHER CONTROL tab

questions.stem: What will happen if you press the ZERO POD CONTROLS button while a joystick or foot pedal is displaced from its mechanical neutral position?
options: The device will remain in standby mode until manually reset
The pod controls will be permanently disabled as a safety measure
The zero function will fail and display an error code on the screen
The device will suddenly activate when the joystick or pedal returns to neutral
questions.correct_answer: The device will suddenly activate when the joystick or pedal returns to neutral
questions.explanation: This is a critical safety concern—pressing ZERO POD CONTROLS with a displaced joystick or pedal does not wait for you to return it to neutral; it sets the zero point at that displaced position, causing immediate and potentially dangerous activation when the control returns to its natural neutral state.
questions.source_quote:
If a joystick or foot pedal is away from its mechanical neutral (zero) position when you
tap the ZERO POD CONTROLS button, the device will suddenly activate when the joystick or pedal
returns to its n

questions.stem: When an operator logs into the ROV control system, what happens to their tactile control settings?
options: The settings are stored on the ROV hardware itself and don't transfer between stations
The settings are system-wide and apply to all operators equally
The settings must be manually adjusted each time a new operator logs in
The settings follow the operator and are applied automatically from their user profile
questions.correct_answer: The settings follow the operator and are applied automatically from their user profile
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that tactile control settings are stored as part of each user profile, allowing settings to automatically load when that user logs in. This ensures consistent control feel across different sessions and workstations.
questions.source_quote: Some tactile control settings are part of each user profile

questions.stem: An ROV is observed drifting or producing control outputs even though the pilot's joystick is sitting at its mechanical neutral position. What is the most likely cause of this behavior?
options: Excessive electromagnetic interference from nearby ROV systems
Improper tether management causing signal cable damage
A depleted battery in the portable control console
Mechanical wear or drift in the flight joystick or foot pedal
questions.correct_answer: Mechanical wear or drift in the flight joystick or foot pedal
questions.explanation: Mechanical wear or drift in a flight joystick or foot pedal can cause the device to produce control output even when it is at its mechanical neutral position, resulting in unexpected ROV movement. This is a physical degradation issue, not an electronic or power-related problem.
questions.source_quote: Mechanical wear or drift in a flight joystick or foot pedal can cause the device to unexpectedly operate even though it is at its mechanical neutral position

questions.stem: What is the first step when setting control device neutral position?
options: Display any CONFIGURATION screen
Navigate to the DIAGNOSTICS menu
Select the CALIBRATION wizard
Access the TROUBLESHOOTING section
questions.correct_answer: Display any CONFIGURATION screen
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the first step in setting control device neutral position is to display any CONFIGURATION screen. This accesses the necessary interface for the calibration procedure.
questions.source_quote: Display any CONFIGURATION screen

questions.stem: How do you configure tactile control settings separately for the left and right control stations on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Press the function buttons on the controller itself
Use the physical knobs on the main console panel
Select the appropriate client computer on the screen
Access the system settings from the toolbar menu
questions.correct_answer: Select the appropriate client computer on the screen
questions.explanation: The tactile control settings are configured by selecting the appropriate client computer on the screen — tap LEFT CLIENT COMPUTER or RIGHT CLIENT COMPUTER to configure that specific station's tactile controls.
questions.source_quote: tap RIGHT CLIENT COMPUTER to configure the tactile controls for the right control station, or LEFT CLIENT COMPUTER for the left control station

questions.stem: What does zeroing the flight joysticks and foot pedals correct?
options: Loss of hydraulic pressure in the manipulator arm
Navigation errors from GPS satellite signal interference
Complete system failure due to power loss
Unexpected device operation caused by mechanical wear or drift
questions.correct_answer: Unexpected device operation caused by mechanical wear or drift
questions.explanation: Zeroing flight joysticks and foot pedals realigns sensor readings to compensate for mechanical wear or drift, which restores normal device operation. The other options describe more serious failures that require different troubleshooting procedures.
questions.source_quote: you can "zero" flight joysticks and foot pedals

questions.stem: What does the flight joystick configuration screen allow you to do?
options: Program the tether management system parameters
Configure the emergency recovery sequence
Adjust the ROV's maximum operating depth settings
Assign pan and tilt controls to a flight joystick
questions.correct_answer: Assign pan and tilt controls to a flight joystick
questions.explanation: The flight joystick configuration screen is specifically designed to let operators map pan and tilt camera functions to the physical joystick controls, which is a standard setup task when configuring an ROV for operation.
questions.source_quote: Assign pan and tilt controls to a flight joystick

questions.stem: What function does the flight joystick configuration screen provide in the Schilling HD ROV control system?
options: Adjusting camera brightness and contrast
Scaling ROV low-speed joystick controls
Configuring thruster calibration settings
Setting ROV depth and pressure limits
questions.correct_answer: Scaling ROV low-speed joystick controls
questions.explanation: The flight joystick configuration screen is specifically designed to allow operators to scale the ROV's low-speed joystick controls, which adjusts how the ROV responds to fine joystick movements during precise maneuvering operations.
questions.source_quote: Scale ROV low-speed joystick controls

questions.stem: Where do you adjust the joystick deadband on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Video overlay settings
Thruster diagnostics menu
Flight joystick configuration screen
Main system status display
questions.correct_answer: Flight joystick configuration screen
questions.explanation: The flight joystick configuration screen is specifically designed for joystick adjustments, including the deadband setting which controls the neutral zone around center position.
questions.source_quote: Adjust the joystick deadband

questions.stem: What does the flight joystick configuration screen allow you to set for the vertical flight control switch?
options: The direction of ROV vertical flight
The camera tilt angle
The sensitivity of the main thrusters
The emergency ascent speed
questions.correct_answer: The direction of ROV vertical flight
questions.explanation: The flight joystick configuration screen specifically provides the option to set the direction of ROV vertical flight for the vertical flight control switch, allowing pilots to customize how the switch responds to inputs.
questions.source_quote: Set the direction of ROV flight for the vertical flight control switch

questions.stem: What does 'zeroing the joystick' mean on an ROV control system?
options: Locking the joystick in place to prevent accidental movement
Setting the neutral position—the position at which the joystick is at rest
Calibrating the maximum throw distance of the joystick
Resetting the joystick buttons to factory defaults
questions.correct_answer: Setting the neutral position—the position at which the joystick is at rest
questions.explanation: Zeroing the joystick establishes the neutral reference point, which is the rest position where the ROV maintains no forward, backward, or rotational movement.
questions.source_quote: Zero the joystick (set the neutral position—the position at which the joystick is at rest)

questions.stem: To access flight control parameters on the Schilling HD ROV control interface, what should you select?
options: THRUSTER CONTROL from the right-side panel
DIAGNOSTICS from the toolbar at the top
SYSTEM SETTINGS from the main menu bar
FLIGHT CONTROL from the left-side list
questions.correct_answer: FLIGHT CONTROL from the left-side list
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that FLIGHT CONTROL is selected from the left-side list on the screen to access flight control parameters.
questions.source_quote: On the list on the left side of the screen, tap FLIGHT CONTROL

questions.stem: When operating the ROV, how should displacement speed be adjusted relative to distance?
options: Use lower speeds for small distances and higher speeds for larger distances
Use higher speeds for small distances and lower speeds for larger distances
Speed should remain constant regardless of the distance traveled
Always use maximum speed regardless of the distance
questions.correct_answer: Use lower speeds for small distances and higher speeds for larger distances
questions.explanation: Matching speed to distance helps maintain proper vehicle control and positioning accuracy—slower speeds give better control for fine adjustments over short distances, while higher speeds are efficient for longer transits.
questions.source_quote: you should use lower speeds for small displacement distances and greater speeds for larger displacements

questions.stem: What does the MRU (Motion Reference Unit) yaw rate sensor measure on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: The pitch angle relative to horizontal
The depth of the ROV below the surface
The rate of rotation around the vertical axis (yaw)
The rate of forward movement through the water
questions.correct_answer: The rate of rotation around the vertical axis (yaw)
questions.explanation: A yaw rate sensor specifically measures how quickly the ROV rotates around its vertical axis, which is essential for tracking heading changes and maintaining orientation during operations.
questions.source_quote: ce unit (MRU) yaw rate sensor.

questions.stem: What does the HEADING OFFSET function do on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Resets the compass to factory calibration settings
Automatically corrects for geographic declination in real-time
Adds a configurable offset to the standard magnetic compass heading
Switches between magnetic and gyrocompass navigation modes
questions.correct_answer: Adds a configurable offset to the standard magnetic compass heading
questions.explanation: HEADING OFFSET is a configurable setting that allows pilots to add an offset to the magnetic compass heading, typically to compensate for known installation angles or local magnetic variations.
questions.source_quote: The HEADING OFFSET panel lets you add an offset to the standard magnetic compass heading

questions.stem: What are the two main purposes of the heading offset feature on an ROV?
options: Compensating for known magnetic irregularities and coordinating headings with another vehicle's compass
Calibrating depth sensors and compensating for water pressure changes
Increasing thrust power and improving ROV maneuverability
Adjusting camera zoom levels and improving video quality in low visibility
questions.correct_answer: Compensating for known magnetic irregularities and coordinating headings with another vehicle's compass
questions.explanation: The heading offset feature allows operators to account for local magnetic disturbances that affect compass accuracy, and it also enables matching or coordinating compass headings between multiple ROVs working together.
questions.source_quote: You can use this feature to compensate for a known magnetic irregularity or to coordinate or match headings with a compass on another vehicle

questions.stem: What happens to the compass displays when you change the heading offset on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Both graphic and digital displays automatically update to show the new heading
A maintenance technician must recalibrate the displays after changing the offset
Only the digital display updates automatically; the graphic display needs manual adjustment
You need to restart the system before the displays show the new offset
questions.correct_answer: Both graphic and digital displays automatically update to show the new heading
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV control system automatically applies heading offset changes to all compass displays—both graphic and digital—without requiring any manual intervention or additional steps from the pilot.
questions.source_quote: Changes in the heading offset are automatically applied to: All compass graphic and digital displays of heading

questions.stem: What should you understand about the sensors on your ROV system when reading the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual?
options: All sensors described in the manual are guaranteed to be installed on every ROV system the manufacturer produces.
Sensor configurations are standardized across all ROV systems, with only minor variations between models.
The sensor information in the manual applies identically to all ROV systems without exception.
Sensor quantity and type vary between ROV systems, so not all sensors described in the manual may be present on your specific system.
questions.correct_answer: Sensor quantity and type vary between ROV systems, so not all sensors described in the manual may be present on your specific system.
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that sensor quantity and type are different for each ROV system, meaning operators should verify which sensors are actually installed on their specific system rather than assuming all described sensors are present.
questions.source_quote: Sensor quantity and type are different for each ROV system

questions.stem: When should an ROV operator use the SENSOR SELECTION panel?
options: Only when all flight sensors have failed
Whenever any sensor on the ROV needs to be checked
During routine TMS deployment procedures
When the ROV has multiple flight sensors of the same type
questions.correct_answer: When the ROV has multiple flight sensors of the same type
questions.explanation: The SENSOR SELECTION panel allows operators to choose which specific sensors the control system uses when there are duplicate sensors of the same type installed on the ROV. This ensures the correct sensor data is being used for flight operations.
questions.source_quote: you must use the SENSOR SELECTION panel to select the sensors

questions.stem: What does the MAX DISPLACEMENT SPEED control on a Schilling HD ROV affect?
options: The maximum thrust force available for all maneuvering modes
The percentage of maximum speed available for DISPLACE operations
The rate at which the ROV changes depth during dives
The percentage of thruster power allocated to camera systems
questions.correct_answer: The percentage of maximum speed available for DISPLACE operations
questions.explanation: The MAX DISPLACEMENT SPEED setting specifically controls what percentage of the ROV's maximum speed can be used during DISPLACE operations, allowing pilots to limit speed for precision maneuvering or safety.
questions.source_quote: In the MAX DISPLACEMENT SPEED panel, tap buttons to increase or decrease the percentage of maximum speed that can be used for DISPLACE operations

questions.stem: During Mid-Water StationKeeping with a functioning inertial navigation instrument, what should you select as the flight sensor?
options: Inertial navigation instrument for heading, altitude, and forward/aft/lateral
Sonar sensor for depth and altitude readings
Inertial navigation instrument for heading only
Depth sensor as the primary flight sensor
questions.correct_answer: Inertial navigation instrument for heading, altitude, and forward/aft/lateral
questions.explanation: The inertial navigation instrument provides accurate positioning data for all flight parameters (heading, altitude, and motion in the forward/aft/lateral axes), making it the appropriate flight sensor for Mid-Water StationKeeping.
questions.source_quote: select it as the flight sensor for heading, altitude, and forward/aft/lat

questions.stem: Can flight control sensors be added to an ROV system after the vehicle leaves the factory?
options: Yes, they can be field-installed by a certified technician
Yes, the pilot can install them during pre-dive preparations
No, they are pre-integrated at the factory and cannot be installed afterward
Yes, but only during annual scheduled maintenance at the manufacturer
questions.correct_answer: No, they are pre-integrated at the factory and cannot be installed afterward
questions.explanation: Flight control sensors are integrated with the control software during manufacturing and this integration cannot be performed afterward. Pilots must understand this limitation when planning operations.
questions.source_quote: They cannot be added to the ROV system later

questions.stem: Before you can change the tether length that must remain on the drum, what must you do first?
options: Vent all hydraulic pressure from the TMS
Disengage the drum clutch
Shut down the control system
Release the tether tension brake
questions.correct_answer: Shut down the control system
questions.explanation: The control system must be shut down before making any adjustments to the tether length on the drum. This is a safety precondition to prevent unintended drum rotation or control commands during the adjustment.
questions.source_quote: Manually spool tether off the drum while the control system is shut down

questions.stem: What happens when you manually spool tether off the drum with the control system shut down?
options: An alarm sounds alerting you to change tether settings
Nothing unusual occurs since manual spooling is normal procedure
The drum automatically rewinds the tether back into position
The control system automatically overrides manual spooling
questions.correct_answer: An alarm sounds alerting you to change tether settings
questions.explanation: When you manually spool tether off the drum with the control system shut down, the system generates an alarm to alert you that tether settings need to be changed. This is a safety feature to ensure proper tether configuration is maintained.
questions.source_quote: Manually spool tether off the drum while the control system is shut down (generates an alarm alerting you to change tether settings)

questions.stem: When the inertial navigation instrument fails during mid-water station keeping, what system should you use for altitude and lateral flight control?
options: Doppler Velocity Log (DVL)
Backup inertial navigation system
Acoustic positioning system
Manual depth and heading hold
questions.correct_answer: Doppler Velocity Log (DVL)
questions.explanation: The DVL is the designated backup for altitude and lateral flight control when the inertial navigation instrument is unavailable. It tracks the ROV's velocity relative to the seabed, allowing precise station keeping even with degraded primary navigation.
questions.source_quote: use the DVL for altitude and lateral

questions.stem: What should you be aware of when adjusting flight sensor panel selections on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: Flight sensor panel selections have dependencies and may affect other system functions
Flight sensor panel selections operate completely independently with no cross-system effects
Only one flight sensor can be active at any given time
Flight sensor settings can only be changed during maintenance mode
questions.correct_answer: Flight sensor panel selections have dependencies and may affect other system functions
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV has interdependent flight sensor panel selections that can impact other system functions, so operators must understand these relationships before making changes.
questions.source_quote: some dependencies between flight sensor panel selections

questions.stem: When you select a Doppler velocity log (DVL) for the ALTITUDE parameter in the FLIGHT CONTROL configuration, what automatically happens in the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel?
options: It is automatically selected in the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel as well
It must be manually selected in the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel
It requires you to restart the configuration software
It automatically disables the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel
questions.correct_answer: It is automatically selected in the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel as well
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the DVL or inertial navigation instrument selected for ALTITUDE is automatically propagated to the FORWARD/AFT/LAT panel. This creates a dependency where the same sensor is used across both panels to ensure consistent navigation data.
questions.source_quote: Configuring General System Parameters FLIGHT Para- meter Dependency ALTITUDE The Doppler velocity log (DVL) or inertial navigation instrument selected in this panel is also auto- matically selected in

questions.stem: What should you do before modifying TMS tether settings?
options: Disable TMS operation using the FLIGHT screen TMS CONTROL panel
Contact the supervisor for permission before modifying TMS settings
Disable TMS operation using the TETHER screen TMS CONTROL panel
Power down the entire ROV system before making any changes
questions.correct_answer: Disable TMS operation using the FLIGHT screen TMS CONTROL panel
questions.explanation: The manual is clear that you must use the FLIGHT screen TMS CONTROL panel to disable TMS operation before changing any tether settings. This prevents accidental TMS movement during configuration.
questions.source_quote: Before you change values for TMS tether settings, use the FLIGHT screen TMS CONTROL panel to disable TMS operation

questions.stem: When TMS operation is disabled, which TMS components lose power?
options: ROV thrusters and navigation systems
All TMS cameras and lighting systems
Drum motor, pinch wheel motor, and latches
TMS frame hydraulics and structural locks
questions.correct_answer: Drum motor, pinch wheel motor, and latches
questions.explanation: Disabling TMS operation cuts electrical power to the drum motor, pinch wheel motor, and latches. These are the primary TMS components that depend on electrical power for operation.
questions.source_quote: This action disables power to the drum motor, pinch wheel motor, and latches

questions.stem: What permission level must your user profile include to operate the control station?
options: Administrator permissions
Guest permissions
Supervisor permissions
Operator permissions
questions.correct_answer: Supervisor permissions
questions.explanation: The control station requires supervisor-level access to function properly. Without supervisor permissions enabled in your user profile, you won't have the necessary authority to control ROV operations.
questions.source_quote: Ensure that the user profile for your control station includes supervisor permissions.

questions.stem: When slippage is detected on an electric TMS unit with diamond screw slip detection enabled, what does the control system do?
options: Automatically switches to manual control mode
Reduces TMS thrust output by 50%
Disables TMS operation and generates a fault condition
Issues a warning but continues normal TMS operation
questions.correct_answer: Disables TMS operation and generates a fault condition
questions.explanation: For electric TMS units with diamond screw slip detection enabled, the control system disables TMS operation and generates a fault condition when slippage is detected. This is a safety response that prevents further operation until the fault is cleared.
questions.source_quote: For an electric TMS only, if you have enabled diamond screw slip detection, the control system disables TMS operation when slippage is detected. A fault condition is generated

questions.stem: When slippage is detected in the TMS, what happens to the TMS system?
options: The system stops the TMS motors but does not disable the TMS and does not generate a fault
The system disables the TMS and generates a fault condition
The system immediately shuts down all ROV operations
The system continues normal TMS operations without any changes
questions.correct_answer: The system stops the TMS motors but does not disable the TMS and does not generate a fault
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, when slippage is detected the TMS stops the motors as a protective response, but the TMS remains enabled and no fault condition is generated. This allows the operator to address the situation without a system shutdown.
questions.source_quote: The TMS is not disabled, and no fault condition is generated

questions.stem: When slip detection occurs on a TMS, what is the difference between standard TMS and electric TMS with diamond screw slip detection enabled?
options: Both standard TMS and electric TMS with diamond screw stop the motor only
Standard TMS triggers a fault condition; electric TMS with diamond screw stops the motor only
Both standard TMS and electric TMS with diamond screw trigger a fault condition
Standard TMS stops the motor only; electric TMS with diamond screw triggers a fault condition
questions.correct_answer: Standard TMS stops the motor only; electric TMS with diamond screw triggers a fault condition
questions.explanation: Standard TMS slip detection simply halts motor movement without generating a fault, while the electric TMS with diamond screw slip detection enabled takes it a step further by triggering an actual fault condition when slip is detected.
questions.source_quote: A fault condition is generated

questions.stem: How do you adjust the TMS low-altitude threshold setting on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Accessing the thruster calibration wizard in the main menu
Using the UP and DOWN buttons on the GENERAL configuration screen
Pressing and holding the MODE button for 5 seconds
Rotating the TURN knob on the TMS diagnostics menu
questions.correct_answer: Using the UP and DOWN buttons on the GENERAL configuration screen
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV uses UP and DOWN buttons on the GENERAL configuration screen to increase or decrease the TMS low-altitude threshold setting. This is a direct control interface for this parameter.
questions.source_quote: tap the UP or DOWN button to increase or decrease the altitude

questions.stem: When entering tether drum parameters into the TMS configuration screen, what is the correct numbering scheme for layers and wraps?
options: Layers can start at 0 with wraps entered as whole numbers only
Layers should always be entered before wraps, regardless of the values
Layers as whole numbers starting with 1, and wraps as fractions or whole numbers starting with 0
Both layers and wraps must be entered as whole numbers starting with 1
questions.correct_answer: Layers as whole numbers starting with 1, and wraps as fractions or whole numbers starting with 0
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that layers always use whole numbers starting at 1 (there is no layer zero), while wraps can be fractional values starting at zero. A partial layer still counts as a full layer, but the wraps on that partial layer can be entered as a decimal fraction.
questions.source_quote: Configuring General System Parameters 11.8.2.1 Counting Layers and Wraps If you are entering values in layers and wraps, be careful to use the correct numbering scheme. ●Layers are always in whole num

questions.stem: What does it indicate when the TMS altitude indicator on the FLIGHT screen turns orange?
options: The TMS altitude sensor has malfunctioned
The TMS is at a safe operating altitude
The TMS is getting too close to the seafloor
The TMS is ascending too rapidly
questions.correct_answer: The TMS is getting too close to the seafloor
questions.explanation: The orange color serves as a visual warning that the TMS altitude has dropped below the safe threshold and is approaching the seafloor. The pilot should take corrective action to increase altitude immediately.
questions.source_quote: the TMS altitude indicator on the FLIGHT screen turns orange

questions.stem: What permission level is required to change TMS low-altitude threshold settings on the control station?
options: Operator permissions
Supervisor permissions
Basic user permissions
Administrator permissions
questions.correct_answer: Supervisor permissions
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that only user profiles with supervisor permissions can modify TMS low-altitude threshold settings, ensuring that critical system parameters are only changed by authorized personnel.
questions.source_quote: Ensure that the user profile for your control station includes supervisor permissions

questions.stem: What does the TMS control system always do regarding tether rendering?
options: Allows the tether to render freely without resistance
Only applies force when manually commanded by the pilot
Applies motor force to resist tether rendering
Reverses motor direction to assist tether rendering
questions.correct_answer: Applies motor force to resist tether rendering
questions.explanation: The TMS control system is designed to always maintain tension on the tether by applying motor force that resists rendering, which prevents slack tether and potential entanglement issues.
questions.source_quote: The control system always applies some force from TMS motors to resist rendering

questions.stem: What is 'rendering' in ROV tether operations?
options: When the tether is pulled off the drum by external forces like ROV movement or water currents, not by the TMS motors
When the tether becomes wrapped around the ROV during maneuvering
When the TMS motors actively pull the tether off the drum during normal deployment
When the tether slips on the drum due to insufficient braking pressure
questions.correct_answer: When the tether is pulled off the drum by external forces like ROV movement or water currents, not by the TMS motors
questions.explanation: Rendering specifically refers to passive tether payout caused by external forces (ROV movement or currents) acting on the tether, rather than the TMS motors actively pulling it off the drum. Understanding this distinction is important for proper tether tension management during operations.
questions.source_quote: Rendering occurs when tether is being pulled off the drum by some force other than the TMS motors

questions.stem: The tether render force adjustment procedure is limited to which type of TMS motor?
options: Hydraulic motors
DC motors
VFD motors
AC induction motors
questions.correct_answer: DC motors
questions.explanation: The tether render force adjustment procedure is specifically designed for TMS units equipped with DC motors and does not apply to VFD motor systems.
questions.source_quote: applies only to TMS units that use DC motors rather than VFD motors

questions.stem: What should you do after entering a device label to store the video device configuration?
options: Tap RESET
Tap BACK
Tap CANCEL
Tap SAVE
questions.correct_answer: Tap SAVE
questions.explanation: Tapping SAVE stores the video device configuration after entering the device label, as specified in the Schilling HD ROV manual's procedure for configuring video devices.
questions.source_quote: Tap SAVE

questions.stem: What is the first step in installing a TAVS video device?
options: Configuring the video switcher settings
Powering on the TAVS video device
Connecting the video output to a monitor
Connecting the input device's cable to the video switcher input port
questions.correct_answer: Connecting the input device's cable to the video switcher input port
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that connecting the input device's cable to the video switcher input port is the first step in the TAVS video device installation procedure.
questions.source_quote: Connect the input device's cable to the desired video switcher input port

questions.stem: What should you do when you are finished making changes in the VIDEO DEVICE CONFIGURATION panel?
options: Tap CANCEL
Tap SAVE
Tap CLOSE
Tap RESET
questions.correct_answer: Tap CLOSE
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, after configuring video devices, you should tap CLOSE to exit the panel.
questions.source_quote: In the VIDEO DEVICE CONFIGURATION panel, tap CLOSE.

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of activating PORT TEST MODE on the ROV system?
options: To boost power to a device that is not responding during normal operation
To transfer device control from the TMS to the ROV control system
To create a temporary test tunnel that bypasses the RTC for device diagnostics
To permanently disconnect a malfunctioning device from the system
questions.correct_answer: To create a temporary test tunnel that bypasses the RTC for device diagnostics
questions.explanation: PORT TEST MODE disengages the device from communicating with the real-time computer (RTC) and directs its signals to a separate test computer, allowing technicians to run diagnostic software without interference from normal system operations.
questions.source_quote: Port Test Mode Use the PORT TEST MODE button in the POWER AND COMMUNICATION panel to create a temporary tunnel for testing a device attached to the ROV system. Typically, devices with a system interfa

questions.stem: When an acknowledged HPU motor temperature issue resolves and the temperature returns to normal, what happens to the issue in the system?
options: The operator must manually clear the issue from all logs
It remains in the log but is marked as 'resolved'
It is automatically archived but still appears in the Active Issues list
It automatically clears from the Active Issues log and status displays
questions.correct_answer: It automatically clears from the Active Issues log and status displays
questions.explanation: According to the manual, once an issue has been acknowledged and the underlying condition (HPU motor temperature) returns to normal, the system automatically clears the issue from the Active Issues log and all status displays. The operator does not need to take any additional action.
questions.source_quote: Issues and Events 3.6 Example of Issue/Event Sequence If the HPU motor temperature rises above the normal range during vigorous operation, the following sequence of actions might occur: HPU motor temp

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, where should you look to find information about Port Test Mode?
options: In the troubleshooting appendix
On the next page
At the beginning of the manual
In a separate technical bulletin
questions.correct_answer: On the next page
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly directs you to the next page when you need information about Port Test Mode. This is a navigation reference to help locate the relevant procedure.
questions.source_quote: Port Test Mode next page.

questions.stem: What does the color of an indicator light displayed next to a platform or subsystem represent?
options: The color of the most serious issue for that platform or subsystem
The current operational status of the entire ROV system
The first issue that was detected for that platform or subsystem
A warning level assigned by the pilot before the dive
questions.correct_answer: The color of the most serious issue for that platform or subsystem
questions.explanation: The indicator light automatically displays the color representing the most critical issue affecting that specific platform or subsystem, so you can quickly identify what needs the most urgent attention.
questions.source_quote:
(a situation likely needs attention)
●Red - Alarm (a situation needs urgent attention)
The indicator light displayed next to a platform or subsystem shows the color of the most serious issue for
that

questions.stem: In the POWER AND COMMUNICATION diagnostics panel, what does the OUT linear gauge indicate?
options: Traffic flowing out of the Ethernet switch and into the port
Traffic flowing out of the port's interface module and into the Ethernet switch
Bidirectional traffic flowing in both directions simultaneously
Total bandwidth utilization measured in megabits per second
questions.correct_answer: Traffic flowing out of the Ethernet switch and into the port
questions.explanation: The OUT gauge represents traffic flowing FROM the Ethernet switch TO the port. Remember: IN and OUT are relative to the node switch, not the port's interface module. The gauge also displays percentage of full scale, not megabits per second.
questions.source_quote: Port POWER AND COMMUNICATION Diagnostics Panel 5.3 Port Ethernet Traffic In the POWER AND COMMUNICATION panel for an individual device, the IN linear gauge indicates Eth- ernet traffic flowing out of

questions.stem: What is the first troubleshooting step when one or more CPU reset status indicators show red on the ROV diagnostics display?
options: Immediately check and reseat the power connections to the affected thrusters
Long press the ROV image on the flight device screen and select ENABLE ALL THRUSTERS from the menu
Power down and restart the entire ROV system from the TMS
Replace the CPU module in the thruster showing the red indicator
questions.correct_answer: Long press the ROV image on the flight device screen and select ENABLE ALL THRUSTERS from the menu
questions.explanation: According to the manual, when CPU reset status indicators show red (indicating voltage dropped below threshold), the first step is to long press the ROV image and select ENABLE ALL THRUSTERS. Only if the indicators remain red after this should you check power connections.
questions.source_quote: ROV Diagnostics Details CPU Reset The CPU reset status indicator for a thruster shows the status of the CPU's power supply. If the CPU reset status indicators for one or more thrusters are red, this i

questions.stem: When configuring a rack node SIM port in the Schilling HD ROV system, what is the correct procedure?
options: Manually type the port number into a text input field
Click the 'Auto-Detect' button to find available ports automatically
Choose a port profile from a list of pre-configured settings
Select a port from the drop-down list of available rack node SIM ports
