questions.source_quote: Install the cover, making sure the O-ring is installed correctly.

questions.stem: When preparing a NEXANS tether, what length should you cut the three grounds to?
questions.explanation: The procedure explicitly states to cut the three grounds to 12.7 cm (5 in) and strip the ends before twisting them together and landing them into a single ring lug. Note that 38 mm (1.5 in) is the length used for TYCO tether grounds, which is a common distractor.
questions.source_quote: If you have a NEXANS tether: Cut the three grounds to 12.7 cm (5 in) and strip the ends off of them. Twist these grounds together and land them into a single ring lug. Then, skip to step 15. If you ha

questions.stem: When preparing conductors for connection on the ROV or TMS, what must you do regarding their length?
options: Cut them to the exact lengths specified in the dimensional image
Cut them to standard factory lengths regardless of the image
Cut them longer than specified and trim during installation
Leave them at their current length and focus on preparing the grounds/drains
questions.correct_answer: Cut them to the exact lengths specified in the dimensional image
questions.explanation: The procedure requires cutting conductors to precise lengths as shown in the dimensional image to ensure proper fit and function. The grounds/drains are prepared separately in subsequent steps.
questions.source_quote: Cut the conductors to the correct lengths according to the dimensions shown in the image above. The grounds/drains will be prepared in a few steps.

questions.stem: What must you do before working on the ROV tether?
options: Ensure the ROV is surfaced and powered down
Lock out and tag out all electrical power to the tether
Turn off only the TMS surface power supply
Disconnect the tether from the TMS frame
questions.correct_answer: Lock out and tag out all electrical power to the tether
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that all electrical power to the tether must be locked out and tagged out before any work begins. This prevents accidental energization and protects personnel from electrical hazards.
questions.source_quote: Do not work on the tether until all electrical power to it is locked out and tagged out.

questions.stem: When separating conductors during Quick Disconnect tether termination, into how many groups should they be organized?
options: Four groups
Three groups
Two groups
Five groups
questions.correct_answer: Three groups
questions.explanation: The procedure requires separating conductors into three distinct groups: HPU wires (10 AWG), IP wires (18 AWG for NEXANS or 20 AWG for TYCO), and Ground (drain) wires. Organizing them this way makes the termination work more manageable.
questions.source_quote: Quick Disconnect, Tether: Terminate 9. Separate the conductors into 3 groups so that they are easier to work with: — HPU wires (10 AWG) — IP wires (18 AWG for NEXANS, 20 AWG for TYCO) — Ground (drain)

questions.stem: When connecting fibers to a QD socket on an ROV, what are the correct steps?
options: Insert the fibers into the QD socket without winding them, then reference the chart afterward
Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the highest numbered QD socket available
Plug the fibers directly into any available QD socket without winding them first
Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID# according to the chart
questions.correct_answer: Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID# according to the chart
questions.explanation: The procedure requires you to wind the fibers around the QD socket first, then plug them into the specific socket ID number designated by the chart. This ensures proper fiber management and correct connections for ROV operation.
questions.source_quote: Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID# according to the chart provided above.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, what is the correct procedure for stripping conductor ends?
options: Strip to a standard fixed length of 1 inch regardless of wire size
Strip the ends of the conductors to the dimensions shown in the manual
Strip all the insulation off the conductors as completely as possible
Strip only the outer cable jacket and leave individual conductors unstripped
questions.correct_answer: Strip the ends of the conductors to the dimensions shown in the manual
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that conductors must be stripped to the exact dimensions indicated in the accompanying diagram, ensuring proper connection without risking damage from over-stripping or poor contact from under-stripping.
questions.source_quote: Strip the ends of the conductors to the correct lengths according to the dimensions shown in the image above.

questions.stem: When a technical drawing in the manual states 'This drawing is not to scale,' what does it mean?
options: The drawing contains errors and should be discarded
The drawing shows the exact dimensions of all components
The proportions in the drawing may not accurately represent the actual size relationships between components
The drawing was made without following proper technical standards
questions.correct_answer: The proportions in the drawing may not accurately represent the actual size relationships between components
questions.explanation: A 'not to scale' notice indicates that the drawing is for illustrative purposes only and does not show accurate proportional relationships between components.
questions.source_quote: This drawing is not to scale.

questions.stem: When installing the ground wire during ROV preparation, what type of connector should be attached to the end of the wire?
options: Fork terminal
Spade lug
Ring lug
Banana plug
questions.correct_answer: Ring lug
questions.explanation: A ring lug provides a secure, permanent connection that won't accidentally disconnect, ensuring reliable grounding continuity throughout the operation.
questions.source_quote: Install a ring lug onto the end of this ground wire.

questions.stem: When installing the largest Oetiker clamp from the kit, where should it be placed?
options: Underneath the drain wires, at the midpoint of the cable
Over the main power conductors, at the edge of the insulation
In the middle of the insulation covering the wires
Over the drain wires, at the edge of the insulation
questions.correct_answer: Over the drain wires, at the edge of the insulation
questions.explanation: The Oetiker clamp secures drain wires specifically and must be positioned at the insulation edge for proper strain relief and electrical continuity.
questions.source_quote: Install the kit’s largest Oetiker clamp over the drain wires, at the edge of the insulation.

questions.stem: What should you do with the two long drain wires during ROV preparation?
options: Connect each one to a separate grounding terminal
Wrap them individually with electrical tape
Cut them off and discard them
Twist them together to create a thick ground
questions.correct_answer: Twist them together to create a thick ground
questions.explanation: The procedure requires twisting the two long drain wires together to create a thick ground, which ensures proper grounding for safe ROV operation.
questions.source_quote: Twist the two long drain wires together to create a thick ground (see the image above).

questions.stem: When installing the fiber whip into the housing, what is the correct orientation?
options: Face up into the socket
Face down into the socket
Sideways into the socket
Face down but outside the socket
questions.correct_answer: Face down into the socket
questions.explanation: The fiber whip must be oriented face down when snapping it into the socket to ensure proper connection and alignment as shown in the manual's diagrams.
questions.source_quote: Snap the fiber whip into the housing and orient it face down into the socket, as shown in Figure 25 on the previous page Figure 27

questions.stem: During cable or connector preparation on an ROV, when should you pull the drain wires back over the insulation?
options: During initial inspection before making any cuts
After the heat shrink has already been applied
Before stripping any insulation from the conductors
After completing wire connections but before applying heat shrink
questions.correct_answer: After completing wire connections but before applying heat shrink
questions.explanation: Drain wires are pulled back over the insulation after all electrical connections are finished as a preparatory step, ensuring the shielding is properly positioned before the final protective heat shrink covering is applied.
questions.source_quote: Pull the drain wires back over the insulation.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, what does Figure 19 typically reference?
options: A detailed diagram or illustration of ROV components or systems
A maintenance procedures checklist
A safety warning summary
A troubleshooting flowchart
questions.correct_answer: A detailed diagram or illustration of ROV components or systems
questions.explanation: In technical operator manuals like the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, figures are primarily used to provide detailed visual references of ROV components, systems, or operational diagrams. This is standard practice for illustrating equipment layouts and system configurations.

Figure 19
18.

questions.stem: When should the tether quick disconnect be terminated?
options: When the TMS winch is running
Before every dive regardless of conditions
Only during routine maintenance
At the end of deployment or in an emergency situation
questions.correct_answer: At the end of deployment or in an emergency situation
questions.explanation: The quick disconnect is terminated (closed/secured) at the conclusion of ROV operations to safely secure the tether connection, or immediately during an emergency requiring rapid tether release. It is not a routine pre-dive step, nor is it related to winch operation timing.
questions.source_quote: Quick Disconnect, Tether: Terminate

questions.stem: When securing the housing during ROV/TMS operation, what method should be used?
options: Cable tie the housing in place
Secure the housing with mechanical bolts
Use wire rope to lash the housing
Apply adhesive sealant to the housing
questions.correct_answer: Cable tie the housing in place
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states to cable tie the housing in place as shown in Figure 25, which is the standard procedure for securing this component.
questions.source_quote: Cable tie the housing in place, as shown in Figure 25 on the previous page

questions.stem: When managing fiber optics on the ROV, what should you do with any unused fibers?
options: Cut them off to reduce weight and clutter
Coil them loosely in the TMS basket
Route them along the main tether cable
Tuck them under the cable tie
questions.correct_answer: Tuck them under the cable tie
questions.explanation: Unused fibers should be tucked under the cable tie to keep them secure and prevent them from getting caught or damaged during operations.
questions.source_quote: Tuck the unused fibers under the cable tie.

questions.stem: When maintaining fiber optic cables on an ROV, what should be done with the excess parts of unused fibers protruding from their furcation tubing?
options: Trim them to serve as spares
Leave them protruding to prevent connector damage
Coil them and store them inside the TMS cage
Completely remove and discard them
questions.correct_answer: Trim them to serve as spares
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that excess fiber protruding from furcation tubing should be trimmed and kept as spares, not discarded or left unsecured. These trimmed fibers serve as backup lengths in case repairs are needed later.
questions.source_quote: Trim the excess parts of the unused fibers that are protruding out of their furcation tubing. They will serve as spares.

questions.stem: When terminating fibers F1, F2, and F3, what type of connectors should be used with the Corning UniCam Pretium termination kit?
options: FC connectors from the spare parts locker
ST connectors supplied with the ROV system
SC connectors available in the tool bag
LC connectors from the fiber kit
questions.correct_answer: ST connectors supplied with the ROV system
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that ST connectors (supplied with your ROV system) must be used when terminating fibers F1, F2, and F3 with the Corning UniCam Pretium kit. Using a different connector type would not match the system specifications.
questions.source_quote: Terminate fibers F1, F2, and F3 using the Corning UniCam Pretium termination kit (instructions included) and the ST connectors supplied with your ROV system.

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure for installing fibers at the QD socket?
options: Plug the fibers directly into the QD socket without winding them first
Wind the fibers around the socket after plugging them in
Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID# according to the chart
Use any QD socket ID# regardless of what the chart indicates
questions.correct_answer: Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID# according to the chart
questions.explanation: The procedure explicitly requires winding fibers around the QD socket first, then plugging them into the specific QD socket ID# indicated on the provided chart. Skipping steps or ignoring the chart can lead to incorrect fiber routing and potential connection issues.
questions.source_quote: Wind the fibers around the QD socket and plug them into the QD socket ID#, according to the chart provided above.

questions.stem: When installing the ground conductors to the QD nut, what is the correct procedure regarding tension?
options: The ground conductors should be torqued to the maximum specification
The ground conductors should be pulled as tight as possible for maximum conductivity
The ground conductors should have some slack and not be pulled too tight
The ground conductors should be installed without the lock washer
questions.correct_answer: The ground conductors should have some slack and not be pulled too tight
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that ground conductors should have some slack and not be pulled too tight. This prevents strain on the connection and allows for proper grounding performance.
questions.source_quote: Land both ground ring lugs onto the QD nut with the provided screw and lock washer. The ground conductors should have some slack in them and not be pulled too tight (see Figure 26 on the previous page

questions.stem: What is the clamp on the T-box connection used to hold in place?
options: Navigation sensor cables
Hydraulic control lines
Emergency repair tools
Blank plugs or caps for sealing connections
questions.correct_answer: Blank plugs or caps for sealing connections
questions.explanation: The clamp on the T-box connection is specifically designed to hold blanking plugs or caps in place, which can be used to seal either type of connection to prevent contamination or water ingress when not in use.
questions.source_quote:
so it can
be used to blank either type of connection. The clamp on the T-box connection can be used to hold the

questions.stem: What parameters will trigger an IPDS alarm in the Schilling ROV?
options: Thruster alignment, winch tension, and camera focus
Video signal strength, data bandwidth, and communication latency
Hydraulic pressure, motor temperature, and voltage levels
Transformer core temperature, water detection, and impedance between earth and 48V conductors
questions.correct_answer: Transformer core temperature, water detection, and impedance between earth and 48V conductors
questions.explanation: According to the manual, IPDS alarms are triggered when upper or lower limits are exceeded for transformer core temperature, water detection in transformer/backbone/Multi Pak/DC Pak, and impedance between earth and the 48V conductors (and/or return) as detected by the IMD.
questions.source_quote: Instrument Power Distribution System (IPDS) Service 7.1.2 IPDS Alarms An alarm is produced when an upper or lower limit value is exceeded for the parameters listed below. ●Transformer core temperature

questions.stem: What hazard does the IPDS present according to the operator manual?
options: The hazard is limited to overheating, not electrical shock
It produces voltages that can cause injury or death
It has no electrical hazards since it's computer-controlled
It only poses a minor shock risk comparable to household batteries
questions.correct_answer: It produces voltages that can cause injury or death
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly warns that voltages used and produced by the IPDS can cause injury or death. This is a critical safety warning that all personnel must heed when working with or near the IPDS.
questions.source_quote: SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages used and produced by the IPDS can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: Before making an electrical connection on the ROV, what components should you inspect?
options: The connector interface and O-ring
The wire gauge and current rating
The mating connector's locking mechanism
The cable jacket and shielding
questions.correct_answer: The connector interface and O-ring
questions.explanation: The connector interface and O-ring must be inspected before every connection to ensure a proper seal and prevent electrical faults or leaks in the ROV system.

questions.stem: The 'AC POWER' status light can be used to verify what, but NOT to determine what specific voltage level?
options: It cannot tell you if the AC power circuit breaker is closed
It cannot tell you if the PDU is receiving any voltage
It cannot tell you whether the system is using 4160 VAC or 3000 VAC
It cannot tell you if AC power is being supplied
questions.correct_answer: It cannot tell you whether the system is using 4160 VAC or 3000 VAC
questions.explanation: The 'AC POWER' status light only indicates that AC power is present, but it does not differentiate between voltage levels. Operators must use proper voltage identification methods rather than relying on this status light to determine the specific VAC being used.
questions.source_quote: DO NOT use the “AC POWER” status light to determine whether 4160 VAC or 3000 VAC power

questions.stem: During an electrical system check on the ROV, what should you do with AC lights or other AC devices on the vehicle?
options: Leave them running continuously throughout all procedures
Keep them turned off to avoid interfering with other measurements
Remove them from the vehicle before performing any tests
Briefly turn them on and measure/record the readings
questions.correct_answer: Briefly turn them on and measure/record the readings
questions.explanation: The procedure requires briefly activating AC devices so you can measure and record their electrical characteristics. This allows you to verify proper operation and collect baseline data during the system check.
questions.source_quote: If there are any AC lights or other AC devices on the vehicle, briefly turn them on. Measure and record

questions.stem: What is the correct sequence of actions when performing Test 2 to measure AC voltage on the ROV?
options: Measure AC voltage with lights off, then turn on lights to record the reading
Turn on AC lights briefly, measure and record the AC voltage, turn off the lights, then turn off IPDS power
Record the voltage reading, turn on AC lights, and then turn off IPDS power
Turn off IPDS power first, then turn on AC lights to measure voltage
questions.correct_answer: Turn on AC lights briefly, measure and record the AC voltage, turn off the lights, then turn off IPDS power
questions.explanation: The procedure requires you to turn on AC lights briefly, measure and record the AC voltage as Test 2, immediately turn off the lights after measurement, and finally turn off IPDS power at the PDU.

options: 40 horsepower per thruster
40 volts electrical rating
40 pounds thrust capacity
40 meters maximum depth

Rated thrust of 30 horsepower
Rated cable length of 30 meters

questions.stem: In the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, what does the specification 'Rated at 40' most likely refer to?
options: Rated thrust capacity in kilowatts (kW)
Electrical current rating in amperes
Vehicle weight in kilograms
Maximum operating depth in meters
questions.correct_answer: Rated thrust capacity in kilowatts (kW)
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD is a work-class ROV, and 'Rated at 40' in this context refers to its thrust capacity rating of 40 kW, which indicates the maximum thrust output the propulsion system can generate for maneuvering the vehicle.

Skip any components that appear worn or damaged
Use all components supplied in assembly
Use components from previous assemblies if available

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of maintaining notes during ROV operations?
options: To communicate with the ROV pilot in real-time during operations
To replace the need for formal documentation and reporting
To record observations, procedures performed, and any anomalies encountered during the dive
To track equipment maintenance schedules only
questions.correct_answer: To record observations, procedures performed, and any anomalies encountered during the dive
questions.explanation: Notes during ROV operations serve as a real-time record of what is observed and performed during the dive, providing documentation that can be referenced later for reporting, troubleshooting, or compliance purposes.

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of adding lead weights (ballast) to an ROV during pre-dive preparation?
options: To improve the ROV's acoustic communication signal strength
To protect the ROV's electrical components from water pressure
To increase the ROV's maximum operational depth rating
To increase the ROV's negative buoyancy and help it descend through the water column
questions.correct_answer: To increase the ROV's negative buoyancy and help it descend through the water column
questions.explanation: Lead weights are added to ROVs to achieve negative buoyancy, which allows the vehicle to descend under its own weight without struggling against buoyancy. This ballast is essential for maintaining proper depth control and operational stability during underwater missions.
questions.source_quote:
LEAD
A.

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the tether on a Schilling HD ROV system?
options: To provide physical buoyancy control for the ROV
To anchor the ROV in strong currents
To transmit power, video signals, and control data between the ROV and the operator station
To store data collected during the dive
questions.correct_answer: To transmit power, video signals, and control data between the ROV and the operator station
questions.explanation: The tether is the critical link that allows the pilot to send commands to the ROV while receiving video feedback and telemetry data, enabling real-time operation from the surface. Without this connection, the ROV would be unable to function as a remotely operated system.
questions.source_quote: OTHERWISE INDICATED.

questions.stem: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes an ROV from an AUV (Autonomous Underwater Vehicle)?
options: An ROV can only operate in shallow water while AUVs work in deep water
An ROV is smaller than an AUV
An ROV is tethered and controlled by a human operator, while an AUV operates autonomously without a tether
An ROV uses solar power while an AUV uses battery power
questions.correct_answer: An ROV is tethered and controlled by a human operator, while an AUV operates autonomously without a tether
questions.explanation: ROVs maintain a physical tether connection to the surface vessel that provides power, communication, and real-time operator control. AUVs, by contrast, are pre-programmed to operate independently without a tether, making their own navigation decisions based on onboard sensors and programming.
questions.source_quote:
NOTES:
2.

questions.stem: What should the pilot verify when conducting a visual inspection of the ROV tether before deployment?

questions.correct_answer: Hold the flat of the rod with a small wrench
questions.explanation: The flat of the rod provides a secure bearing point for a wrench, anchoring the rod so it doesn't spin when the nut is turned. This prevents damage to the rod and ensures safe removal.
questions.source_quote: Hold the flat of the rod with a small wrench while removing the nut

questions.stem: What is the correct first step when performing maintenance on the vertical buoyancy rods of a Schilling HD ROV?
options: Disconnect the electrical wiring from the rod sensors
Apply lubricant to the rod mounting points
Replace the seals on the buoyancy rod connectors
Remove the nuts and washers from the bottom of the vertical buoyancy rods
questions.correct_answer: Remove the nuts and washers from the bottom of the vertical buoyancy rods
questions.explanation: Following the proper sequence, you must first remove the nuts and washers from the bottom of the vertical buoyancy rods before accessing or working on the rods. The other options describe tasks that come later in the maintenance process or are unrelated to the initial disassembly step.
questions.source_quote: Remove the nuts and washers from the bottom of the vertical buoyancy rods

questions.stem: What safety issue can occur when buoyancy blocks are placed on a vessel deck?
options: They can become unstable
They need no special handling
They must be fully submerged at all times
They will automatically sink
questions.correct_answer: They can become unstable
questions.explanation: Buoyancy blocks may shift or roll on a vessel deck due to ship motion, creating a stability hazard. Proper securing or stowage is required to prevent accidents.
questions.source_quote: Buoyancy blocks may be unstable when placed on the deck.

questions.stem: Why must buoyancy blocks be physically supported while on deck?
options: To maintain proper thermal regulation during operations
To keep them away from electromagnetic interference sources
To prevent them from absorbing water and becoming waterlogged
To prevent them from falling over and causing injury or damage
questions.correct_answer: To prevent them from falling over and causing injury or damage
questions.explanation: Buoyancy blocks are heavy and unstable when standing upright on deck. Without physical support, they can tip over and seriously injure personnel or damage equipment nearby.
questions.source_quote: Ensure buoyancy blocks are supported to prevent them from falling over.

questions.stem: What must you do before removing the horizontal buoyancy rods from the ROV?
options: Remove the nuts and washers from the front of the buoyancy rods
Pull the rods straight out without removing any hardware first
Remove the nuts and washers from the back of the rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts
Remove the rods first, then take off the nuts and washers afterward
questions.correct_answer: Remove the nuts and washers from the back of the rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts
questions.explanation: The procedure requires removing the nuts and washers from the back of the horizontal buoyancy rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts BEFORE taking the rods out. Skipping this step or doing it in the wrong order could damage the hardware or cause components to fall into the water.
questions.source_quote: Remove the nuts and washers from the back of the horizontal buoyancy rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts, and remove the rods from the buoyancy.

questions.stem: Before extracting the horizontal buoyancy rods, what must be removed first?
options: The buoyancy foam
The horizontal rod brackets
Nuts and washers
The forward vertical thruster mounts
questions.correct_answer: Nuts and washers
questions.explanation: The nuts and washers are located on the back of the horizontal buoyancy rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts and must be removed before the rods can be safely extracted from the buoyancy.
questions.source_quote: Remove the nuts and washers from the back of the horizontal buoyancy rods near the forward vertical thruster mounts, and remove the rods from the buoyancy.

questions.stem: What is the FIRST step in the buoyancy block removal procedure?
options: Remove the buoyancy side and aft guards
Remove the buoyancy forward guard
Loosen the mounting bolts
Disconnect the TMS cable connections
questions.correct_answer: Remove the buoyancy side and aft guards
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that removing the buoyancy side and aft guards is explicitly the first step. The forward guard and other components are removed later in the procedure.
questions.source_quote: Remove the buoyancy side and aft guards.

questions.stem: Before removing any buoyancy rods from the ROV, what must be confirmed?
options: The thrusters are disabled and the ROV is grounded
The ROV has been fully depressurized and disconnected from the TMS
The lifting device is supporting the full weight of the buoyancy block
The buoyancy rods are safely stored in a designated container
questions.correct_answer: The lifting device is supporting the full weight of the buoyancy block
questions.explanation: The lifting device must support the full weight of the buoyancy block before removing any buoyancy rods to prevent the block from dropping unexpectedly, which could cause equipment damage or personal injury.
questions.source_quote: ensure the lifting device is supporting the weight of the buoyancy block

questions.stem: When lifting the ROV, where must the buoyancy lifting pad eyes be positioned in relation to the crane?
options: Positioned behind the crane
Lined up vertically with the crane
Angled forward of the crane
Offset slightly to one side for balance
questions.correct_answer: Lined up vertically with the crane
questions.explanation: The buoyancy lifting pad eyes must align vertically with the crane to ensure the lifting force is applied directly through the ROV's center of gravity, preventing dangerous swinging or tilting during hoisting operations.
questions.source_quote: Position the ROV directly under the lifting equipment so that the buoyancy lifting pad eyes line up vertically with the crane.

questions.stem: When should the port and starboard vertical thruster nozzles be removed?
options: When the TMS winch system malfunctions
When they obstruct access to the buoyancy block
At the start of every dive regardless of conditions
When the ROV is operating in deep water

questions.source_quote: Remove the port and starboard vertical thruster nozzles if necessary.

questions.stem: During which maintenance procedure should the ROV bullet guard be removed from around the bullet?
options: Pre-deployment TMS connection
Buoyancy installation process
Routine post-dive inspection
Buoyancy removal process
questions.correct_answer: Buoyancy removal process
questions.explanation: The bullet guard is specifically removed as part of the buoyancy removal process, not during installation or other procedures. This step must be completed before the buoyancy modules can be safely extracted.
questions.source_quote: Remove the ROV bullet guard from around the bullet.

questions.stem: What must you do before removing rods from the ROV's buoyancy block?
options: Properly support the buoyancy block
Remove the rods as quickly as possible
Check if the block appears stable before supporting
Only support the block when working at depths greater than 500 meters
questions.correct_answer: Properly support the buoyancy block
questions.explanation: The buoyancy block must be properly supported before rod removal to prevent injury, death, or equipment damage from the block falling or shifting unexpectedly.
questions.source_quote: Failure to properly support the buoyancy block could result in injury or death to personnel and damage to equipment

questions.stem: What must be supporting the buoyancy block before any buoyancy rods are removed?
options: An overhead crane or lifting device
The TMS tether must be disconnected first
A secondary support chain from the A-frame
The ROV must be fully powered down
questions.correct_answer: An overhead crane or lifting device
questions.explanation: The buoyancy block must be physically supported by an overhead crane or lifting device before removing any vertical or horizontal buoyancy rods to prevent the block from falling and causing injury or damage.
questions.source_quote: overhead crane or other lifting device is supporting the buoyancy block prior to removal of the vertical or horizontal buoyancy rods

questions.stem: What must be set up before removing buoyancy rods from the ROV buoyancy block?
options: Hydraulic leveling jacks at each corner of the subsea frame
A crane or lifting device with lifting eyes attached to the designated lift points on the buoyancy block
Additional flotation bags inflated and secured to the frame
A work platform positioned directly above the ROV for technician access
questions.correct_answer: A crane or lifting device with lifting eyes attached to the designated lift points on the buoyancy block
questions.explanation: The buoyancy block requires crane support before removing rods because the block becomes unstable and potentially hazardous once buoyancy is altered. The lifting eyes provide secure attachment points specifically designed for this purpose.
questions.source_quote: Set up a crane or lifting device with lifting eyes attached to the lift points in the buoyancy block. Before removing any buoyancy rods

questions.stem: How are horizontal buoyancy rods attached to the ROV upper frame?
options: Bolted into captive nuts and secured with Loctite
Clamped using adjustable brackets
Welded directly to the frame structure
Inserted into rubber grommets and strapped down

questions.explanation: The horizontal buoyancy rods are specifically bolted into captive nuts in the ROV upper frame and held in place with Loctite to prevent loosening from vibration and movement during operations.
questions.source_quote: The rods are bolted into captive nuts in the ROV upper frame and held in place with Loctite

questions.stem: What is the primary function of vertical buoyancy rods during ROV operations?
options: They provide additional buoyancy lift to the ROV system
They serve as alignment guides when lowering the buoyancy assembly onto the ROV frame
They house electrical cables connecting to the tether management system
They secure the ROV frame to the vessel deck during deployment
questions.correct_answer: They serve as alignment guides when lowering the buoyancy assembly onto the ROV frame
questions.explanation: Vertical buoyancy rods are designed to act as alignment guides, ensuring the buoyancy assembly is properly positioned when being lowered onto the ROV frame during deployment and maintenance procedures.
questions.source_quote: the vertical rods will be used for alignment

questions.stem: When can you release tension on the lifting device during ROV deployment?
options: Immediately after hooking up the ROV to the lift point
After the buoyancy rods have been installed
When the ROV is secured to the deployment station
Once the ROV is fully submerged in the water
questions.correct_answer: After the buoyancy rods have been installed
questions.explanation: You must maintain tension on the lifting device until after the buoyancy rods are installed. Releasing tension too early could allow the ROV to shift or fall before it's properly stabilized by the buoyancy system.
questions.source_quote: Maintain tension with the lifting device until after the buoyancy rods have been installed

questions.stem: When is it safe to remove the lift straps from a Schilling HD ROV?
options: After all buoyancy rods are installed and torqued to specification
When the ROV's main power has been turned off
Once the ROV is safely in the water
After the TMS umbilical has been disconnected

questions.stem: When should the buoyancy mounting spacers be installed relative to the buoyancy blocks?
options: At the same time as the buoyancy blocks
After the buoyancy blocks have been positioned
Only when the ROV is in the water
Before positioning the buoyancy blocks
questions.correct_answer: Before positioning the buoyancy blocks
questions.explanation: The buoyancy mounting spacers must be installed on the upper ROV frame first, as they create the proper mounting interface for the buoyancy blocks. Skipping this step or installing them out of sequence could result in improper block placement and potential damage.

questions.stem: What is the recommended method for removing the pump given its weight of 90 kg (200 lb)?
options: Hand-carry it since it's not too heavy
Drag it along the deck to avoid lifting equipment
Use a hoist to lift and remove it
Use a forklift to transport it

questions.stem: When removing the pump from the hydraulic system, why might it be necessary to rotate the reservoir can?

So the accumulator clears the mounting locations on the reservoir can
To align the pressure relief valve with the pump inlet
To release pressure from the accumulator before removal

questions.explanation: The accumulator is mounted in a position where it would interfere with the pump removal path. Rotating the reservoir can allows the accumulator to clear its mounting locations, providing the clearance needed to safely remove the pump.
questions.source_quote: It may be necessary to simultaneously rotate the reservoir can as the pump is removed in order for the accumulator to clear the mounting locations on the reservoir can.

questions.stem: When should the pump be raised to the upright position during pump body installation?
options: After installing the pump body
Before installing the pump body
During the pump body installation process
Before removing the old pump body from the ROV
questions.correct_answer: Before installing the pump body
questions.explanation: The pump must be raised to the upright position first, before beginning the pump body installation process. This ensures proper positioning and safety access for the subsequent installation steps.
questions.source_quote: Raise the pump to the upright position and install the lifting straps.

questions.stem: According to the operator manual, when should you cover the ROV unit?
options: When you are not working on it
Only during transport
Only when storing for long periods
When it's raining

questions.explanation: The manual states to cover the unit when you are not working on it to protect it from debris and contamination in the work area.

questions.stem: When removing the hydraulic pump from the reservoir, where should it be placed?
options: In a collection container for used fluid
On any available work surface
On a clean, flat surface
On the vessel deck for easy access
questions.correct_answer: On a clean, flat surface
questions.explanation: The pump must be placed on a clean, flat surface to prevent contamination of the hydraulic system and ensure the pump remains stable and undamaged during inspection or maintenance.
questions.source_quote: Lift the pump out of the reservoir and place it on a clean, flat surface.

questions.stem: What hazard exists when the pump is removed from the hydraulic reservoir?
options: The reservoir will release pressure but contain no oil
The oil will evaporate quickly once the pump is removed
The reservoir will be completely empty and safe to handle
Oil will remain in the reservoir and can spill if the reservoir is tipped on its side
questions.correct_answer: Oil will remain in the reservoir and can spill if the reservoir is tipped on its side
questions.explanation: The hydraulic reservoir retains oil even after pump removal. If the reservoir is tipped or tilted, this trapped oil will spill out, creating a slip hazard and potential environmental contamination.
questions.source_quote: There will still be oil in the reservoir after the pump is removed, and this oil will spill if the reservoir is tipped on its side.

questions.stem: When should lifting straps be installed during the pump replacement procedure?
options: After pressure testing the pump
After connecting the pump to hydraulic lines
Before pump body installation
After pump body installation
questions.correct_answer: Before pump body installation
questions.explanation: Lifting straps must be installed on the pump before the pump body is installed to ensure safe handling and proper positioning during the installation process.
questions.source_quote: install the lifting straps.

questions.stem: What must you do before removing the pump from the hydraulic reservoir?
options: Release all hydraulic pressure from the system
Have a container ready to catch oil
Check the oil level in the reservoir
Clean the area around the reservoir
questions.correct_answer: Have a container ready to catch oil
questions.explanation: The manual specifically requires having a container ready to catch oil when removing the pump from the reservoir. This prevents environmental contamination and oil spills during the maintenance procedure.
questions.source_quote: Have a container ready to catch this oil.

questions.stem: During pump service, what components on the head plate should be inspected?

questions.stem: According to the safety warning in the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, how much does the pump housing weigh?

About 100 pounds (45 kilograms)
Around 150 pounds (70 kilograms)

questions.stem: What should you do when installing the pump assembly if the shaft spline won't align?

Force the spline with a pry bar

Rock the unit until the shaft spline aligns

questions.stem: What is the primary function of the frame on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: To house the main power generation system
To control the depth of the ROV during diving
To generate thrust for vehicle movement through water
To provide structural support and protect internal components during operations
questions.correct_answer: To provide structural support and protect internal components during operations

questions.stem: Why is high-pressure nitrogen considered dangerous in ROV operations?
options: It displaces oxygen and can cause asphyxiation in enclosed spaces
It is highly flammable and poses a fire risk
It can cause frostbite if it escapes rapidly from a valve
It reacts explosively with hydraulic fluid
questions.correct_answer: It displaces oxygen and can cause asphyxiation in enclosed spaces
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is inert but dangerous because it displaces oxygen in enclosed or poorly ventilated spaces, creating an asphyxiation hazard. This is why ROV technicians must be aware of nitrogen cylinder locations and ensure proper ventilation when working in confined areas.
questions.source_quote: High-pressure nitrogen is dangerous.

Clockwise until it stops

questions.stem: Where are the pump cans with accumulators located in the UHD-III ROV main hydraulic circuit?

questions.stem: What must be done to a hydraulic circuit before performing disassembly maintenance?

Disconnect all electrical connections only
Connect it to the deck HPU and drain the system
Isolate it and leave it pressurized for easier access

questions.stem: During seal keeper service on the Schilling HD ROV, what should you do with the removed hex head cap screws?

Set them aside as scrap metal for disposal
Retain them—they are needed for reinstallation in later steps

questions.stem: What are the three primary safety hazards during HPU head plate shaft seal replacement?

Steam burns, toxic gas inhalation, and drowning risks
Electrical shock, pressurized hydraulic fluid, and heavy lifting
Chemical exposure, radiation, and extreme temperature variations

These three hazards are directly referenced in the Schilling HD ROV manual safety section for this maintenance task.

questions.stem: How many people are required to safely lift the pump according to the safety rules?

Two people
At least one person plus a hoist
Three people

Apply pressure to only one side of the seal mount
Apply pressure evenly to both sides of the seal mount
Apply maximum pressure quickly to one side at a time

questions.stem: When adjusting jacking screws to level or lift the ROV, what is the correct procedure to prevent one side from being raised higher than the other?

Turn them evenly at approximately 1/2 turn each
Turn them as quickly as possible to save time
Turn each screw completely before moving to the next one

questions.stem: Before using the jacking screws on the Schilling HD ROV, what must be done first?

Thread HHCS into the jacking screw holes and tighten by hand

Only at 50% command setting
Both 50% and 100% command settings

The Operator Manual's maintenance checklist
The dedicated Test Results Form from the service manual
