Into the mounting bracket area of the ROV
questions.correct_answer: Into the mounting bracket area of the ROV
questions.explanation: The valve pack must be secured in its designated mounting bracket area within the ROV frame. This location ensures proper hydraulic routing and protects the component from damage during operations.
questions.source_quote: Place the valve pack into the ROV mounting bracket area.

questions.stem: What should you do when the valve pack does not clear the ROV frame during maintenance?
options: Remove the valve pack entirely from the frame
Loosen the nuts on the opposite end of the supply and return tubes to create working clearance
Replace the supply and return tubes with longer ones
Loosen the mounting bolts holding the valve pack to the frame
questions.correct_answer: Loosen the nuts on the opposite end of the supply and return tubes to create working clearance
questions.explanation: The procedure specifies loosening the nuts on the opposite end of the supply and return tubes so the tubes can be moved out of the way, creating the necessary working clearance without removing or replacing components.
questions.source_quote: It may be necessary to loosen the nuts on the opposite end of the supply and return tubes so the tubes can be moved out of the way.

questions.stem: Where should all flexible hydraulic lines be connected during hydraulic system setup?
options: To the hydraulic pump unit
To the hydraulic reservoir
To the hydraulic manifold
To the valve pack
questions.correct_answer: To the valve pack
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifically instructs to connect all flexible hydraulic lines to the valve pack during setup.
questions.source_quote: Connect all the flexible hydraulic lines to the valve pack.

questions.stem: What is the correct sequence when setting up the hydraulic system?
options: Fill the valve pack before connecting the hydraulic lines
Connect only the supply line before filling
Connect the hydraulic lines after filling the system
Connect the supply and return hydraulic lines to the valve pack before filling
questions.correct_answer: Connect the supply and return hydraulic lines to the valve pack before filling
questions.explanation: The hydraulic lines must be connected to the valve pack first to ensure a complete circuit before introducing hydraulic fluid. This prevents air from entering the system and ensures proper fluid distribution during filling.
questions.source_quote: Connect the supply and return hydraulic lines at side of the valve pack.

questions.stem: During the filling procedure, where should you bleed air from to ensure complete filling?
options: The vent screws on the front of the valve pack
The pressure relief valves on the hydraulic manifold
After the filling procedure is complete
The vent screws on the rear of the valve pack
questions.correct_answer: The vent screws on the front of the valve pack
questions.explanation: According to the manual, you should bleed air from the vent screws on the front of the valve pack while filling to ensure the system fills completely with no trapped air.
questions.source_quote: bleed air from the vent screws on the front of the valve pack

questions.stem: What is the primary reason you must connect hydraulic hoses to the correct valve during ROV operations?
options: To reduce hydraulic fluid consumption
To prevent system damage or malfunction
To increase hydraulic system pressure
To extend the lifespan of the hydraulic hoses
questions.correct_answer: To prevent system damage or malfunction
questions.explanation: Connecting hoses to the wrong valve can cause system damage or malfunction, which is why always verifying the correct valve connection is critical for safe ROV operation.
questions.source_quote: Make sure to connect the hoses to the correct valve.

questions.stem: What are the two types of directional control valves that can be installed inside an ROV valve pack?
options: Proportional and solenoid
Manual and automatic
Inline and rotary
Hydraulic and pneumatic
questions.correct_answer: Proportional and solenoid
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the directional control valves inside ROV valve packs come in two types: proportional valves and solenoid valves.
questions.source_quote: There are two types of directional control valves that can be installed inside the valve pack. The control valve types are; proportional and solenoid.

questions.stem: What equipment should be used to fill the valve pack with hydraulic oil?
options: Compensation cart
Pressure test fixture
Hydraulic hand pump
Pneumatic grease gun
questions.correct_answer: Compensation cart
questions.explanation: The compensation cart is specifically designed to fill the valve pack with hydraulic oil per the standard filling procedure.
questions.source_quote: Use the comp cart to fill the valve pack with oil.

questions.stem: What is the primary function of proportional valves in a hydraulic system?
options: Completely stop hydraulic flow when needed
Cool the hydraulic fluid
Maintain constant hydraulic pressure
Regulate hydraulic flow rate
questions.correct_answer: Regulate hydraulic flow rate
questions.explanation: Proportional valves are designed to modulate hydraulic flow rate, allowing precise control over actuator speed and movement. Unlike simple on/off valves, they can vary flow incrementally based on the input signal.
questions.source_quote: Proportional valves are used to regulate hydraulic flow rate.

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of proportional valves that the PCA controller in SA valve packs can control?
options: 8 proportional valves
16 proportional valves
4 proportional valves
12 proportional valves
questions.correct_answer: 8 proportional valves
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the PCA controller in SA valve packs has a limitation of controlling up to 8 proportional valves. This is a hardware limitation that operators must consider when planning hydraulic system configurations.
questions.source_quote: the PCA controller in the SA valve packs can control up to 8 proportional valves

questions.stem: How do solenoid valves operate in a hydraulic system?
options: They can be adjusted to any position between fully open and fully closed
They operate continuously at a variable speed
They are either fully on or fully off
They vary their output based on the strength of the input signal
questions.correct_answer: They are either fully on or fully off
questions.explanation: Solenoid valves are binary devices—they simply open or close completely in response to an electrical signal, with no intermediate positions or gradual adjustments.
questions.source_quote: Solenoid valves are either on or off.

questions.stem: When should the pop-off valve on the back of the housing be opened?
options: After filling the valve pack with oil
After performing the hydraulic system pressure test
Only when the hydraulic system is already pressurized
Before filling the valve pack with oil
questions.correct_answer: Before filling the valve pack with oil
questions.explanation: The pop-off valve must be opened before filling to prevent pressure buildup during the filling process.
questions.source_quote: Open the pop-off on the back of the housing

questions.stem: What is the first step to replace a valve according to the manual?
options: Remove the valve pack cover by removing the M6 socket head cap screws
Disconnect the hydraulic lines attached to the valve
Depressurize the entire hydraulic system
Label and remove the electrical connectors from the valve
questions.correct_answer: Remove the valve pack cover by removing the M6 socket head cap screws
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that removing the valve pack cover by removing the M6 socket head cap screws is the first step before accessing and replacing the valve.
questions.source_quote: Remove the M6 SHCS from the valve pack cover and remove the cover.

questions.stem: How are valve pack functions configured on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Automatically by the ROV's depth sensor
Through a dedicated hydraulic pressure panel
By manually adjusting valve screws with a tool
Through user control screens
questions.correct_answer: Through user control screens
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, valve pack functions on the Schilling HD ROV are configured directly through user control screens, making adjustments quick and accessible to the operator without physical intervention.
questions.source_quote: Valves are interchangeable and the valve packs are configured for control in the user control screens.

questions.stem: What is one of the proportional valves controlled by the PCA in the Schilling HD ROV hydraulic system?
options: The electro-hydraulic bypass valve
The main hydraulic pump motor controller
The pressure relief valve (PRV) for the valve pack
The thrust vector control actuator
questions.correct_answer: The pressure relief valve (PRV) for the valve pack
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the PCA controls proportional valves, and one of these is specifically the pressure relief valve (PRV) for the valve pack.
questions.source_quote: one of which will be the valve pack PRV (pressure relief valve)

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, what should you do with the PCA during certain operations?
options: Keep the PCA in its mounted position
Remove the PCA from the ROV entirely
Move the PCA out of the way
Secure the PCA with locking pins
questions.correct_answer: Move the PCA out of the way
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states to move the PCA out of the way during the relevant procedure, indicating it may obstruct access or visibility when in its default position.
questions.source_quote: nd move the PCA out of the way.

questions.stem: What is the function of the pressure reducing valve in the Schilling HD ROV hydraulic system?
options: Filters contaminants from the hydraulic oil
Regulates the temperature of the hydraulic fluid
Maintains constant pressure in the valve pack main oil galley
Controls the flow rate of hydraulic fluid to the thrusters
questions.correct_answer: Maintains constant pressure in the valve pack main oil galley
questions.explanation: The pressure reducing valve is specifically designed to maintain a consistent, predetermined pressure level in the valve pack main oil galley, ensuring stable hydraulic system operation.
questions.source_quote: The pressure reducing valve is used to maintain a constant pressure in the valve pack main oil galley

questions.stem: When installing proportional or solenoid valves in the hydraulic system, what must you ensure about the ports?
options: They must be aligned with the corresponding hydraulic lines
They should face away from the operator for safety
They can be installed in any orientation as long as they are tight
They must be rotated 90 degrees for better flow
questions.correct_answer: They must be aligned with the corresponding hydraulic lines
questions.explanation: Proper port alignment ensures correct fluid flow paths through the valve and prevents leaks, damage, or system malfunction. Misaligned ports can cause cross-connection of hydraulic lines or stress on fittings.
questions.source_quote: When installing the proportional or solenoid valves make sure to align the ports with the

questions.stem: When disconnecting the electrical leads from the pressure reducing valve during maintenance, which terminal block and pins should you use?
options: The main power junction box terminals A and B
Terminal block 6 (TB6) pins 2 and 4
Terminal block 8 (TB8) pins 2 and 4
Terminal block 8 (TB8) pins 1 and 3
questions.correct_answer: Terminal block 8 (TB8) pins 1 and 3
questions.explanation: The pressure reducing valve is connected at terminal block 8 on the PCA, specifically at pins 1 and 3. Using the wrong terminal block or pins could damage components or create safety hazards.
questions.source_quote: Disconnect the two electrical leads from the pressure reducing valve located on the PCA at terminal block 8 (TB8) pin 1 and 3

questions.stem: What must be done to O-rings before installing control valves?
options: Coat them with grease
Lubricate them with hydraulic oil
Wipe them clean with solvent
Apply lubricant after installation
questions.correct_answer: Lubricate them with hydraulic oil
questions.explanation: Hydraulic oil is the specified lubricant for O-rings in this hydraulic system. Using the correct lubricant ensures proper sealing and compatibility with the hydraulic fluid.
questions.source_quote: O-rings are installed in the new valve, and they are lubricated with hydraulic oil

questions.stem: What must you verify when installing control valves in a hydraulic system?
options: That the valve is pre-pressurized to test for leaks
That the ports align with the valve pack body
That the pressure port is oriented facing upward
That the mounting bolts are tightened before checking alignment
questions.correct_answer: That the ports align with the valve pack body
questions.explanation: Proper port alignment ensures the valve functions correctly and prevents leakage or damage to the hydraulic system. Misaligned ports can cause pressure drops, fluid leaks, or complete valve failure.
questions.source_quote: the ports align with the valve pack body when installing control valves

questions.stem: What is the correct torque specification for M4 socket head cap screws during reinstallation of the Schilling HD ROV?
options: 3.5 Nm (31 in-lb)
2.8 Nm (24 in-lb)
5.0 Nm (44 in-lb)
1.8 Nm (16 in-lb)
questions.correct_answer: 2.8 Nm (24 in-lb)
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifies 2.8 Nm (24 in-lb) for M4 socket head cap screws. Always use the exact torque specification to prevent under-tightening (which can cause loosening) or over-tightening (which can strip threads or damage components).
questions.source_quote: Torque the M4 SHCS to 2.8 Nm (24 in-lb)

questions.stem: When removing the valve pack cover during routine maintenance, how many fasteners need to be removed?
options: All 14 fasteners
8 fasteners
Only 2 fasteners
12 fasteners
questions.correct_answer: 12 fasteners
questions.explanation: According to the manual, only 12 of the 14 total fasteners on the valve pack cover need to be removed. The remaining 2 fasteners retain the middle spacer to the viewing cover and serve a structural purpose during reassembly.
questions.source_quote: Only 12 of the fasteners need to be removed. Two of the fasteners are used to keep the middle spacer attached to the viewing cover

questions.stem: When removing the PRV (Pressure Relief Valve) on the Schilling HD ROV, what must be done with cable ties?
options: Cable ties should be left in place during PRV removal
Cable ties necessary to access the PRV must be cut and removed
New cable ties should be installed before removing the PRV
Cable ties should be reused after PRV removal
questions.correct_answer: Cable ties necessary to access the PRV must be cut and removed
questions.explanation: To access and remove the PRV, you must first cut and remove any cable ties that are blocking or securing the valve. This is a necessary step in the removal procedure.
questions.source_quote:
those cable ties necessary to remove
the PRV.

questions.stem: Who is responsible for fastening signs to energy-isolating devices on an ROV?
options: Authorized personnel
Any ROV pilot on shift
The client representative
The equipment manufacturer
questions.correct_answer: Authorized personnel
questions.explanation: Only personnel who have been specifically authorized to work on energy-isolating devices should fasten warning signs to them. This ensures the lockout/tagout procedure is properly implemented by trained individuals who understand the hazards.
questions.source_quote: signs fastened by authorized personnel to energy-isolating devices.

questions.stem: What hazard exists when operating the flow controller without the spiral retaining ring fitted?
options: It will automatically shut down to prevent system damage
It can be fully unscrewed under high pressure, creating a potential injury hazard
It will operate normally with slightly reduced performance
It may cause minor seepage but remains safe to operate
questions.correct_answer: It can be fully unscrewed under high pressure, creating a potential injury hazard
questions.explanation: The spiral retaining ring prevents the flow controller from unscrewing under pressure. Without it, the high-pressure conditions can cause the controller to fully unscrew, posing an injury risk to nearby personnel.
questions.source_quote:
Do not operate the flow controller without the spiral retaining ring fitted, as this will
allow it to be fully unscrewed under high pressure conditions resulting in potential injury.

questions.stem: Before performing any work on the PDU, what must you do with external power sources?
options: Rely on the PDU's automatic power-shutoff feature
Turn off the PDU control panel main switch
Disconnect only the primary power cable
Disconnect or de-energize all external power sources supplying energy to the PDU
questions.correct_answer: Disconnect or de-energize all external power sources supplying energy to the PDU
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental electrical safety rule. All external power sources must be disconnected or de-energized before working on the PDU to prevent electric shock, equipment damage, or accidental system activation. Relying solely on control panel switches, automatic features, or disconnecting only one cable leaves other power paths energized.
questions.source_quote:
Ensure that all external power
sources supplying energy to the PDU are dis-
connected or de-energized.

questions.stem: What serious injuries can high-pressure pneumatic and hydraulic systems cause?
options: Piercing skin and causing blindness
Electrical shocks from system components
Burns from overheated hydraulic fluid
Hearing loss from system vibrations
questions.correct_answer: Piercing skin and causing blindness
questions.explanation: High-pressure systems store tremendous energy that, if released, can penetrate human tissue like a projectile or force fluid into the eyes at high velocity, causing blindness.
questions.source_quote:
High-pressure pneumatic and hydraulic systems contain energy that can pierce the skin
or cause blindness.

questions.stem: What is the primary function of the Power Distribution Unit (PDU) in a Schilling HD ROV?
options: To store electrical energy in batteries for backup power
To distribute electrical power from the surface supply to various ROV subsystems and equipment
To generate electrical power for the ROV while submerged
To convert seawater into fresh water for the ROV cooling system
questions.correct_answer: To distribute electrical power from the surface supply to various ROV subsystems and equipment
questions.explanation: The PDU's main role is to take the electrical power supplied from the surface through the tether and distribute it safely to the ROV's various subsystems, including thrusters, cameras, lights, and instrumentation, ensuring each component receives the appropriate voltage and current.
questions.source_quote: ation.

questions.stem: According to equipment safety procedures, what should you be able to do with all energy sources before performing work on ROV equipment?
options: Redirect them to backup systems
Readily identify and isolate them
Document their exact location in the system
Calculate their total power consumption
questions.correct_answer: Readily identify and isolate them
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental lockout/tagout safety principle — before any work begins, you must be able to quickly find and isolate every energy source feeding the equipment to prevent accidental energization.
questions.source_quote: All energy sources for the equipment can be readily identified and isolated.

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to initiate a PDU circuit test on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Turn the circuit's TEST key to the test position
Turn the POWER key to the test position
Press the TEST button on the main control panel
Select 'Circuit Test' from the touchscreen menu
questions.correct_answer: Turn the circuit's TEST key to the test position
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, you initiate a PDU circuit test by turning the circuit's TEST key to the test position. This physical key action activates the test function for that specific circuit.
questions.source_quote: Turn the circuit's TEST key to initiate the test.

questions.stem: Where can potentially lethal voltages exist in the ROV system?
options: Only in the deck cable and LARS
Throughout the entire system, including the control van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
Only in the control van and PDU
Only inside the ROV itself
questions.correct_answer: Throughout the entire system, including the control van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that shock hazard voltages are present throughout the ROV system in all listed locations. You must treat every component as potentially live and dangerous, not just the obvious ones.
questions.source_quote:
SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages present throughout the ROV system, including in the con-
trol van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV, can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: Which areas of the ROV system can contain dangerous voltages that could cause injury or death?
options: Only the control van and PDU
All of the listed areas: control van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
Only the deck cable and TMS
Only the ROV itself
questions.correct_answer: All of the listed areas: control van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that dangerous voltages are present throughout the entire ROV system, not just in isolated areas. This means every component from the surface equipment to the underwater ROV poses a shock hazard.
questions.source_quote:
SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages present throughout the ROV system, including in the con-
trol van, PDU, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV, can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: What happens when you turn the TEST key to test the PDU circuit?
options: The PDU automatically shuts down
The main power supply disconnects
The system resets to factory defaults
The PDU alarm buzzer sounds
questions.correct_answer: The PDU alarm buzzer sounds
questions.explanation: The TEST function on the PDU activates the alarm buzzer to verify the buzzer circuit is working properly. This is a simple test to confirm the audible alarm will function if needed during operations.
questions.source_quote: The PDU alarm buzzer sounds.

questions.stem: When testing PDU circuits, what is the correct approach according to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual?
options: Test multiple circuits at once to save time
Test circuits in groups of two or three
Test each circuit individually
Test circuits only when the ROV is fully powered off
questions.correct_answer: Test each circuit individually
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that each circuit must be tested individually. Testing multiple circuits simultaneously can mask faults or create unsafe conditions because a problem on one circuit may be hidden by another.
questions.source_quote: Each circuit must be tested individually.

questions.stem: How do you silence the alarm buzzer on the PDU?
options: Press the physical reset button on the main console
Use the controls on the PDU touchscreen
Use the joystick from the pilot station
Turn off the PDU main power switch
questions.correct_answer: Use the controls on the PDU touchscreen
questions.explanation: The PDU has an integrated touchscreen interface with controls that allow the operator to acknowledge and silence alarm buzzers directly at the unit itself.
questions.source_quote: Silence the buzzer on the PDU touchscreen.

questions.stem: What should you do if a circuit breaker fails to trip during testing?
options: Lock out and tag out the entire system, report the condition, and do not use the system until a qualified electrical technician corrects the problem
Continue using the system but monitor it more closely for issues
Replace the breaker yourself if you have the proper tools available
Reset the breaker manually and test it again
questions.correct_answer: Lock out and tag out the entire system, report the condition, and do not use the system until a qualified electrical technician corrects the problem
questions.explanation: A circuit breaker that fails to trip is a serious safety hazard because it cannot protect the circuit from overloads. The system must be taken out of service immediately, locked out and tagged out to prevent accidental energization, and only a qualified electrical technician should diagnose and repair the problem.
questions.source_quote: If any circuit breaker fails to trip, lock out and tag out the entire system, report the condition, and do not use the system

questions.stem: What must be done to the PDU control power circuit breaker before testing the emergency stop button?
options: Set it to STANDBY
Leave it in its current position
Set it to OFF
Set it to ON
questions.correct_answer: Set it to ON
questions.explanation: The PDU control power circuit breaker must be in the ON position as a precondition before testing the emergency stop button. Without power enabled through the PDU, the emergency stop circuit cannot be tested for proper operation.
questions.source_quote: Set the PDU control power circuit breaker to the ON position.

questions.stem: When the TEST key is activated on the ROV's electrical system, what is the expected result?
options: The entire electrical system should power down temporarily
The circuit's breaker should remain closed while a warning light activates
The circuit's breaker should trip as expected
The TEST key function should be automatically bypassed for safety
questions.correct_answer: The circuit's breaker should trip as expected
questions.explanation: The TEST key is designed to verify that the circuit protection is functioning correctly by intentionally causing the breaker to trip. This confirms the breaker can open the circuit when needed during an actual fault condition.
questions.source_quote: The circuit's breaker trips.

questions.stem: What must be done BEFORE pressing the emergency stop button on the PDU?
options: Set the main circuit breaker to the OFF position
Wait for the control system to complete its boot sequence
Disconnect the auxiliary power supply
Set the main circuit breaker to the ON position
questions.correct_answer: Set the main circuit breaker to the ON position
questions.explanation: The PDU requires the main circuit breaker to be in the ON position before the emergency stop button can function properly. This ensures the system has power and is ready to respond to an emergency stop command.
questions.source_quote: Set the PDU main circuit breaker to the ON position.

questions.stem: What must be done before testing the emergency stop button on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Connect the tether management system
Activate the sonar imaging system
Turn PDU CONTROL POWER ON
Engage the hydraulic bypass valve
questions.correct_answer: Turn PDU CONTROL POWER ON
questions.explanation: The PDU control power must be energized before testing the emergency stop button so the circuit has power to disable. Skipping this step means you're not properly testing the emergency stop function.
questions.source_quote: Turn PDU CONTROL POWER on.

questions.stem: Where is the emergency stop button located on the Schilling HD ROV PDU for testing?
options: PDU rear panel
ROV main frame
Control pod housing
PDU front panel
questions.correct_answer: PDU front panel
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, the emergency stop button that needs to be tested is located on the PDU front panel. This is the designated testing location for this safety system check.
questions.source_quote: Press the emergency stop button on the PDU front panel.

questions.stem: What should happen to the main circuit breaker when the emergency stop button is pressed?
options: It should automatically reset
It should remain closed to maintain power
It should trip only after a 30-second delay
It should trip
questions.correct_answer: It should trip
questions.explanation: Pressing the emergency stop button triggers the main circuit breaker to trip, cutting power to the ROV systems as a safety measure.
questions.source_quote: The main circuit breaker trips.

questions.stem: What should happen to the MAIN CB ON indicator light when the emergency stop button is pressed?
options: It should turn off
It should remain illuminated
It should flash rapidly
It should turn on
questions.correct_answer: It should turn off
questions.explanation: When the emergency stop is activated, the main circuit breaker is de-energized, so the MAIN CB ON light (which indicates the breaker is energized) will turn off to show power has been cut.
questions.source_quote: The MAIN CB ON light turns off.

questions.stem: When should you unlock and open the upper PDU door to test the door interlock?
options: During any routine maintenance window
When the ROV is completely powered down
While the main circuit breaker is de-energized
While the main circuit breaker is energized
questions.correct_answer: While the main circuit breaker is energized
questions.explanation: The door interlock must be tested while the main circuit breaker is energized to verify it properly de-energizes the circuit when the door is opened, ensuring the safety interlock functions correctly under load.
questions.source_quote: Unlock and open the upper PDU door

questions.stem: The PDU firmware and software upgrade procedure applies to which ROV systems?
options: All Schilling ROV systems including UHD-I
HD, UHD-II, and UHD-III systems
HD and UHD-II systems only
UHD-II and UHD-III systems only
questions.correct_answer: HD, UHD-II, and UHD-III systems
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that the PDU firmware and software upgrade procedure applies to HD, UHD-II, and UHD-III ROV systems. This is a limitation of the procedure—it does not apply to UHD-I or other Schilling models not listed.
questions.source_quote: found in HD, UHD-II, and UHD-III ROV systems

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, which PLC model requires a firmware upgrade?
options: GE RX3i
Allen-Bradley ControlLogix
Siemens S7-300
Schneider Modicon
questions.correct_answer: GE RX3i
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifically identifies the GE RX3i as the PLC model that requires the firmware upgrade. The other options are common PLC brands used in industrial applications but are not the model referenced in this manual.
questions.source_quote: GE RX3i PLC

questions.stem: Which human-machine interface (HMI) on the Schilling HD ROV requires software installation?
options: Allen-Bradley PanelView
Schneider Electric Magelis
Siemens Simatic HMI
GE QuickPanel
questions.correct_answer: GE QuickPanel
questions.explanation: The GE QuickPanel is the designated HMI on the Schilling HD ROV that requires software installation to function. This is a specific hardware requirement for operator interface setup.
questions.source_quote: GE QuickPanel

questions.stem: What must you do BEFORE turning on PDU CONTROL POWER?
options: Set the PDU control power circuit breaker to the ON position
Activate the camera system
Open the TMS override valve
Turn on the hydraulic power unit
questions.correct_answer: Set the PDU control power circuit breaker to the ON position
questions.explanation: The PDU control power circuit breaker must be set to ON first as a safety prerequisite before activating PDU CONTROL POWER. This establishes proper power distribution to the control circuits in the correct operational sequence.
questions.source_quote: Set the PDU control power circuit breaker to the ON position

questions.stem: Which of the following Windows operating systems are supported for performing PDU firmware upgrades on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Windows 2000 or Windows XP only
Windows 2000, XP, Vista, or Windows 7
Windows 8, 8.1, or Windows 10
Linux, Mac OS, or Windows 7
questions.correct_answer: Windows 2000, XP, Vista, or Windows 7
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, the PDU firmware upgrade software is compatible only with Windows 2000, XP, Vista, and Windows 7. Using newer operating systems like Windows 8 or 10, or alternative operating systems like Linux or Mac OS, is not supported for this procedure.
questions.source_quote: Windows 2000, XP, Vista, or Windows 7

questions.stem: What must you do BEFORE turning on the PDU CONTROL POWER?
options: Engage the main hydraulic pump
Set the control power circuit breaker to ON
Power up the sonar system
Activate the TMS power switch
questions.correct_answer: Set the control power circuit breaker to ON
questions.explanation: The control power circuit breaker must be set to ON first to provide power to the PDU control circuits before the PDU CONTROL POWER can be activated. This sequence ensures proper power distribution and prevents electrical faults during startup.
questions.source_quote: Turn PDU CONTROL POWER on

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure for the PDU main circuit breaker when opening the PDU door?
options: Set the circuit breaker to ON before opening the door
Set the circuit breaker to OFF before opening the door
The circuit breaker position does not matter when opening the door
Set the circuit breaker to OFF after opening the door
questions.correct_answer: Set the circuit breaker to ON before opening the door
questions.explanation: The circuit breaker must be ON so the safety monitoring system stays active and can detect/log when the PDU door is opened. This ensures continuous surveillance of the equipment enclosure.
questions.source_quote: Set the PDU main circuit breaker to the ON position

questions.stem: Which of the following operating systems is NOT supported for PDU service on a Windows PC?
options: Windows Vista
Windows 8
Windows 7
Windows XP
questions.correct_answer: Windows 8
questions.explanation: The manual specifically lists Windows 2000, XP, Vista, and Windows 7 as supported operating systems for PDU service. Windows 8 is not included in this approved list.
questions.source_quote: A PC with one of the following operating systems: Windows 2000, XP, Vista, or Windows 7.

questions.stem: Which components require software to display the Schilling Robotics PDU control system?
options: Only the HMI
Both the HMI and the CPU
Neither the HMI nor the CPU
Only the CPU
questions.correct_answer: Both the HMI and the CPU
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, both the HMI and the CPU each require software to display the Schilling Robotics PDU control system. Neither component can display the system without its required software.
questions.source_quote: The HMI and the CPU each require software to display the Schilling Robotics PDU control system.

questions.stem: What type of USB drive is required for PDU firmware and software upgrades on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: USB-C flash drive
USB 3.0 A-type drive
USB 2.0 A-type drive
USB 2.0 C-type drive
questions.correct_answer: USB 2.0 A-type drive
questions.explanation: The manual specifically requires a blank USB 2.0 A-type drive for PDU firmware and software upgrades. Using the wrong USB type or version could prevent the upgrade from loading properly.
questions.source_quote: a blank USB drive (2.0 A-type)

questions.stem: When upgrading software on the PDU, what must you do with the GE PLC and HMI software?
options: Only upgrade whichever component needs a newer version
Upgrade the HMI software first, then the PLC separately
Upgrade the PLC software first, then the HMI separately
Upgrade them at the same time
questions.correct_answer: Upgrade them at the same time
questions.explanation: The GE PLC and HMI hardware components are interdependent and both require software updates to function properly together. Failing to upgrade them simultaneously can cause compatibility issues or system failures.
questions.source_quote: The GE PLC and HMI hardware components each use software that must be upgraded at the same time.

questions.stem: Which type of external memory card used with the ROV system is larger, the SD card or the CF card?
options: The manual does not specify their relative sizes
They are approximately the same size
CF (Compact Flash) card
SD (Secure Digital) card
questions.correct_answer: CF (Compact Flash) card
questions.explanation: The CF (Compact Flash) card is physically larger than the SD (Secure Digital) card. When storing or handling these memory devices, remember that CF cards require more space than the smaller SD cards.
questions.source_quote: "Secure digital" is a type of external media (memory device) that is somewhat smaller than CF.

questions.stem: Where should the geupdate folder be placed on the USB drive for the Schilling HD ROV system to recognize it?
options: In any location on the USB drive as long as it's named correctly
In a folder named 'updates' on the USB drive
Inside a subdirectory on the USB drive
On the root of the USB drive
questions.correct_answer: On the root of the USB drive
questions.explanation: The geupdate folder must sit directly on the root of the USB drive, not nested inside any subdirectory, for the ROV system to properly locate and execute the software update files.
questions.source_quote: The geupdate folder must sit on the root of the USB drive

questions.stem: What is another common name for a USB drive?
options: Hard drive
Ethernet cable
Memory stick
Power strip
questions.correct_answer: Memory stick
questions.explanation: A USB drive is commonly referred to as a memory stick, thumb drive, or flash drive. These terms are used interchangeably in the industry to describe portable USB storage devices.
questions.source_quote: Universal serial bus external memory device, commonly referred to as memory stick, thumb drive, flash drive, etc.

questions.stem: What must be set to 2.0 in the Electrical and PDU subsystem?
options: 2.0 volts
2.0 amps
2.0 watts
2.0 ohms
questions.correct_answer: 2.0 volts
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifies that the voltage parameter in the Electrical and PDU subsystem must be maintained at 2.0 volts for proper operation.
questions.source_quote: must be 2.0

questions.stem: What does the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual state about USB drive compatibility?
options: USB drives cannot be used with the ROV system
Only brand-name USB drives are supported
Not all USB drive types may be compatible
All USB drive types are guaranteed to work
questions.correct_answer: Not all USB drive types may be compatible
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly warns that other types of USB drives may not be compatible with the ROV system. Operators should verify USB drive compatibility before attempting to use them.
questions.source_quote: Other types of USB drive types may not be compatible.

questions.stem: During ROV system maintenance, what is the correct procedure for installing software and firmware?
options: They have separate installation processes that must follow the order specified in the service document
Firmware should always be installed after software to avoid conflicts
They can be installed in any order since they are independent systems
They must be installed together as a single combined package
questions.correct_answer: They have separate installation processes that must follow the order specified in the service document
questions.explanation: Software and firmware are distinct components with their own installation procedures that must be followed in a specific sequence as outlined in the service documentation.
questions.source_quote: The software and firmware each have separate installation processes and these processes must occur in the order indicated in this document.

questions.stem: What might you need to do with downloaded files before using them, depending on your operating system?
options: Format the USB drive and retransfer the files
Run an executable installer from the folder
Unzip the folder and copy the files
Rename the folder before copying it
questions.correct_answer: Unzip the folder and copy the files
questions.explanation: According to the operator manual, you may need to unzip the folder then copy the files depending on your operating system before the files can be used.
questions.source_quote: Depending upon your operating system, you may need to unzip the folder then copy the files.

questions.stem: What must you do with the ZIP file after extracting the firmware files from the USB drive?
options: Delete the ZIP file from the root of the USB drive
Keep the ZIP file on the USB drive as a backup copy
Move the ZIP file to a subfolder on the USB drive
Rename the ZIP file to indicate it has been extracted
questions.correct_answer: Delete the ZIP file from the root of the USB drive
questions.explanation: The ZIP file must be deleted from the root of the USB drive after firmware extraction to prevent confusion and ensure only the extracted files are used during the firmware loading process.
questions.source_quote: Delete the ZIP file from the root of the USB drive

questions.stem: When should you upgrade the PLC firmware on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: Only when the PLC fails to respond to commands
Whenever a new software version is released, regardless of compatibility
When the PLC firmware is not compatible with the current software upgrade version
Whenever you perform routine maintenance on the ROV
questions.correct_answer: When the PLC firmware is not compatible with the current software upgrade version
questions.explanation: The operator manual specifies that the PLC firmware must be upgraded only when it is incompatible with the current software upgrade version, so that is the correct condition for an upgrade.
questions.source_quote: The PLC also has firmware that must be upgraded when it is not compatible with the current software upgrade version.

questions.stem: After checksum completes successfully on external media, what is the next step according to the firmware update procedure?
options: Restart the GE CPU to verify the update
Remove the external media from the ROV
Run the checksum verification again to confirm integrity
Copy the remaining files to their respective destinations
questions.correct_answer: Copy the remaining files to their respective destinations
questions.explanation: The procedure clearly states that after checksum completes successfully on external media, you should copy the remaining files to their respective destinations using the same process described for the GE CPU Firmware ZIP file.
questions.source_quote: Once checksum has completed successfully on the external media, use the process described above for the GE CPU Firmware ZIP file to copy the remaining files to their respective external media

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to verify firmware integrity on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Double-click the Chk-MD5.BAT file on the external media
Run the Chk-MD5.BAT file from the ROV's internal memory
Access the firmware verification menu through the control interface
Double-click the Chk-MD5.EXE file on the external media
questions.correct_answer: Double-click the Chk-MD5.BAT file on the external media
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states to double-click the Chk-MD5.BAT file on the external media (USB, CF, or SD) to verify firmware integrity. Using the .EXE file or running from internal memory would not follow the correct procedure.
questions.source_quote: Double click on the Chk-MD5.BAT file on the external media (USB, CF, or SD)

questions.stem: How do you determine if the HMI on a Schilling ROV is a QuickPanel or a QuickPanel+?
options: Look at the rear connection panel for model markings
Check the front panel label on the HMI
Read the serial number label on the power distribution unit
Access the diagnostic menu through the control software
questions.correct_answer: Check the front panel label on the HMI
questions.explanation: The HMI type (QuickPanel vs QuickPanel+) is identified by checking the front panel label directly on the HMI unit itself. This is the designated identification point specified in the procedure.
questions.source_quote: Check the front of the HMI to see if you have a QuickPanel or a QuickPanel+

questions.stem: When should the GE software source files be checksummed in relation to loading them into the PLC?
options: During the loading process
At any time during the operation
After loading them into the PLC
Before loading them into the PLC
questions.correct_answer: Before loading them into the PLC
questions.explanation: The GE software source files can only be checksummed before loading into the PLC because the loading process modifies the files on the external memory device. Once the loading process occurs, the files are changed and can no longer be verified by checksum.
questions.source_quote: the GE software source files can only be checked before loading them into the PLC. This is because the loading process will change the files on the external memory device

questions.stem: How many HMI software files do you need for your Schilling HD ROV system?
options: One
Two
Four
Three
questions.correct_answer: One
questions.explanation: Only one HMI software file is required for the entire system. You select the correct file based on the type of HMI device that is installed on your particular system.
questions.source_quote: You will only need one HMI file, depending upon which type of HMI device you have on your system

questions.stem: To check the firmware version on the Schilling HD ROV, which page should you navigate to?
options: System configuration screen
PLC STATUS & SETUP page
Main diagnostic menu
Boot-up splash screen
questions.correct_answer: PLC STATUS & SETUP page
questions.explanation: The PLC STATUS & SETUP page displays firmware version information as the dedicated location for viewing this data in the system.
questions.source_quote: look on the PLC STATUS & SETUP page to view the firmware version number

questions.stem: What should you do if the checksum reports that the copy process was NOT successful?
options: Power cycle the entire ROV system immediately
Replace the PDU and run a full diagnostic
Retry the copy process or try a different approved USB drive
Reformat the USB drive and start from scratch
questions.correct_answer: Retry the copy process or try a different approved USB drive
questions.explanation: The manual states that when checksum reports an unsuccessful copy, you should retry the process or try a different approved USB drive. This is the specified corrective action for this error condition.
questions.source_quote: If checksum reports that the copy process was NOT successful, you must try again or try a another approved USB drive

questions.stem: What position should the RDSD switch be set to before uploading firmware?
options: Set the RDSD switch to the UPLOAD position
Set the RDSD switch to the DOWNLOAD position
Turn off the RDSD switch completely
Leave the RDSD switch in its current position
questions.correct_answer: Set the RDSD switch to the UPLOAD position
questions.explanation: The RDSD switch must be set to the UPLOAD position prior to initiating a firmware upload. This positioning configures the system to accept the new firmware file correctly.
questions.source_quote: Set the RDSD switch (see image below) to UPLOAD

questions.stem: What must you do before performing a PLC CPU firmware update on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Power down the ROV for a minimum of 30 minutes
Replace the PLC CPU battery with a fresh one
Disconnect all thruster feedback cables from the PLC
Save or back up all programs and data resident in the PLC CPU
questions.correct_answer: Save or back up all programs and data resident in the PLC CPU
questions.explanation: The firmware update procedure requires you to first preserve all existing programs and data by saving or backing them up. Without this backup, the firmware update could result in permanent data loss, requiring time-consuming reconfiguration or component replacement.
questions.source_quote: Save or back up any programs or data resident in the PLC CPU before performing the firmware update.

questions.stem: After completing a firmware update on the ROV's PLC, what must you do next?
options: Restart the entire ROV system
Restore the programs and data to the PLC CPU
Replace the PLC CPU module
Recalibrate all sensor systems
questions.correct_answer: Restore the programs and data to the PLC CPU
questions.explanation: Firmware updates wipe the PLC's memory, so you must restore the programs and data resident in the PLC CPU to return the system to operational status.
questions.source_quote: you must restore programs and data resident in the PLC CPU

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure when finished using the PLC with a USB drive?
options: Just switch off the PLC power supply and leave the USB drive in
Remove the USB drive first, then switch off the PLC power supply
Switch off the PLC power supply, then remove the USB drive
Remove the USB drive, then switch off the PLC power supply
questions.correct_answer: Switch off the PLC power supply, then remove the USB drive
questions.explanation: Always power down the PLC before removing the USB drive to prevent data corruption or damage to the equipment. Removing the USB while the system is still powered can cause read/write errors and potential hardware issues.
questions.source_quote: Switch off the PLC power supply and remove the USB drive.

questions.stem: When upgrading software following a firmware update on the Schilling HD ROV (e.g., going from Rev 10 to Rev 11), is it necessary to save current programs?
options: No, saving current programs is not required
Yes, you must save programs only when the revision jump is two or more versions
Yes, you must save current programs before any software or firmware change
No, saving programs is only required when downgrading software
questions.correct_answer: No, saving current programs is not required
questions.explanation: According to the manual, when upgrading software after a firmware update, the current programs do not need to be saved because the upgrade process preserves them.
questions.source_quote: If you are upgrading the software after the firmware update (e.g. going from Rev 10 to Rev 11), you do not need to save the current programs

questions.stem: What does it mean when the RDSD LED turns green?
options: There is a fault in the system
The system is powered off and in standby
The system is ready to begin firmware upload
The system is currently uploading firmware
questions.correct_answer: The system is ready to begin firmware upload
questions.explanation: According to the manual, the green RDSD LED specifically indicates that the system is ready to begin firmware upload, not that an upload is in progress or that there is a fault.
questions.source_quote: The RDSD LED will turn green

questions.stem: How do you initialize the CPU according to the operator manual?
options: Turn on the PLC power switch first, then press and hold the START button
Press the START button twice while turning on the PLC power switch
Press and release the START button quickly, then turn on the PLC power switch
Press and hold the START button while turning on the PLC power switch
questions.correct_answer: Press and hold the START button while turning on the PLC power switch
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states you must press and hold the START button first, then while continuing to hold it, turn on the PLC power switch. Reversing the order or changing the button press method will not properly initialize the CPU.
questions.source_quote: Press and hold the START button. While continuing to hold the START button, turn on the PLC power switch.

questions.stem: What type of USB drive must you use when uploading firmware to the CPU?
options: USB 2.0 A-type flash drive that is blank
USB 2.0 C-type flash drive that is blank
Any USB flash drive, as long as it is blank
USB 3.0 A-type flash drive with the latest firmware
questions.correct_answer: USB 2.0 A-type flash drive that is blank
questions.explanation: The CPU only accepts USB 2.0 A-type drives, and using a blank drive prevents conflicts with existing firmware during the upload process.
questions.source_quote: Insert a blank USB drive into the CPU (use only USB drive 2.0 A-type)

questions.stem: Before uploading firmware to the PLC, what should you set the CPU run mode to?
options: STOP
RUN
PAUSE
PROGRAM
questions.correct_answer: STOP
questions.explanation: Setting the CPU to STOP mode halts all processing, preventing the PLC from executing code during the firmware upload which could cause errors or damage.
questions.source_quote: Set RUN MODE to STOP

questions.stem: How many times should you push the START button to initiate the firmware upload process?
options: Press the RESET button instead
Push the START button once
Hold the START button for three seconds
Push the START button twice
questions.correct_answer: Push the START button once
questions.explanation: The firmware upload process begins immediately with a single push of the START button. Additional pushes or holding the button are unnecessary and may interfere with the upload.
questions.source_quote: Push the START button once, and the process will begin

questions.stem: On UHD-III PDUs, where is the control power breaker located?
options: Power distribution panel
Main control console
Emergency shutdown panel
PDU junction box
questions.correct_answer: Power distribution panel
questions.explanation: The control power breaker on UHD-III PDUs is specifically located in the power distribution panel. This is the designated location for this critical control component.
questions.source_quote: On UHD-III PDUs, turn on the control power breaker that is located in the power distribution panel

questions.stem: Before performing any service or maintenance on the Power Distribution Unit (PDU), what must be confirmed first?
options: All power sources to the PDU are disconnected and locked out
The PDU housing is cool to the touch
Backup batteries are fully charged
The ROV is fully powered up and operational
questions.correct_answer: All power sources to the PDU are disconnected and locked out
questions.explanation: Electrical service procedures require that all power sources be disconnected and locked out before beginning any maintenance work to prevent electric shock, arc flash, or accidental energizing of components. This is a fundamental safety requirement for all electrical equipment servicing.
questions.source_quote: Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Service

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure to start the QuickPanel touch screen on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Turn the PDU supply breaker to the ON position
Press the power button directly on the QuickPanel touch screen
Navigate to the system startup menu and select QuickPanel
Turn the main bus voltage selector to the 24V setting
questions.correct_answer: Turn the PDU supply breaker to the ON position
questions.explanation: The QuickPanel touch screen is powered through the PDU, so the correct startup procedure is to turn the PDU supply breaker to the ON position. This supplies power to the entire panel system including the touch screen.
questions.source_quote: Start the QuickPanel touch screen by turning the PDU supply breaker to the ON position

questions.stem: What happens when the QuickPanel touch screen starts up?
options: The screen displays diagnostic information automatically
A prompt displays asking for user confirmation
The system automatically connects to the ROV
A prompt displays asking for confirmation before shutdown
questions.correct_answer: A prompt displays asking for user confirmation
questions.explanation: According to the manual, when the QuickPanel touch screen starts up, it displays a prompt asking for user confirmation before proceeding. This is a safety feature to ensure intentional system activation.
questions.source_quote: When the QuickPanel touch screen starts up, the following prompt displays

questions.stem: According to the operator manual, what is the maximum acceptable time for downloading software to the CPU?
options: Less than 1 minute
Less than 3 minutes
Less than 5 minutes
Less than 10 minutes
questions.correct_answer: Less than 3 minutes
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that the software download to the CPU must complete in less than three minutes as a precondition for proper system operation.
questions.source_quote: The time required to download software to the CPU should be less than three minutes.

questions.stem: When the QuickPanel touch screen prompts you to load new software from the compact flash card, what should you do?
options: Select Yes
Select No
Select Cancel
Select OK
questions.correct_answer: Select Yes
questions.explanation: The manual specifically instructs to select 'Yes' on the prompt to load new software from the compact flash card onto the QuickPanel touch screen.
questions.source_quote: Select Yes. The QuickPanel touch screen will load the new software from the compact flash card

questions.stem: If the RDSD LED does not turn green after inserting the USB drive for a PLC software update, what should you do before contacting Schilling Robotics technical support?
options: Press the start button repeatedly until the LED turns green
Immediately try a different USB drive
Turn off the PLC and restart the entire procedure
Unplug and reinsert the USB drive, waiting 5 seconds between attempts, up to four times
questions.correct_answer: Unplug and reinsert the USB drive, waiting 5 seconds between attempts, up to four times
questions.explanation: The manual states that the CPU may not recognize the USB drive on the first attempt. You should unplug and reinsert the USB drive, waiting 5 seconds between tries, and try this at least four times before escalating to technical support. This is a normal occurrence and not a malfunction.
questions.source_quote: Turn on the PLC power switch. The PLC will power up to normal operation with the upgraded firmware. CPU Software: Update Refer to the photo of the PLC for the following steps. 1. Ensure that the PLC p

questions.stem: How do you select the ROV system type on the PDU?
options: Press and hold the Type selector button for 3 seconds until the desired type appears
Press anywhere on the Type selector window to toggle through HD, UHD2, or UHD3
Access the System Configuration menu from the main touchscreen
Rotate the physical dial next to the display until the correct type is selected
questions.correct_answer: Press anywhere on the Type selector window to toggle through HD, UHD2, or UHD3
questions.explanation: The PDU uses a touch-sensitive Type selector window where a single press anywhere on it cycles through the available ROV system types in sequence: HD, UHD2, or UHD3.
questions.source_quote: Press anywhere on the PDU Type selector window to toggle through the ROV system types (HD, UHD2, or UHD3)

questions.stem: After completing all settings on the PDU, what button should you tap to return to the main screen?
options: The yellow BACK button
The red EXIT button
The green CONFIRM button
The blue NEXT button
questions.correct_answer: The blue NEXT button
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, after completing all PDU settings, you should tap the blue NEXT button to return to the main screen.
questions.source_quote: tap the blue NEXT button to display the main screen

questions.stem: After selecting the system type, what button do you press to display the voltage setup page?
options: Press NEXT
Press MENU
Press BACK
Press ENTER
questions.correct_answer: Press NEXT
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states that after selecting the system type, pressing NEXT will display the voltage setup page. This is the correct sequence for proceeding through the PDU setup wizard.
questions.source_quote: Press NEXT. The voltage setup page will be displayed.

questions.stem: When setting the nominal voltage on the PDU, how do you select the correct voltage range?
options: Press the RESET button first, then manually enter the voltage value
Tap the CHANGE buttons to scroll through available values until the correct range appears
Rotate the voltage dial until the exact value is displayed
Hold the CHANGE button continuously until the voltage locks in automatically
questions.correct_answer: Tap the CHANGE buttons to scroll through available values until the correct range appears
questions.explanation: The procedure requires tapping the CHANGE buttons to toggle through the available nominal voltage values, not holding them or using a dial. This allows the operator to scroll through the preset options until the correct range is displayed.
questions.source_quote: Toggle through the available nominal voltage values by tapping each of the CHANGE buttons until the correct voltage range is displayed

questions.stem: How do you access the SETUP & STATUS page on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Navigate to it through the DIAGNOSTICS menu
Double-click the background of the main screen
Press the ESC key twice from any screen
Click the SETUP & STATUS button on the main screen
questions.correct_answer: Click the SETUP & STATUS button on the main screen
questions.explanation: The SETUP & STATUS page is accessed directly by clicking its corresponding button on the main screen. This is the primary navigation path for operators to view system status and configuration settings.
questions.source_quote: Click on the SETUP & STATUS button to display that page

questions.stem: To access the PANEL SETUP AND INFORMATION page on the Schilling HD ROV, which button should you select?
options: Tap the STATUS button
Press the DIAGNOSTICS button
Select the PANEL SETUP button
Press the MAIN MENU button
questions.correct_answer: Select the PANEL SETUP button
questions.explanation: The PANEL SETUP button is the dedicated control that opens the PANEL SETUP AND INFORMATION page, allowing access to panel configuration settings and system information.
questions.source_quote: Select the PANEL SETUP button to display the PANEL SETUP AND INFORMATION page

questions.stem: How many central unit relay PCBs are located in the Compass PDU?
options: One
Three
Two
Four
questions.correct_answer: Three
questions.explanation: The Compass PDU is specifically designed with three central unit relay PCBs. This is a fixed configuration in the Schilling HD ROV system, so any other number would indicate an incorrect system description.
questions.source_quote: There are three PDU central unit relay PCBs in the Compass PDU.

questions.stem: When the QuickPanel is not mounted on the PDU, which option should you select to configure remote connectivity?
options: SET IP TO LOCAL HMI
SET IP TO REMOTE HMI (148)
CHECK PDU NETWORK STATUS
MOUNT QUICKPANEL TO PDU
questions.correct_answer: SET IP TO REMOTE HMI (148)
questions.explanation: When the QuickPanel is not physically mounted on the PDU, the SET IP TO REMOTE HMI (148) option must be selected to establish remote connectivity with the system.
questions.source_quote: select SET IP TO REMOTE HMI (148) if the QuickPanel is mounted elsewhere

questions.stem: Which ROV product lines does this procedure apply to?
options: HD ROV and UHD-III ROV
HD ROV and UHD-IV ROV
UHD-I ROV and UHD-III ROV
HD ROV and UHD-II ROV
questions.correct_answer: HD ROV and UHD-III ROV
questions.explanation: According to the manual, this procedure is specifically designed for the HD ROV and UHD-III ROV product lines.
questions.source_quote: Product Line: HD ROV, UHD-III ROV

questions.stem: What steps must be performed after installing a PDU before the system is ready for operation?
options: Check hydraulic fluid levels and inspect all connections for leaks
Calibrate the manipulator arm and test all thruster response
Run the built-in diagnostic test and review error logs
Power down and back up, then verify system functionality using the power page on the control console
questions.correct_answer: Power down and back up, then verify system functionality using the power page on the control console
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that after PDU installation, a complete power cycle (power down then back up) must be performed, followed by verification of system functionality using the power page on the control console. This ensures the PDU is properly initialized and all systems are responding correctly.
questions.source_quote: completely power down the PDU, power it back up, and perform a full functionality check (including using the power page on the control console for system functionality verification)

questions.stem: What must you do with the SD or CF memory card after installing the PDU?
options: Remove the memory card from the PDU
Replace the memory card with a new one after installation
Format the memory card before removal
Leave the memory card in place for backup storage
questions.correct_answer: Remove the memory card from the PDU
questions.explanation: The system will not function correctly if the memory card is left in place after installation. Always remove the memory card to ensure proper PDU operation.
questions.source_quote: Make sure to remove the SD or CF memory card. The system will not function correctly if

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, how many technicians are required to perform this procedure?
options: 1 technician
2 technicians
3 technicians
A full team of 4 technicians
questions.correct_answer: 1 technician
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that a single technician can safely perform this procedure, which is a personnel efficiency feature of this particular maintenance task.
questions.source_quote: Personnel: 1 technician

questions.stem: When removing or replacing CU relay PCBs from the PDU, what is the correct procedure?
options: PCBs can be replaced in any order as long as the power supply is disconnected first
Multiple PCBs can be removed simultaneously as long as they are properly labeled before removal
Remove and replace only one CU relay PCB at a time to prevent cross-connections between PCBs
All PCBs should be removed at once to minimize the time the system is open
questions.correct_answer: Remove and replace only one CU relay PCB at a time to prevent cross-connections between PCBs
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual explicitly states that only one CU relay PCB should be removed or replaced at a time to prevent cross-connections between the PCBs. This safety rule protects the system from damage that could occur if multiple PCBs are exposed simultaneously.
questions.source_quote: Remove and replace only one CU relay PCB at a time to avoid any cross-connections between the PCBs

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, when are lethal voltages still present inside the PDU?
options: Even when the main circuit breaker is open
Only when the system is completely powered down
Only when the PDU door is open
Only during emergency shutdown procedures
questions.correct_answer: Even when the main circuit breaker is open
questions.explanation: The manual specifically warns that lethal voltages can be present inside the PDU even when the main circuit breaker is open. This means you cannot rely on the main breaker alone to confirm the system is safe — always treat all PDU components as potentially energized.
questions.source_quote: lethal voltages are present inside the PDU even when the PDU main circuit breaker is open

questions.stem: What must you do to ALL external circuit breakers before servicing the PDU?
options: Open and lock out/tag out all of them
Turn them off and proceed immediately
Verify they are properly labeled
Disconnect only the main power cable
questions.correct_answer: Open and lock out/tag out all of them
questions.explanation: For electrical safety during PDU servicing, you must physically open AND apply lock out/tag out procedures to ALL external circuit breakers. This ensures the equipment cannot be re-energized while personnel are working, protecting against accidental startup.
questions.source_quote: open and lock out/tag out ALL external circuit breakers that control power to the PDU

questions.stem: Which panel must you open to access the central unit relay PCBs?
options: The rear panel
The top panel
The right side PDU panel
The left side PDU panel
questions.correct_answer: The right side PDU panel
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV manual, the central unit relay PCBs are located in the central panel, and the right side PDU panel provides access to them.
questions.source_quote: Locate the right side PDU panel and open it to gain access to the central unit relay (CU) PCBs in the central panel.

questions.stem: When must PDU lockout/tagout (LOTO) be performed according to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual?
options: During routine cleaning of the PDU
After completing the procedure
Before beginning the procedure
Only if the PDU shows signs of malfunction
questions.correct_answer: Before beginning the procedure
questions.explanation: PDU LOTO is a safety precondition that must be completed before starting any procedure to ensure electrical isolation and prevent accidental energization.
questions.source_quote: PDU: Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) on page 390

questions.stem: Where are the central unit relay PCBs located?
options: In the junction boxes mounted on the ROV frame
In the central panel of the PDU
In the operator control room consoles
In the remote panels along the tether
questions.correct_answer: In the central panel of the PDU

questions.stem: What are the test voltages for dual-voltage slip rings rated at 6600 VAC and 4500 VAC?
options: 8500 VDC and 6000 VDC
11000 VDC and 7500 VDC
10000 VDC and 7000 VDC
9600 VDC and 5800 VDC
questions.correct_answer: 9600 VDC and 5800 VDC
questions.explanation: For dual-voltage slip rings, the test voltage is approximately 1.45 times the rated voltage. The 6600 VAC rating uses 9600 VDC testing, and the 4500 VAC rating uses 5800 VDC testing.

questions.stem: At what temperature are winding resistance measurements performed, and what is the tolerance?
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that nominal winding resistance is measured at 25 degrees Celsius with a tolerance of plus or minus 10 percent. This standardized condition ensures consistent and comparable readings across different units.
questions.source_quote: Nominal Winding Resistance (Ω) @ 25°C ± 10%

questions.stem: What is the insulation resistance test voltage for motors with a 3000 VAC operating voltage?
options: 4200 VDC
3000 VDC
5000 VDC
2500 VDC
questions.correct_answer: 4200 VDC
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV specifications, motors operating at 3000 VAC require an insulation resistance test voltage of 4200 VDC to safely verify insulation integrity without damaging the windings.
questions.source_quote: Operating Voltage (VAC) 3000, Test Voltage (VDC)* 4200

questions.stem: Under what ambient temperature condition should motor winding resistance values be measured according to the Schilling HD ROV manual?
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that motor winding resistance must be measured at 25°C with a tolerance of ±10%, which gives a range of 22.5°C to 27.5°C to ensure consistent and comparable readings.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV operator manual, what is the minimum acceptable insulation resistance reading?
options: Greater than 1 megohm
Greater than 20 megohms
Greater than 5 megohms
Greater than 10 megohms
questions.correct_answer: Greater than 10 megohms
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that acceptable insulation resistance must be greater than 10 megohms. Anything below this threshold indicates degraded insulation that could create shock or short-circuit hazards in the ROV's electrical system.
questions.source_quote: Acceptable Insulation Resistance (MΩ) >10

questions.stem: What is the nominal winding resistance of the 75 horsepower motor at 25 degrees Celsius?
options: 6.1 ohms
2.8 ohms
8.5 ohms
4.3 ohms
questions.correct_answer: 4.3 ohms
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, the 75 horsepower motor has a nominal winding resistance of 4.3 ohms measured at 25 degrees Celsius. This baseline resistance value is used for motor diagnostics and troubleshooting.

questions.stem: What is the nominal winding resistance of the 150 horsepower motor at 25 degrees Celsius?
options: 0.8 ohms
1.8 ohms
1.2 ohms
2.8 ohms
questions.correct_answer: 1.8 ohms
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV specifications, the 150 horsepower motor has a nominal winding resistance of 1.8 ohms measured at 25 degrees Celsius. This value is used as a baseline when performing continuity and resistance checks during troubleshooting.

questions.stem: What are the operating voltage and insulation resistance test voltage requirements for Sytek motors with part numbers 005-4841, 005-4814/005-5819, and 005-5820/005-5772?
options: 2400 VAC operating voltage and 3600 VDC test voltage
3000 VDC operating voltage and 4200 VAC test voltage
4000 VAC operating voltage and 5000 VDC test voltage
3000 VAC operating voltage and 4200 VDC test voltage
questions.correct_answer: 3000 VAC operating voltage and 4200 VDC test voltage
questions.explanation: The Sytek motors listed are rated for 3000 VAC during normal operation. For insulation resistance testing, the manual specifies 4200 VDC, which is a standard practice to verify insulation integrity at a higher potential than operating voltage.

questions.stem: When configuring the insulation resistance test unit, where should you obtain the resistance values from?
options: The ROV diagnostic software database
The calibration sticker on the test unit itself
The default factory settings in the PDU menu
The Resistance Value Tables on page 439
questions.correct_answer: The Resistance Value Tables on page 439
questions.explanation: The manual specifically directs you to use the Resistance Value Tables on page 439 when configuring the insulation resistance test unit. These tables contain the precise resistance values required for accurate testing.
questions.source_quote: Refer to the values specified in the Resistance Value Tables on page 439 to configure the insulation resistance test unit

questions.stem: What is the nominal winding resistance of the 200 horsepower motor at 25 degrees Celsius?
options: 1.5 ohms
3.2 ohms
1.9 ohms
2.5 ohms
questions.correct_answer: 1.9 ohms
questions.explanation: The nominal winding resistance of 1.9 ohms is a specified operating parameter for the 200 HP motor measured at a standard reference temperature of 25°C. This baseline value is essential for motor diagnostics and comparing resistance readings during troubleshooting.

questions.stem: What is required when removing the slip ring from the winch due to its weight?
options: A lifting device or two workers
Three workers are required to safely remove it
A single worker wearing safety gloves
The slip ring can be removed by one person without equipment
questions.correct_answer: A lifting device or two workers
questions.explanation: The slip ring weighs over 40 kg (88 lb), which exceeds what a single person can safely handle. The manual specifies that either a lifting device or two workers must be used to prevent injury during removal.
questions.source_quote:
The slip ring weighs over 40 kg (88 lb) and will require a lifting device or two workers
to remove it from the winch.

questions.stem: When installing the J-box, what must you do before securing it to the mounting plates?
options: Tighten the mounting bolts and then verify alignment
Align the mounting holes with the correct location on the mounting plates
Secure the J-box first, then adjust the position as needed
Position the J-box anywhere initially, then secure and check alignment later

questions.explanation: The J-box must be properly positioned with its mounting holes aligned with the mounting plates before securing, otherwise the unit may not be mounted correctly or could be damaged during installation.

options: Only test the 8th conductor if your system has one
Replace the 8th conductor before conducting any resistance tests
Never test the 8th conductor since it is optional
Always test the 8th conductor as it is standard in all systems

questions.stem: What fasteners are used to secure the J-box to the mounting plates on the winch?

Six hex head cap screws with washers
Four hex head cap screws without washers
Four hex head cap screws with washers

questions.explanation: The manual specifies that four hex head cap screws (HHCS) with washers are the correct fasteners to secure the J-box to the winch mounting plates. The hex head design provides the necessary torque capability, and the washers ensure proper load distribution.

Self-locking nuts
Wing nuts
Standard hex nuts

questions.stem: What safety step must be completed before removing a winch slip ring from the Schilling HD ROV winch assembly?

The winch must be fully energized to test the slip ring
The winch hydraulic pressure must be at maximum operating pressure
The winch must be de-energized and lockout/tagout procedures applied

questions.stem: Why must mounting bolts be coated with Aqua Shield?

To create a watertight seal between mounted components
To reduce vibration and prevent bolt loosening
To improve electrical grounding between components

questions.stem: When torquing hex head cap screws on the ROV, what specification should you use?

The maximum torque setting on your torque wrench
The torque value shown on the accompanying drawing
Hand-tightness until the screw feels snug

This ensures you use the exact torque value required for that specific application, preventing over-tightening or under-tightening that could lead to failure.

questions.stem: What is the correct order for assembling the J-box mounting stack?
options: Washer, HHCS, J-box, washer, mounting plate
HHCS, washer, J-box, washer, mounting plate
HHCS, washer, J-box, mounting plate, washer
HHCS, J-box, washer, washer, mounting plate

questions.explanation: The correct assembly order is HHCS first (acting as the anchor), followed by washer, then the J-box, another washer, and finally the mounting plate on top. This stacking sequence ensures proper load distribution and secure mounting.

questions.stem: What might be required before you can install the rotating J-box?

Remove the winch drum entirely from the assembly
Rotate the winch drum to bring the mounting location into a suitable working position
Secure the winch drum in its current position with locking pins

questions.stem: What must be applied to the threads when installing the J-box mounting hardware?

Teflon tape
Loctite 271 thread-locking compound

questions.stem: Before installing the winch slip ring, what must you do to the mounting area?

Flush it with fresh water to clean it

Remove all packing material and debris from it

Four

questions.stem: What must be done to threaded mounting holes before installing equipment?
