Use a tap to chase the threads and remove debris or metal shavings
Blow out loose material with compressed air
Fill the holes with sealant to prevent corrosion

questions.stem: What must be verified before using a slip ring model from Schilling Robotics?

The model matches the part list and the Schilling Robotics part number is confirmed on the shipping box
The model number matches the previous installation configuration

questions.stem: What is required when lifting the slip ring assembly?

Two workers or a lifting device
A single worker wearing protective gloves
Three workers minimum

questions.stem: What must you do with the slip ring assembly when placing it on a work bench?

Secure it to the bench to prevent it from rolling off
Place it on a padded surface to protect it from damage

questions.stem: When installing protective sleeving on slip ring cables, how far should the material be cut?

From the rear of the slip ring through the rotating 90-degree fitting and a few inches beyond the fitting end
Only to cover the rotating 90-degree fitting

options: Pull it through the winch center first, then attach the fish wire
Attach it to the fish wire and pull it through the motor housing first
Attach it to the fish wire and pull it through the winch center and sleeving tube
Insert it directly into the sleeving tube without using the fish wire

questions.stem: When installing protective material over the slip ring cable bundle end, what should be used to secure it to the housing?

Cable tie
Zip screws
Electrical tape

questions.stem: When installing the slip ring on the Schilling HD ROV, what must you avoid doing to the cable?

Bending the cable at a sharp 45-degree angle
Using excessive torque on the mounting bolts

questions.source_quote: Make sure to NOT kink or pinch the cable when installing the slip ring.

questions.stem: When installing the slip ring assembly, how should it be lifted into position?
options: Lift the slip ring assembly quickly into position while a second worker pulls the cable through the sleeving tube
Lower the slip ring assembly into position while a second worker pulls the cable through the sleeving tube
Lift the slip ring assembly slowly into position without any additional help
Lift the slip ring assembly slowly into position while a second worker pulls the cable through the sleeving tube

questions.explanation: The procedure requires a controlled, slow lift to avoid damaging the assembly, and a second worker must pull the cable through the sleeving tube simultaneously to prevent binding or cable damage.

questions.stem: When installing the six hex head cap screws during winch drum maintenance, what adhesive product should be applied to the threads?

Teflon thread seal tape
White lithium grease
Anti-seize compound

questions.stem: How many hex head cap screws need to be installed through the flange and into the front of the winch drum?

Install the slip ring first, then slide the gasket over the cable bundle
Place the gasket directly onto the slip ring mounting flange before routing the cable
Position the gasket at the end of the cable bundle after the slip ring is installed

questions.stem: When preparing to cut excess material from the anti-rotation bar, how far below the forks should you mark it?

25 mm (1 inch) below the forks

At the same level as the forks

questions.stem: When torquing hex head cap screws on the ROV, what pattern should you use to ensure even torque distribution?

questions.stem: Where does the slip ring front cable bundle connect on a Schilling HD ROV?

questions.stem: What must you obtain before performing slip ring removal or replacement on the Schilling HD ROV?

A print of LARS interconnect drawing 035-1071
Permission from the vessel's deck supervisor
A copy of the ROV年度 maintenance checklist

questions.stem: When performing procedures on J-boxes, what is true about rotating and stationary J-boxes?

Only stationary J-box procedures should be followed
Rotating J-boxes require additional specialized steps
The procedures are the same for both types

questions.stem: When routing wires during slip ring service, how many loops should you create?

1 loop

questions.stem: Before proceeding with electrical work on the ROV, what must you verify?

Later in the procedure, after initial installation is complete
Before connecting any equipment to the cables
Immediately after labeling the cables

Tether communication lines

questions.stem: When preparing a cable for connection, how much of the plastic cover should you trim to expose the braided shield?

50 mm (2 inches)
75 mm (3 inches)
150 mm (6 inches)

Only remove the braided shield, keeping the plastic cover intact
Remove the braided shield first, then cut the plastic cover
Remove both the plastic cover and braided shield simultaneously

questions.stem: When installing heat shrink tubing on a cable, where should the tubing be positioned before applying heat?

Slightly offset from the cut end to allow for adjustment

questions.stem: When installing heat shrink tubing on a cable during ROV electrical maintenance, what length should you cut and when should you slide it onto the cable?

50 mm (2 inches) section, slide it on before shrinking
10 mm (0.5 inch) section, slide it on before shrinking
25 mm (1 inch) section, slide it on after shrinking
questions.correct_answer: 25 mm (1 inch) section, slide it on before shrinking
questions.explanation: The correct procedure is to cut a 25 mm (1 inch) section of heat shrink tubing and slide it onto the cable before applying heat to shrink it. This allows the tubing to be properly positioned before being secured.
questions.source_quote: Cut a 25 mm (1 inch) section of heat shrink tubing and slide it on the cable

questions.stem: When should you heat shrink wire labels during the wiring process?
options: As each individual wire is connected
Before inserting wires into the terminal block
After all wires are tightened into position
After the entire system has been powered up and tested
questions.correct_answer: After all wires are tightened into position
questions.explanation: Heat shrinking labels after all wires are tightened ensures they are aligned and clearly visible, rather than having misaligned labels if you shrink them prematurely.
questions.source_quote: You will position the labels and heat shrink them into position after all the wires are tightened into position on the terminal block. That way the labels will all be aligned and clearly visible.

questions.stem: During wire preparation for the slip ring terminal block, when should you heat shrink cable labels?
options: After the wires are connected to the terminal block
Immediately after sliding them onto the conductors
Only after the labels have been slid onto the conductors
Before sliding them onto the conductors
questions.correct_answer: Only after the labels have been slid onto the conductors
questions.explanation: The procedure requires sliding cable labels onto conductors first, then waiting to heat shrink them until later in the process. Heat shrinking too early would make it difficult to position the labels correctly.
questions.source_quote: Slide the cable labels on to the conductors, but do NOT heat shrink them at this time.

questions.stem: When preparing wires for connection in the PDU, how much insulation should you strip from the end of each wire?
options: About 10 mm (3/8 inch)
About 15 mm (5/8 inch)
About 5 mm (3/16 inch)
About 25 mm (1 inch)
questions.correct_answer: About 15 mm (5/8 inch)
questions.explanation: The manual specifies stripping approximately 15 mm (5/8 inch) of insulation from each wire end before connection. This length provides enough exposed conductor for a proper electrical connection while still leaving sufficient insulation for strain relief.
questions.source_quote: Strip off about 15 mm (5/8 inch) of the cover off the end of each wire.

questions.stem: Where are the DECK CABLE and SLIP RING identification tags located on the junction box?
options: On the top cover of the junction box
On the back wall of the junction box
On the side wall of the junction box
On the front panel of the junction box
questions.correct_answer: On the back wall of the junction box
questions.explanation: The identification tags for both the DECK CABLE and SLIP RING are mounted on the back wall of the junction box, making them visible when accessing the box from behind during connection or maintenance operations.
questions.source_quote: Notice the DECK CABLE and SLIP RING identification tags on the back wall of the junction box.

questions.stem: According to the terminal block wiring assignment, which component connects to terminal JB1:4?
options: TMS IPDS B
ROV IPDS A
ROV HPU A
TMS IPDS A
questions.correct_answer: TMS IPDS A
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that JB1:4 is assigned to TMS IPDS A. Mixing up these connections could cause communication failures between the TMS and ROV systems, potentially compromising vehicle control and data transmission.
questions.source_quote: Connect all the wires into the terminal block exactly as shown in the photo. JB1:1 ROV HPU A through JB1:7 ROV IPDS A

questions.stem: After completing a slip ring replacement, where should the electrical leads from both the deck cable and the tether be connected?
options: To the slip ring housing
To the J-box cover bolts
To the terminal block
To the winch motor junction
questions.correct_answer: To the terminal block
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that after slip ring replacement, both sets of electrical leads (from the stationary J-box deck cable and the winch tether) must be attached to the terminal block to restore proper electrical connections.
questions.source_quote: bring in the electrical leads from the deck cable (for the stationary J-box) and the tether (from the winch), and attach them to the terminal block

questions.stem: What must you do after completing a slip ring replacement procedure?
options: Notify the dive team that the ROV is ready for deployment
Immediately power up the system to test functionality
Double check all connections before returning to service
Complete the maintenance log and stow equipment
questions.correct_answer: Double check all connections before returning to service
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states to double check all connections after finishing the slip ring replacement before returning to service. This verification step prevents electrical faults, shorts, or loose connections that could cause equipment failure or safety hazards during operation.
questions.source_quote: When finished, double check again

questions.stem: When installing fiber cables in the J-box, what is the correct procedure?
options: Route all fiber cables into the J-box and through the cable organizers before securing any components
Route fiber cables and secure components at the same time for efficiency
Route fiber cables into the J-box after all components are already secured in place
Secure all components first, then route the fiber cables through the organizers
questions.correct_answer: Route all fiber cables into the J-box and through the cable organizers before securing any components
questions.explanation: The procedure requires routing fiber cables into the J-box and through cable organizers first, before securing any components. This ensures proper cable management and prevents damage to fibers when installing other equipment.
questions.source_quote: carefully route all the fiber cables into the J-box and through the cable organizers

questions.stem: Why do HPU conductors require extra care during handling and routing?
options: They may lack double insulation, reducing protection against electrical hazards
They operate at extremely high voltages that can cause burns
They contain hazardous fluids that could leak during movement
They are designed to be replaced after every mission
questions.correct_answer: They may lack double insulation, reducing protection against electrical hazards
questions.explanation: HPU conductors may not have double insulation, which means they have less protection against electrical hazards compared to fully insulated conductors. This requires operators to exercise extra caution during handling and routing to prevent damage or shock.
questions.source_quote: HPU conductors may not have double insulation

questions.stem: What material is the fiber optic mounting board made of?
options: Anodized aluminum
Stainless steel
Plexiglass
Carbon fiber composite
questions.correct_answer: Plexiglass
questions.explanation: The fiber optic mounting board is specifically made of plexiglass, which provides transparency for visual inspection while being lightweight and non-conductive.
questions.source_quote: bolt on the plexiglass fiber optic mounting board

questions.stem: When preparing wire leads for the terminal block, what method should you use to strip the wire ends?
options: Strip an additional 2mm beyond what was used for slip ring conductors
Use a standard 5mm strip for all wire types
The same method used for the slip ring conductors
A different method than what was used for slip ring conductors
questions.correct_answer: The same method used for the slip ring conductors
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that you must use the identical stripping method for terminal block leads as was used for the slip ring conductors to ensure consistent wire preparation and proper electrical connections throughout the PDU.
questions.source_quote: Strip the wire ends the same as done with the slip ring

questions.stem: When assembling the fiber optic cable on the Schilling HD ROV, what is the correct sequence?
options: Connect the fiber end terminals only, skipping the fiber splitter
Connect the fiber end terminals first, then attach the fiber splitter
Attach the fiber splitter and connect terminals simultaneously
Attach the fiber splitter first, then connect the fiber end terminals
questions.correct_answer: Connect the fiber end terminals first, then attach the fiber splitter
questions.explanation: The procedure requires connecting fiber end terminals before attaching the fiber splitter, as these are separate assembly steps with a specific order.
questions.source_quote: Attach the fiber splitter to the cable

questions.stem: When attaching fiber end terminals to fiber optic cables, what instructions must you follow?
options: Follow any standard fiber optic termination procedure you know
Refer to the general ROV maintenance manual
Use the cable manufacturer's generic guidelines
Use the instructions supplied in the fiber termination kit
questions.correct_answer: Use the instructions supplied in the fiber termination kit
questions.explanation: The fiber termination kit includes specific instructions designed for that particular equipment. Following these ensures proper, safe attachment and prevents damage to the fiber optic connections.
questions.source_quote: Use the instructions supplied in the fiber termination kit to connect the fiber end terminals

questions.stem: How should hose clamps securing the Tygon tube be tightened according to the Schilling HD ROV manual?
options: Until the tube shows slight deformation
Until the clamp makes a clicking sound
With a wrench until snug
Hand-tight only
questions.correct_answer: Hand-tight only
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that hose clamps securing the Tygon tube must be tightened hand-tight only. Using tools can overtighten the clamps, potentially damaging the tube or compromising the hydraulic seal.
questions.source_quote: Tighten both hose clamps (3) hand-tight.

questions.stem: When routing the Tygon tube and conductors for the hydraulic system, what must you do before securing the tube ends?
options: Connect the electrical connectors before routing the tube
Route the tube and conductors into separate junction boxes
Route the Tygon tube and conductors into the stationary J-box
Secure both ends of the Tygon tube onto the hose barbs
questions.correct_answer: Route the Tygon tube and conductors into the stationary J-box
questions.explanation: The Tygon tube and conductors must be routed into the stationary J-box BEFORE securing the tube ends. This sequence ensures proper alignment and prevents having to disconnect secured fittings to reroute components.
questions.source_quote: Route the Tygon tube and conductors into the stationary J-box. Secure both ends of the Tygon tube onto the hose barbs.

questions.stem: What must be completed before proceeding with operations on the stationary J-box?
options: Pressure testing of the J-box seals
Visual inspection of the J-box housing
Calibration of the J-box sensors
Wiring must be completed according to drawing 035-1071
questions.correct_answer: Wiring must be completed according to drawing 035-1071
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly requires that stationary J-box wiring be completed according to drawing 035-1071 as a precondition before proceeding. Skipping or modifying this step could result in improper electrical connections and system failures.
questions.source_quote: Confirm that the stationary J-box wiring has been completed as per 035-1071.

questions.stem: When should labels be installed onto slip ring conductors during maintenance?
options: During initial assembly of the slip ring
Before removing the slip ring from the housing
During the termination process
After performing a conductivity test
questions.correct_answer: During the termination process
questions.explanation: Labels are installed as part of the termination process to properly identify slip ring conductors. This ensures correct wiring and facilitates future maintenance and troubleshooting.
questions.source_quote: Install labels (15) onto slip ring conductors.

questions.stem: What must be completed before proceeding with any work on the rotating J-box?
options: All seals must be replaced with new O-rings
The J-box must be disconnected from the main harness
Wiring must be completed according to drawing 035-1071
Hydraulic pressure must be reduced to zero
questions.correct_answer: Wiring must be completed according to drawing 035-1071
questions.explanation: The rotating J-box wiring is a critical precondition that must be verified complete per drawing 035-1071 before any further work can proceed. Skipping this step could result in electrical faults or system damage.
questions.source_quote: Confirm that the rotating J-box wiring has been completed as per 035-1071.

questions.stem: What must you do before returning an ROV system to service after maintenance or repair?
options: Perform a visual inspection of the exterior
Check that the power supply is connected
Conduct all required cable tests
Run a standard operational test
questions.correct_answer: Conduct all required cable tests
questions.explanation: The safety rule explicitly states that all required cable tests must be completed before returning a system to service. This ensures the electrical integrity and safe operation of the ROV system.
questions.source_quote: Do not put a system back into service without conducting the following tests.

questions.stem: During a pre-dive inspection, what must you verify about the fibers?
options: That fibers are properly secured into the coupling bracket
That fibers are clean and free of debris
That fibers are routed in the correct direction
That the coupling bracket is properly lubricated
questions.correct_answer: That fibers are properly secured into the coupling bracket
questions.explanation: Properly secured fibers prevent signal loss and mechanical failure during operations. Loose fibers can disconnect or sustain damage, compromising ROV functionality.
questions.source_quote: Verify that fibers are properly secured into the coupling bracket.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV operator manual, when must you test the electrical and fiber optic cables?
options: During every pre-dive check
Before beginning any maintenance work on the ROV
Only when the system fails during operation
After completing the slip ring installation, before returning the system to service
questions.correct_answer: After completing the slip ring installation, before returning the system to service
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states it is mandatory to test the electrical and fiber cables upon completion of slip ring installation before putting the system back into service. This ensures all cable connections are properly made and functioning before the ROV is deployed.
questions.source_quote: It is mandatory to test the electrical and fiber cables upon completion of the slip ring installation before putting the system into service.

questions.stem: What is the minimum ambient temperature required when applying the door seal gasket?
questions.explanation: The gasket material requires a minimum ambient temperature of 60°F to properly cure and form a reliable seal. Applying it in colder conditions can result in an inadequate seal.
questions.source_quote: ambient temperature must be above +60° F

questions.stem: During a pre-dive inspection of the ROV, what must you verify about the slip ring conductors?
options: That they are color-coded according to a legend chart
That they are wrapped in electrical tape
That they are properly secured into terminal blocks
That they are coated with anti-corrosion lubricant
questions.correct_answer: That they are properly secured into terminal blocks
questions.explanation: Loose conductors in terminal blocks can cause intermittent electrical connections or complete failure during operations, which could compromise the ROV's functionality and safety.
questions.source_quote: Verify that conductors are properly secured into terminal blocks.

questions.stem: When performing maintenance on the J-boxes of a Schilling HD ROV, how should the slip ring conductor bundle be routed?
options: Neatly routed inside of both J-boxes
Routed through a separate conduit away from the J-boxes
Loosely coiled outside the J-boxes for easy access
Secured with tape directly against the J-box exterior
questions.correct_answer: Neatly routed inside of both J-boxes
questions.explanation: The operator manual specifies that the slip ring conductor bundle must be routed neatly inside both J-boxes. This ensures proper organization, prevents cable damage, and maintains clear access for maintenance and inspection.
questions.source_quote: Neatly route the slip ring conductor bundle inside of both J-boxes.

questions.stem: Before performing J-box door seal maintenance, what safety procedure must be completed?
options: Disconnecting the TMS tether
A visual inspection of the J-box door
Lockout/Tagout procedures
Cleaning the door seal area
questions.correct_answer: Lockout/Tagout procedures
questions.explanation: Lockout/Tagout procedures are required before any maintenance work on the J-box to ensure the system is in a safe, de-energized state. Skipping this step could expose technicians to electrical hazards or unexpected system activation.
questions.source_quote: Prepare the system for safe working conditions. See your ROV system's technical manual for Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) procedures.

questions.stem: What tools are required to remove and replace the seal on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Power drill, wire cutters, and hammer
Putty knife, marking pen, and scissors
Screwdriver, wrench, and pliers
Torque wrench, socket set, and hex key
questions.correct_answer: Putty knife, marking pen, and scissors
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifies that technicians must use a putty knife, marking pen, and scissors specifically for seal removal and replacement procedures. Using the wrong tools could damage the seal or surrounding components.
questions.source_quote: Tools: Putty knife, Marking pen, Scissors

questions.stem: How many technicians are required to perform this procedure?
options: 3 technicians
1 technician
A team of 4 technicians
2 technicians
questions.correct_answer: 1 technician
questions.explanation: According to the personnel requirements, only one technician is needed to complete this procedure safely and effectively.
questions.source_quote: Personnel: 1 technician

questions.stem: What is the part number for the J-box door seal kit in the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual?
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual lists the J-box door seal kit as part number 008-1050. The other options are close variations that do not match the manual's listing.
questions.source_quote: 008-1050 KIT, J-BOX DOOR SEAL

questions.stem: When performing J-box door seal maintenance on the Schilling HD ROV, what hazards should you be aware of?
options: Mechanical and electrical hazards
Only mechanical hazards
Only electrical hazards
Chemical and radiation hazards
questions.correct_answer: Mechanical and electrical hazards
questions.explanation: The J-box contains both mechanical components and electrical systems, so technicians must watch for dangers from moving parts as well as electrical shock during door seal maintenance.
questions.source_quote: Safety Hazards: Mechanical, electrical

questions.stem: Which ROV models and components does this procedure specifically apply to?
options: All Schilling ROV models including UHD-IV
Any Schilling ROV model at both topside and underwater J-boxes
HD ROV, UHD-II ROV, UHD-III ROV, and UHD-IV ROV underwater J-boxes
HD ROV, UHD-II ROV, and UHD-III ROV topside J-boxes
questions.correct_answer: HD ROV, UHD-II ROV, and UHD-III ROV topside J-boxes
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states this procedure applies only to HD ROV, UHD-II ROV, and UHD-III ROV topside J-boxes, making it a model-specific and component-specific limitation.
questions.source_quote: Product Line: HD ROV, UHD-II ROV, UHD-III ROV

questions.stem: What type of silicone sealant is required when sealing the J-box door on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: Silicone sealant rated for indoor use only
Automotive-grade silicone sealant
Silicone sealant rated for outdoor use
Any standard silicone sealant will work
questions.correct_answer: Silicone sealant rated for outdoor use
questions.explanation: The manual specifically requires silicone sealant rated for outdoor use to ensure proper sealing of the J-box door against environmental conditions.
questions.source_quote: Sealant (generic silicone sealant rated for outdoor use)

questions.stem: What should you do before removing an old gasket during replacement?
options: Mark its current location in each corner
Remove it first and then mark its position
Apply lubricant to make removal easier
Clean the sealing surface with solvent
questions.correct_answer: Mark its current location in each corner
questions.explanation: Marking the gasket location before removal ensures you can align the new gasket correctly, which is critical for maintaining a proper seal in the PDU.
questions.source_quote: Mark the current gasket location in each corner.

questions.stem: When removing an old gasket from an ROV door, what tool should you use?
options: A flathead screwdriver
A power drill with a wire wheel attachment
A wire brush
A putty knife
questions.correct_answer: A putty knife
questions.explanation: The manual specifies using a putty knife to scrape off the old gasket. This tool is ideal because its thin, flexible blade can slide under the gasket without gouging or scratching the door surface, unlike more aggressive tools.
questions.source_quote: Scrape off the old gasket with a putty knife, being careful not to scratch or gouge the door surface.

questions.stem: During gasket replacement on the ROV door, what materials should be used to clean the door surface?
options: Compressed air and a wire brush
Solvent-based degreaser and steel wool
Isopropyl alcohol and an abrasive pad
Fresh water and a soft cloth
questions.correct_answer: Isopropyl alcohol and an abrasive pad
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, isopropyl alcohol combined with an abrasive pad is the specified method for cleaning the door surface during gasket replacement to ensure proper adhesion of the new gasket.
questions.source_quote: Isopropyl alcohol and an abrasive pad

questions.stem: When preparing a surface for a new gasket installation, what condition must the surface be in?
options: Coated with a thin layer of lubricant
Slightly damp to help the gasket seat
Completely free of all debris
Wiped clean but minor debris is acceptable
questions.correct_answer: Completely free of all debris
questions.explanation: Any debris left on the surface can prevent the new gasket from sealing properly, leading to leaks or failure.
questions.source_quote: Remove as much of the existing gasket as possible. Clean the surface so that it is free of all debris.

questions.stem: When installing the gasket on the J-box lid, where should you begin?
options: At the bottom of the J-box lid
At a pre-marked corner at the top of the J-box lid
In the center and work outward toward the edges
At any corner that is easily accessible
questions.correct_answer: At a pre-marked corner at the top of the J-box lid
questions.explanation: The procedure specifically requires starting at a pre-marked corner at the top of the J-box lid to ensure proper alignment and seating of the gasket.
questions.source_quote: Starting at one of the pre-marked corners at the top of the J-box lid

questions.stem: When installing a new door gasket on the ROV, which direction should the adhesive side face?
options: Adhesive side facing toward the door frame
Adhesive side facing down, onto the door surface
Adhesive side facing toward the hinge side of the door
Adhesive side facing up, away from the door surface
questions.correct_answer: Adhesive side facing down, onto the door surface
questions.explanation: The gasket must be installed with the adhesive side down so it sticks directly to the door surface, creating a proper seal when the door is closed.
questions.source_quote: place the new gasket, adhesive side down, onto the door

questions.stem: When scraping off the old gasket from the ROV door, what must you avoid doing?
options: Wearing safety gloves during the procedure
Scratching or gouging the door surface
Applying gasket remover solvent to soften the old material
Using a soft nylon scraper to remove the gasket
questions.correct_answer: Scratching or gouging the door surface
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly warns against scratching or gouging the door surface during gasket removal. Damaging the door surface could compromise the integrity of the seal and lead to leaks or further maintenance issues. The other options describe safe, standard practices for gasket replacement.
questions.source_quote: being careful not to scratch or gouge the door surface

questions.stem: When installing the door gasket on the J-box door, where should you begin?
options: At the top of the door
At the center of the door
At one of the pre-marked corners
At any random point along the edge
questions.correct_answer: At one of the pre-marked corners
questions.explanation: The gasket installation must start at a pre-marked corner to ensure proper alignment and seating. These marks indicate the correct starting positions for a secure fit.
questions.source_quote: Starting at one of the pre-marked corners, slowly place the gasket

questions.stem: When installing a gasket on the ROV, what does the safety manual instruct you to do?
options: Pre-cut the gasket if it appears too large
Do not pre-cut the gasket before installation
Pre-cut the gasket and then test the seal
Pre-cut the gasket to ensure it fits properly
questions.correct_answer: Do not pre-cut the gasket before installation
questions.explanation: The safety manual explicitly states not to pre-cut the gasket before installation. Pre-cutting can damage the gasket material or compromise the seal, leading to leaks or failure. Always install the gasket as provided and trimmed only during proper installation if specified.
questions.source_quote: Do not pre-cut the gasket.

questions.stem: When installing the top gasket on the HD ROV door, which orientation should you use according to the operator manual?
options: Horizontally from one top corner to the opposite corner
Vertically from the top edge to the bottom edge
Diagonally across the door surface
In a spiral pattern starting from the center
questions.correct_answer: Horizontally from one top corner to the opposite corner
questions.explanation: The manual specifies applying the top gasket horizontally from one corner to the opposite corner to ensure proper sealing alignment on the door surface.
questions.source_quote: onto the door horizontally, from one top corner to the other

questions.stem: When installing the gaskets, how much should the vertical gasket overlap each horizontal gasket?
options: One-quarter of an inch (1/4")
One-eighth of an inch (1/8")
One-half of an inch (1/2")
Three-quarters of an inch (3/4")
questions.correct_answer: One-eighth of an inch (1/8")
questions.explanation: The manual is clear that the vertical gasket must overlap each horizontal gasket by exactly one-eighth of an inch (1/8") to ensure a proper seal between the components.
questions.source_quote: overlap the horizontal gasket by 1/8" and extend vertically down to overlap the other horizontal gasket by 1/8"

questions.stem: After positioning the gaskets, what should you do with the overlapping portion of the vertical gasket?
options: Leave it folded down away from the horizontal gasket
Remove it completely from the assembly
Press it firmly against the ROV frame for added sealing
Lift and tuck it so it rests firmly against the horizontal gasket
questions.correct_answer: Lift and tuck it so it rests firmly against the horizontal gasket
questions.explanation: The procedure step explicitly states to lift and tuck the overlapping portion of the vertical gasket so it rests firmly against the horizontal gasket. This ensures proper sealing alignment between the two gasket surfaces.
questions.source_quote: Lift and tuck the overlapping portion of the vertical gasket so that it rests up against the horizontal gasket

questions.stem: When installing the gasket on the ROV door, which direction should the adhesive side face?
options: Adhesive side facing toward the door frame
Adhesive side facing upward away from the door surface
Adhesive side facing outward away from the door
Adhesive side facing downward toward the door surface
questions.correct_answer: Adhesive side facing downward toward the door surface
questions.explanation: The gasket must be installed with the adhesive side down so it bonds directly to the door surface, creating a proper seal. Installing it upside down would prevent the adhesive from making contact with the door.
questions.source_quote: place the gasket, adhesive side down, onto the door vertically

questions.stem: After installing a gasket with sealant, what is the correct way to handle any excess sealant?
options: Leave it in place to provide additional sealing
Rinse it off with fresh water
Gently wipe it off the gasket
Allow it to dry completely before removing
questions.correct_answer: Gently wipe it off the gasket
questions.explanation: The procedure calls for gently wiping excess sealant off the gasket immediately after installation. This removes material that could interfere with the seal or catch debris without disturbing the gasket itself.
questions.source_quote: Remove excess sealant by gently wiping it off.

questions.stem: When installing a door gasket on an ROV, what must you do to ensure proper adhesion?
options: Stretch the gasket slightly to ensure a loose fit
Press firmly on all parts of the gasket and verify it is fully adhered around the entire door surface
Apply a lubricant to the gasket to make installation easier
Install the gasket after the door is completely secured to the frame
questions.correct_answer: Press firmly on all parts of the gasket and verify it is fully adhered around the entire door surface
questions.explanation: The gasket must be pressed firmly and verified as fully adhered around the entire door surface to create a proper seal and prevent leaks or contamination during ROV operations.
questions.source_quote: Make sure to press firmly on all of the gasket and verify that the gasket is adhered around the entire door surface.

questions.stem: What does a properly installed gasket create around the door surface of an ROV enclosure?
options: A continuous seal at all contact points with the enclosure
A temporary seal that must be reapplied before each dive
A seal only at the corners where pressure is highest
A seal that requires additional silicone reinforcement
questions.correct_answer: A continuous seal at all contact points with the enclosure
questions.explanation: The gasket is specifically designed to form a continuous, uninterrupted seal along the entire door surface where it contacts the enclosure, ensuring watertight integrity throughout the sealing perimeter.
questions.source_quote: The gasket forms a continuous seal around the surface of the door at all points where contact will be made with the enclosure.

questions.stem: When installing a gasket on the ROV, what alignment procedure should you follow?
options: Align the gasket edges with the corner markings and ensure the gasket is vertical and parallel to the cover sides
Match the gasket to the cover shape and secure it with adhesive before checking alignment
Align the gasket with the center of the cover and ensure it is perpendicular to the cover sides
Center the gasket on the cover and ensure it is horizontal for even pressure distribution
questions.correct_answer: Align the gasket edges with the corner markings and ensure the gasket is vertical and parallel to the cover sides
questions.explanation: The gasket must be aligned with corner markings to ensure proper seating, and it must be vertical and parallel to the cover sides to maintain an even seal around the entire perimeter. Incorrect alignment can cause leaks or premature gasket failure.
questions.source_quote: The gasket edges should align with the corner markings, and the gasket should be vertical and parallel to the cover sides.

questions.stem: What must you do with foil and plastic wrapping on conductors before making connections?
options: Only remove the plastic wrapping, but keep the foil
Leave the wrapping in place to protect the conductors
Remove all foil and plastic wrapping
Cover the conductors with additional wrapping material
questions.correct_answer: Remove all foil and plastic wrapping
questions.explanation: Foil and plastic wrapping must be completely removed before making connections to ensure proper electrical contact and prevent connection failures or hazards.
questions.source_quote: Remove any foil and plastic wrapping from the conductors.

questions.stem: After installing a new seal on the ROV access door, what is the recommended waiting period before exposing it to normal conditions?
options: No waiting period is required
24 to 48 hours
At least 72 hours
Up to 12 hours
questions.correct_answer: Up to 12 hours
questions.explanation: The seal needs time to properly set and cure in a clean, dry environment away from direct sunlight. Exposing it prematurely can compromise the seal integrity and lead to leaks.
questions.source_quote: keep the door and new seal clean, dry, and out of direct sunlight for up to 12 hours

questions.stem: What must you do before working on the umbilical cable?
options: Confirm the work area is clear of other personnel
Lock out and tag out all electrical power to it
Verify the cable appears visually intact
Disconnect the main power cable from the TMS
questions.correct_answer: Lock out and tag out all electrical power to it
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that all electrical power to the umbilical must be locked out and tagged out before any work begins. This prevents unexpected energization and protects personnel from electrical hazards.
questions.source_quote:
Do not work on the umbilical cable until all electrical power to it is locked out and
tagged out.

questions.stem: When crimping high-power TYCO cable conductors with the left crimp tool, what AWG size and die should you use?
options: 8 AWG with die #10
10 AWG with die #8+
10 AWG with die #10
8 AWG with die #8+
questions.correct_answer: 8 AWG with die #8+
questions.explanation: The left crimp tool is designated for high-power conductors, and TYCO cable specifically requires 8 AWG with die #8+ for proper crimping.

questions.stem: When installing the fiber whip into the housing socket, which orientation is correct?

questions.stem: When removing the AC J-box from the mounting plate on a Schilling ROV, what should be done first?

Apply lubricant to the mounting bolts before removal
Disconnect the electrical connections inside the AC J-box
Remove the four M6x20 HHCS bolts holding the AC J-box to the mounting plate
questions.correct_answer: Remove any cable ties holding cables to the AC J-box
questions.explanation: According to the procedure, cable ties must be removed first to free the cables before removing the mounting bolts. The bolts are removed in the subsequent step.
questions.source_quote: Remove any cable ties holding cables to the AC J-box. 9. Remove the four M6x20 HHCS bolts holding the AC J-box to the mounting plate.

questions.stem: When removing the AC J-Box controller from the ROV frame, what type of surface should you place the AC J-box on?
options: Any available surface in the work area
A wet or damp surface
A clean, flat surface
A rubber insulated mat
questions.correct_answer: A clean, flat surface
questions.explanation: Placing the AC J-box on a clean, flat surface prevents contamination and physical stress on the unit during the removal procedure.
questions.source_quote: Remove the AC J-box from the ROV frame. AC J-Box Controller Removal 1. Place the AC J-box on a clean, flat surface. 2.

questions.stem: According to safety warnings in the ROV operator manual, where are shock hazards present in the ROV system?
options: Only when the ROV is submerged in seawater
Throughout the ROV system, including the control van and deck cable
Only at the main power junction box on the vessel
Only inside the ROV itself, not in surface equipment
questions.correct_answer: Throughout the ROV system, including the control van and deck cable
questions.explanation: The safety warning explicitly states that shock hazards exist throughout the entire ROV system, not just in isolated areas. Both the control van (surface control station) and the deck cable (which carries power to the ROV) contain dangerous voltages.
questions.source_quote:
SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages throughout the ROV system, including in the control van,
deck cable

questions.stem: Where can dangerous voltages that cause injury or death be found in an ROV system?
options: Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
Only in the ROV itself when it's submerged underwater
Only in the TMS (Tether Management System) during deployment
Only in the deck cable when it's being handled by personnel
questions.correct_answer: Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.explanation: The technical manual clearly states that dangerous voltages exist throughout the entire ROV system, not just in isolated areas. Personnel must treat all components—from the control van to the ROV itself—as potentially lethal at all times.
questions.source_quote:
Voltages throughout the ROV system (including in the control van, deck cable, LARS,
TMS, and ROV) can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: When should you observe high voltage safety practices on the ROV system?
options: When the system is powered down for storage
Only when the ROV is submerged in water
Whenever system power is enabled
Only during maintenance procedures
questions.correct_answer: Whenever system power is enabled
questions.explanation: High voltage can be present in the ROV system whenever power is enabled, not just during specific operations. This means safety practices must be followed at any time the system has power.
questions.source_quote: • Observe high voltage safety practices whenever system power is enabled.

questions.stem: What should you assume about ROV electrical equipment until you have confirmed it is isolated?
options: That it is safe to touch
That it is grounded
That it is powered down
That it is energized
questions.correct_answer: That it is energized
questions.explanation: The fundamental safety rule is to always assume equipment is energized until you have physically confirmed isolation. This conservative approach prevents dangerous assumptions that could lead to electrical shock or injury.
questions.source_quote: ALWAYS assume equipment is energized until you have CONFIRMED that it is isolated,

questions.stem: What does the ROS Lights Control Panel switch configuration allow the pilot to manage?
options: The ROV's acoustic positioning system
The ROV's video recording and storage functions
The ROV's main propulsion thrusters
The power and brightness levels of the ROV's illumination lights
questions.correct_answer: The power and brightness levels of the ROV's illumination lights
questions.explanation: The ROS Lights Control Panel switch configuration is specifically designed to control the ROV's lighting system, allowing the pilot to manage power to the lights and adjust brightness levels for optimal underwater visibility.
questions.source_quote: ROS Lights: Control Panel Switch Configuration

questions.stem: At what resistance value does the line isolation monitor trigger a fault alarm on the 26 VDC bus?
options: 200 kilohms
100 kilohms
150 kilohms
50 kilohms
questions.correct_answer: 100 kilohms
questions.explanation: The line isolation monitor constantly measures resistance between the vehicle chassis and the 26 V bus lines, triggering an alarm when this resistance drops below 100 kilohms, indicating potential ground fault conditions.
questions.source_quote: Subsea Node: Troubleshooting 1.4 26 VDC Line Isolation (“Ground Fault”) Monitor Each node is equipped a line isolation monitor circuit that is used to determine the resistance between the vehicle fram

questions.stem: Before disconnecting or connecting power cables on an ROV instrument, what must you do?
options: Disconnect the main power supply first
Turn OFF instrument power
Set the system to standby mode
Turn ON instrument power
questions.correct_answer: Turn OFF instrument power
questions.explanation: The manual is explicit: always turn OFF instrument power before disconnecting or connecting power cables. This prevents electrical shocks and damage to the equipment.
questions.source_quote: Always turn OFF instrument power before disconnecting or connecting power cables.

questions.stem: When performing the 26 VDC Line Isolation troubleshooting procedure, how long should you wait between turning on each device?
options: 5 seconds
10 seconds
1 minute
30 seconds
questions.correct_answer: 5 seconds
questions.explanation: The procedure specifies waiting 5 seconds between turning on each device. This allows the fault indicator to stabilize and clearly show which device is causing the fault when the indicator increases.
questions.source_quote: 26 VDC Line Isolation: Troubleshoot 2. Start turning on the last devices that were turned off, waiting 5 seconds between each device. When the device with the fault is turned on, the fault will increa

questions.stem: According to the operator manual, where can dangerous shock voltages be found in an ROV system?
options: Only in the TMS and deck cable
Only within the ROV itself
Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
Only in the control van and LARS
questions.correct_answer: Throughout the entire ROV system, including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly states that voltages throughout the ROV system—including the control van, deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV—can cause injury or death. You must treat all components as potential shock hazards.
questions.source_quote:
SHOCK HAZARD! Voltages throughout the ROV system, including the control van,
deck cable, LARS, TMS, and ROV, can cause injury or death.

questions.stem: According to the troubleshooting procedure for high-side isolation faults, what is the approximate response time of the line isolation monitor?
options: 10 seconds
1 second
3 seconds
5 seconds
questions.correct_answer: 3 seconds
questions.explanation: The line isolation monitor has an approximate response time of 3 seconds. This allows the technician to identify which device is faulty by turning them off one per second and watching when the fault indicator clears.

questions.stem: What does the designation 'SW_RET26' represent in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Secondary Wire Return 26 VDC
Single Wire Return 26 VDC
Switched Return 26 VDC
Surge Relay Return 26 VDC
questions.correct_answer: Switched Return 26 VDC
questions.explanation: SW_RET26 stands for 'Switched Return 26 VDC,' indicating a switched power return circuit operating at 26 volts direct current in the ROV's electrical distribution system.
questions.source_quote: SWITCHED_RETURN_26_VDC (SW_RET26)

questions.stem: What is the recommended procedure to speed up electrical diagnostic troubleshooting on an ROV?
options: Turn off devices one by one, one per second, while watching for changes
Turn off all devices at once to isolate the problem faster
Wait 10 seconds between turning off each device to ensure stability
Turn devices on and off rapidly to see what happens
questions.correct_answer: Turn off devices one by one, one per second, while watching for changes
questions.explanation: The procedure specifies turning off devices one at a time at one-second intervals while observing for changes. This methodical approach isolates which device is causing the problem by identifying when the issue stops occurring after a specific device is turned off.
questions.source_quote: To speed the diagnostic process, one by one turn all devices off, one per second, while watching the

questions.stem: What does the "+26_VDC_SW" designation refer to on the Schilling HD ROV?
options: A constant 26 Volt DC power supply circuit
A 26 Volt AC power supply circuit
A switched 26 Volt DC power supply circuit
A 260 Volt DC power supply circuit
questions.correct_answer: A switched 26 Volt DC power supply circuit
questions.explanation: The designation "+26_VDC_SW" indicates a 26 Volt Direct Current power supply that is switched (controlled on/off), where "SW" stands for switched. This is distinct from a constant power supply, which would not include the "SW" suffix.
questions.source_quote: +26_VDC_SWITCHED (+26VDC_SW)

questions.stem: What should you visually inspect on power cables before deploying the ROV?
options: The manufacturer date printed on the jacket
Visible damage, cuts, or exposed conductors
The number of copper strands inside the cable
The cable's length measurement
questions.correct_answer: Visible damage, cuts, or exposed conductors
questions.explanation: Power cables must be inspected for any visible damage such as cuts, abrasions, or exposed conductors before deployment to prevent electrical faults or safety hazards during ROV operations.
questions.source_quote: POWER CABLES

questions.stem: During 26 VDC line isolation troubleshooting, if the low-side ground fault disappears after you disconnect the power cable from a node, what is the likely source of the fault?
options: The IPDS unit
The main power supply
The DC Pak or Multi Pak
That specific node or one of its ports
questions.correct_answer: That specific node or one of its ports
questions.explanation: The procedure states that if the low-side ground fault goes away after disconnecting the power cable from a node, the fault is associated with that node or one of the ports on that node. This is the diagnostic logic of the isolation process.
questions.source_quote: 26 VDC Line Isolation: Troubleshoot 3. Turn on instrument power. — If the low-side ground fault has gone, the fault is associated with that node or one of the ports. Turn off instrument power and reco

questions.stem: When troubleshooting multiple ground faults on the CHASSIS (CHA), what should you try if the electrical isolation procedure does not resolve the issue?
options: Increase system voltage to locate the fault
Mechanical isolation
Refer to the SeaNet Connector Service Instruction for data pins 4-7
Replace the entire CHASSIS assembly
questions.correct_answer: Mechanical isolation
questions.explanation: According to the manual, when electrical isolation fails to troubleshoot multiple ground faults on the CHASSIS, the next step is to try mechanical isolation. This is a secondary troubleshooting procedure used when standard electrical methods are ineffective.
questions.source_quote: CHASSIS (CHA) For information on data pins 4 – 7 see SeaNet Con- nector Service Instruction. If the electrical isolation procedure is unable to troubleshoot multiple ground faults, try mechanical isol

questions.stem: While performing maintenance on an ROV node, what must you ALWAYS do before removing a SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM module?
options: Disconnect the tether cable first
Wait for the module to cool down
Ensure the node is powered down
Remove the module quickly to minimize exposure time
questions.correct_answer: Ensure the node is powered down
questions.explanation: Removing SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM modules while the node is powered creates an electrical hazard. Always power down the node first to ensure safe removal of these components.
questions.source_quote: Do not remove any SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM while the node is powered.

questions.stem: When instructed to shut down the node system, what is the correct procedure?
options: Turn off all node ports
Turn on all node ports
Test all node ports for signal strength
Increase power to all node ports
questions.correct_answer: Turn off all node ports
questions.explanation: The manual clearly states to turn off all node ports when deactivating the node system. This is the direct procedure step that must be followed.
questions.source_quote: Turn off all node ports.

questions.stem: What should you do with the O-ring before installing a module housing?
options: Confirm it is present and apply O-ring lubricant
Apply lubricant directly without checking if it is present
Replace the O-ring before applying any lubricant
Clean the O-ring with solvent before lubricating
questions.correct_answer: Confirm it is present and apply O-ring lubricant
questions.explanation: The procedure explicitly states you must first confirm the O-ring is present on the inside face of the module housing, then apply O-ring lubricant to ensure a proper seal and prevent leaks during operation.
questions.source_quote: Confirm that the O-ring (inside face of the module housing) is present and apply O-ring lubricant.

options: 2 mm, 3 mm, and 4 mm
5 mm, 6 mm, and 8 mm
3 mm, 4 mm, and 5 mm
4 mm, 5 mm, and 6 mm
questions.correct_answer: 4 mm, 5 mm, and 6 mm
questions.explanation: The manual specifically lists hex keys in 4 mm, 5 mm, and 6 mm sizes as the required tools for ROV maintenance procedures.

stems), hex keys (4 mm, 5 mm, 6 mm),

questions.stem: When performing maintenance on the ROV's fiber optic connection, what must you never do?
options: Use an approved wipe to clean the fiber end
Press directly on the fiber end
Visually inspect the fiber end for cracks or chips
Use compressed air to clear any debris
questions.correct_answer: Press directly on the fiber end
questions.explanation: Pressing directly on the fiber end can damage the delicate glass fibers and degrade signal transmission. Always handle fiber ends with care and use proper cleaning tools.
questions.source_quote: DO NOT press directly on the fiber end.

questions.stem: What is the torque range of the torque wrench specified for the Schilling HD ROV hydraulic system maintenance?
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV operator manual specifies a torque wrench with a range of 0-10 Nm (0-100 in-lb) for hydraulic and compensation system work, ensuring precise torque application for delicate fittings.

questions.stem: Before disconnecting electrical connections during Subsea Node PCA removal, what must be verified first?
options: That the ROV manipulator is properly calibrated
That power to the node has been de-energized
That the water depth is within operational limits
That the figure reference number is correct
questions.correct_answer: That power to the node has been de-energized
questions.explanation: Electrical connections must never be disconnected while power is present, as this creates arcing hazards that can damage equipment and injure personnel. Verifying de-energization is a critical safety step before any PCA removal procedure.
questions.source_quote: Subsea Node PCA: Remove and Install Figure 13

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure when installing case nuts onto protruding studs during ROV maintenance?
options: Apply Loctite only after the nuts are fully threaded by power tool
Apply Loctite and hand-thread the case nuts onto the studs
Thread the nuts without Loctite since hand-threading provides enough hold
Tighten the case nuts fully with a wrench before applying Loctite
questions.correct_answer: Apply Loctite and hand-thread the case nuts onto the studs
questions.explanation: The procedure requires applying Loctite adhesive first, then hand-threading the case nuts onto the studs to ensure proper thread engagement and adhesive coverage before final tightening.
questions.source_quote: Apply Loctite and hand-thread the balance of the case nuts onto the protruding studs.

questions.stem: When torquing the case nuts during assembly, what is the correct procedure and specification?
options: Start at the center and work outwards, torquing directly to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb) in one step
Start at the center and work outwards, torquing first to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb)
Start at any position on the assembly, torquing first to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb)
Start at the outer edges and work inward, torquing first to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb), then to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb)
questions.correct_answer: Start at the center and work outwards, torquing first to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb)
questions.explanation: The procedure requires starting at the center of the assembly and working outward to ensure even distribution of clamping force. The two-step torque specification (1.6 Nm then 2.7 Nm) is critical for proper assembly sequence.
questions.source_quote: Starting at the center of the assembly and working outwards, torque the case nuts first to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb).

questions.stem: Before reassembling an ROV component after maintenance, what should you verify about the perimeter O-ring?
options: That it has been lubricated with silicone grease
That it is in the proper position
That it has been replaced with a new one
That it matches the part number on the work order
questions.correct_answer: That it is in the proper position
questions.explanation: The perimeter O-ring must be correctly positioned to form a proper seal; if it's out of position, the component will leak. You don't need to replace it every time, lubricate it (unless specified), or match part numbers for position verification.
questions.source_quote: Verify that the perimeter O-ring is in the proper position.

questions.stem: What is the correct torque sequence when installing the SHCS nuts with wave washers and Loctite?
options: First torque to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb), then to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb)
First torque to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb)
Torque to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb) only
Torque to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb) only
questions.correct_answer: First torque to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb)
questions.explanation: The procedure requires a two-step torque process: initial torque to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb) followed by a final torque to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb) to ensure proper seating and secure fastening of the SHCS nuts.
questions.source_quote: Install the SHCS nuts, each with two wave washers and Loctite, in the five remaining holes in the perimeter of the case. Torque the nuts first to 1.6 Nm (20 in-lb), then to 2.7 Nm (25 in-lb).

questions.stem: When securing the case nuts during case assembly, what is the correct procedure?
options: Apply lubricant to the case bolts, use a wrench to thread the case nuts over the center studs, and tighten firmly.
Apply Loctite to the case bolts, hand-thread the case nuts over the center studs, and secure them finger-tight.
Apply Loctite directly to the case nuts, hand-thread them over the center studs, and torque to specification.
Hand-thread the case nuts over the center studs without any adhesive, then secure them with Loctite.
questions.correct_answer: Apply Loctite to the case bolts, hand-thread the case nuts over the center studs, and secure them finger-tight.
questions.explanation: The correct procedure requires applying Loctite to the case bolts (not the nuts) first, then hand-threading the case nuts over the center studs, and finally securing them only finger-tight. Using a wrench or applying Loctite to the nuts would be incorrect, and Loctite must be applied before assembly, not after.
questions.source_quote: Apply Loctite to the case bolts. Hand-thread the case nuts over the center studs and secure them finger-tight.

questions.stem: When inspecting the inner edge of the large oval region, what should you observe about the O-rings on both sides of the board?
options: The O-rings should NOT be visible
The O-rings should be partially protruding
The O-rings should show signs of wear or cracking
The O-rings should be clearly visible
questions.correct_answer: The O-rings should NOT be visible
questions.explanation: During this inspection step, hidden O-rings indicate proper seating and seal integrity. If the O-rings are visible, it means the board is not seated correctly in the housing, which could lead to leaks or equipment damage.
questions.source_quote: Inspect the inner edge of the large oval region to make sure that the O-rings on both sides of the board are not visible.

questions.stem: During assembly, what two things must you verify about the case nuts, hex nuts, and related hardware?
options: That the bolts are stainless steel and the washers are flat washers
That the nuts are finger-tight and the case is clean
That torque is correct per the assembly drawing and that each bolt/nut has a wave washer between it and the case
That the torque is correct and the nuts are painted red
questions.correct_answer: That torque is correct per the assembly drawing and that each bolt/nut has a wave washer between it and the case
questions.explanation: The procedure requires verifying torque specifications against the assembly drawing and confirming wave washers are installed in the correct location.
questions.source_quote: Referring to the assembly drawing, verify that the torque of the case nuts and hex nuts is correct. Verify that each bolt and nut has a wave washer between it and the case.

questions.stem: How do you access the network settings in the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Click to open System Settings -> Network
Click to open Control Panel -> Network
Click to open Diagnostics -> Network
Click to open Configuration -> Network
questions.correct_answer: Click to open Control Panel -> Network
questions.explanation: According to the Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual, network settings are accessed by clicking to open the Control Panel and then selecting Network from the menu options.

questions.stem: According to the operator manual, when should SFP modules be installed into the PCB SFP cages?
options: When the modules appear dirty or contaminated
Whenever the system is powered down for maintenance
When they have been previously removed
Only when installing brand new modules from the manufacturer
questions.correct_answer: When they have been previously removed
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states to install the SFP modules into the PCB SFP cages 'if they were removed,' indicating this procedure applies when previously removed modules need to be reinstalled.
questions.source_quote: If they were removed, install the SFP modules into the PCB SFP cages.

questions.stem: During HDEVS-compatible camera deck tests, what value should be entered in the Interface metric field when configuring IP settings?
questions.explanation: The procedure specifies that automatic metric should be unchecked and the value 21 entered into the Interface metric field for proper camera communication during deck tests.

questions.stem: What are the correct O-ring part numbers to install on the fiber dome?
questions.explanation: The Schilling HD ROV Operator Manual specifically states that O-rings P/Ns 004-0049 and 004-0103 must be installed on the fiber dome during maintenance. Using the correct part numbers ensures proper sealing and prevents optical signal loss.
questions.source_quote: Install new O-rings (P/Ns 004-0049 and 004-0103) onto the fiber dome.

questions.stem: What happens if unplugged SeaNet cable connectors are submerged in water?
options: They will leak
They will remain waterproof because the cap protects them
They will cause an immediate system shutdown to prevent damage
They will only leak if the ROV is powered on
questions.correct_answer: They will leak
questions.explanation: SeaNet cable connectors are only sealed when properly connected. When unplugged, the connection point is exposed and water will leak into the connector, potentially causing damage or electrical issues.
questions.source_quote: Unplugged SeaNet cable connectors will leak if submerged.

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure when you need to remove a node interface module (SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM) from a node?
options: Remove the module quickly to minimize exposure time
Only SIM modules require the node to be powered down first
Power down the node before removing the module
It is safe to remove modules while the node is powered if done carefully
questions.correct_answer: Power down the node before removing the module
questions.explanation: Node interface modules must never be removed while the node is powered because this creates an electrical hazard that could damage equipment or injure personnel. Always power down the node completely before removing any interface module.
questions.source_quote:
Never remove a node interface module (SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM) while the node is
powered.

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure when auto-detection is taking too long to identify the COM port on the Schilling HD ROV system?
options: Replace the COM cable before attempting any software-based solutions
Restart the ROV system and wait for automatic detection to retry
Contact technical support immediately without trying any troubleshooting steps
Navigate to the ROS tab and select 'Manage COM Port' to manually designate the correct port
questions.correct_answer: Navigate to the ROS tab and select 'Manage COM Port' to manually designate the correct port
questions.explanation: The manual specifically states that when auto-detection is having trouble, you should select manual mode and navigate to the ROS tab to choose 'Manage COM Port' from the list to designate the proper port.

questions.stem: What is the primary function of the ROS Lights Control Panel switch configuration?
options: To manage the ROV's propulsion system
To control the ROV's sonar imaging
To adjust the ROV's camera zoom settings
To control the on/off operation and intensity of the ROV's lights

questions.stem: Under what condition should you never remove a node interface module?
options: When the system indicator light is amber
While the node is de-powered and grounded
While the node is powered
During scheduled maintenance windows
questions.correct_answer: While the node is powered
questions.explanation: Removing a node interface module while the node is powered can cause electrical damage to the module and poses a safety hazard to personnel. Always de-power the node before removing any interface module.
questions.source_quote:
Never remove a node interface module (SIM, VIM, NIM, or LIM) while the node is
powered.

questions.stem: When assigning node numbers to ROS LED lights connected through light J-boxes on the DTS network, what is the correct rule?
options: All lights must have completely unique node numbers regardless of which J-box they're connected to
Each light connected to the same J-box must have a unique node number, but lights on different J-boxes can reuse node numbers
Lights connected to the Dual SeaNet SIM must use sequential node numbers starting from 1
Node numbers are automatically assigned by the system and cannot be manually programmed
questions.correct_answer: Each light connected to the same J-box must have a unique node number, but lights on different J-boxes can reuse node numbers
questions.explanation: The manual states that each light connected to a particular J-box must have its own unique node number, but these numbers will repeat across J-boxes. This means a light on J-box #1 can have node #3 while a light on J-box #2 also has node #3, but two lights on the same J-box cannot both be node #3.
questions.source_quote: ROS Lights: Control Panel Switch Configuration Connecting ROS 24 VDC LED Lights with Light J-Boxes When connecting the ROS LED lights through a light J-box to the DTS network, it is important to make

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the ROS Lights Control Panel switch configuration?
options: To control the ROV's camera zoom functions
To diagnose light system malfunctions
To automatically adjust light intensity based on depth
To turn the ROV's lights on and off
questions.correct_answer: To turn the ROV's lights on and off
questions.explanation: The ROS Lights Control Panel switch configuration provides the pilot with the ability to manually turn the ROV's lights on and off as needed during operations.
questions.source_quote: ROS Lights: Control Panel Switch Configuration

questions.stem: When removing the pan and tilt assembly, what must you do with the SeaNet cables?
options: Wrap them loosely and set aside for later reconnection
Leave them connected to maintain system integrity
Cut the cables to prevent accidental reconnection
Disconnect them from the actuators, then plug the exposed cables and cap the penetrators
questions.correct_answer: Disconnect them from the actuators, then plug the exposed cables and cap the penetrators
questions.explanation: The procedure requires disconnecting SeaNet cables from the actuators and protecting the exposed ends by plugging the cables and capping the penetrators to prevent contamination and ensure safety.

questions.stem: Before installing the tilt motor during pan and tilt assembly on the Schilling HD ROV, what should you verify?
options: The sonar equipment is operational
All electrical connections are clean and secure
The main frame is properly grounded to the vessel
The hydraulic system is fully pressurized
questions.correct_answer: All electrical connections are clean and secure
questions.explanation: Electrical connections must be clean and secure before installing the tilt motor to ensure proper function and prevent electrical faults or intermittent operation of the pan and tilt system.

questions.stem: According to the Schilling HD ROV troubleshooting procedure, what are the three main causes of fluid appearing on equipment or the deck?
options: Low fluid pressure, high operating temperature, and seal degradation
Fluid leak, compensators not completely filled, and air in circuit lines or components
Clogged filters, loose fittings, and excessive vibration
Pump failure, hose rupture, and contaminated hydraulic fluid
questions.correct_answer: Fluid leak, compensators not completely filled, and air in circuit lines or components
questions.explanation: The manual explicitly lists these three causes in the troubleshooting table for the symptom of fluid on equipment or deck: fluid leak, compensators not completely filled, and air in circuit lines or components. These are the root causes you must check for when this symptom appears.

questions.stem: What is the purpose of disconnecting half of the SeaNet components when troubleshooting a hydraulic leak?
options: To isolate which half of the system contains the leak source
To test whether the disconnect fittings are functioning properly
To reduce system pressure before performing maintenance
To identify which hydraulic pump is causing the pressure loss
questions.correct_answer: To isolate which half of the system contains the leak source

questions.stem: When troubleshooting a compensation system with an absent or inconsistent level reading, what is the first corrective action you should take?

Increase system pressure to force fluid through
Immediately replace the level sensor
Bleed the circuit components and lines to remove trapped air
questions.correct_answer: Bleed the circuit components and lines to remove trapped air

questions.stem: What happens to the circuit if a QD is damaged, disconnected, or improperly connected?
options: The QD will self-seal to prevent any fluid loss
Only the section before the QD will be affected
The circuit past the QD will not be compensated, allowing water to enter
The entire hydraulic system will shut down automatically
questions.correct_answer: The circuit past the QD will not be compensated, allowing water to enter
questions.explanation: The Quick Disconnect (QD) is the compensation boundary for that section of the hydraulic circuit. When it's damaged, disconnected, or improperly connected, the compensation system cannot protect downstream components, leaving them vulnerable to seawater intrusion.
questions.source_quote:
If a QD is damaged, disconnected, or improperly connected, every part of the circuit past
the QD will not be compensated, allowing water to enter.

questions.stem: What should you do if you discover contaminated hydraulic oil leaking near electronic components on an ROV?

Immediately isolate the contaminated oil and prevent it from contacting any electronic components

questions.stem: When is it safe to loosen or remove the compensator housing clamp?
options: When the hydraulic system has been completely depressurized
If the ROV is surfaced and all power has been secured
Only after restraining the spring and piston as directed by a service instruction
Whenever the clamp shows signs of wear or corrosion
questions.correct_answer: Only after restraining the spring and piston as directed by a service instruction

questions.stem: What does Zone 2 indicate in the context of ROV hazardous area classifications?
options: An area with continuous presence of explosive gases requiring full isolation
An area where flammable atmospheres may occasionally occur under abnormal conditions
An area where only battery-powered tools are permitted
An area completely free of any electrical equipment requirements
questions.correct_answer: An area where flammable atmospheres may occasionally occur under abnormal conditions
questions.explanation: Zone 2 is a hazardous area classification indicating that flammable atmospheres are not likely under normal operations but may occur for short periods due to abnormal conditions such as equipment failure or leaks. This distinguishes it from Zone 0 (continuous hazard) and Zone 1 (likely under normal conditions), requiring specific equipment certification and procedures.
questions.source_quote: ,ZONE 2

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the Tether Management System (TMS) on a Schilling HD ROV?
options: To provide additional thrust for the ROV during strong currents
To manage and store the ROV's tether to prevent tangling and reduce deck clutter
To store spare parts and tools for underwater repairs
To house the ROV's main computer and control systems
questions.correct_answer: To manage and store the ROV's tether to prevent tangling and reduce deck clutter
questions.explanation: The TMS (Tether Management System) is designed specifically to manage, store, and pay out the ROV's tether cable, keeping it organized and preventing tangles during operations while also reducing the amount of loose tether on the deck or in the water column.
questions.source_quote:
NOTES:
1.

options: Remote Observation Vehicle
Radio Operated Vessel
Robotic Operational Vehicle

questions.correct_answer: Remotely Operated Vehicle
questions.explanation: ROV stands for Remotely Operated Vehicle, which is a tethered underwater robot controlled by a pilot from the surface. questions.stem: What does the term 'Regardless of Feature Size' (RFS) indicate in an ROV specification?
options: The specification only applies when the feature is at its maximum allowed size
The specification can be overridden if the feature appears normal
The specification is only relevant for small features
The specification applies to the feature regardless of its actual dimensions within tolerance
questions.correct_answer: The specification applies to the feature regardless of its actual dimensions within tolerance
questions.explanation: Regardless of Feature Size (RFS) means that a given tolerance or specification applies uniformly to a feature no matter what its actual dimensions are, as long as those dimensions fall within the acceptable size range. The term ensures consistent application of requirements across all sizes of the same feature.

questions.stem: What is the primary characteristic that defines a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV)?
options: It is controlled by an operator on the surface through a tether
It is designed to function without any connection to a surface vessel
It is autonomously controlled by onboard artificial intelligence
It operates independently using pre-programmed navigation
questions.correct_answer: It is controlled by an operator on the surface through a tether
questions.explanation: The defining feature of an ROV is that it is physically connected to a surface control station via a tether, allowing a human operator to remotely control its functions in real-time. This distinguishes ROVs from AUVs (Autonomous Underwater Vehicles), which follow pre-programmed paths without human intervention.
questions.source_quote:
NOTES:
1.

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of a Remotely Operated Vehicle (ROV) in underwater operations?

To replace all human activities in subsea environments

NOTES:
1.

questions.stem: What is the correct procedure for handling an unused wire that is already soldered to a penetrator pin?
options: Leave the wire as-is since it is already secured by the solder
Completely remove the wire and discard it from the system
Loop the wire back to the same pin and slide shrink tubing (item 22) down over the loop and the solder joint
Apply electrical tape directly over the solder joint without looping
questions.correct_answer: Loop the wire back to the same pin and slide shrink tubing (item 22) down over the loop and the solder joint
questions.explanation: The manual specifies that for unused wires already soldered to penetrator pins, you must loop the wire back to the same pin and cover the loop and solder joint with shrink tubing (item 22) for proper insulation and protection.
questions.source_quote:
NOTES:
FOR AN UNUSED WIRE ALREADY SOLDERED TO THE PENETRATOR PIN,
LOOP THE WIRE BACK TO THE SAME PIN AND SLIDE SHRINK TUBING
(ITEM 22) DOWN OVER THE LOOP AND THE SOLDER JOINT.

BETWEEN THE SHELL AND CASE.
